options.B: Purposes that include improvement of user's situational awareness, surface navigation operations, training, charting and planning
options.E: For air traffic controller shift scheduling

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 note under 'Aerodrome mapping data (AMD)' states: 'Aerodrome mapping data is collected for purposes that include the improvement of the user's situational awareness, surface navigation operations, training, charting and planning.'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as an aeronautical information product?
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 under 'Aeronautical information product' lists: 'Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP), including Amendments and Supplements; Aeronautical Information Circulars (AIC); aeronautical charts; NOTAM; and digital data sets.' Aircraft maintenance logs are not mentioned.

questions.question: What does the acronym AIRAC stand for according to Chapter 1.1?
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 states: 'AIRAC. An acronym (aeronautical information regulation and control) signifying a system aimed at advance notification, based on common effective dates, of circumstances that necessitate significant changes in operating practices.'

questions.question: True or False: An ASHTAM is a NOTAM notifying volcanic activity that is of significance to aircraft operations.
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'ASHTAM. A special series NOTAM notifying by means of a specific format change in activity of a volcano, a volcanic eruption and/or volcanic ash cloud that is of significance to aircraft operations.'

questions.question: What does 'Data integrity (assurance level)' mean as defined in Chapter 1.1?

options.A: The number of digits used to express a value
options.B: The probability that the true value is within an interval
options.C: A degree of assurance that an aeronautical data and its value has not been lost or altered since origination or authorized amendment
options.F: The completeness of a data set

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Data integrity (assurance level). A degree of assurance that an aeronautical data and its value has not been lost or altered since the origination or authorized amendment.'

questions.question: According to the definition of 'Obstacle' in Chapter 1.1, which of the following is considered an obstacle?

options.A: Only mobile objects on the ground
options.B: Fixed or mobile objects located on an area intended for surface movement of aircraft, or that extend above a defined surface to protect aircraft in flight, or that are assessed as a hazard to air navigation
options.F: Any object on the ground regardless of location

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'Obstacle. All fixed (whether temporary or permanent) and mobile objects, or parts thereof, that: a) are located on an area intended for the surface movement of aircraft; or b) extend above a defined surface intended to protect aircraft in flight; or c) stand outside those defined surfaces and that have been assessed as being a hazard to air navigation.'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: What is the horizontal (geodetic) reference system that shall be used for international air navigation according to section 1.2.1?
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.1.1 states: 'The World Geodetic System—1984 (WGS-84) shall be used as the horizontal (geodetic) reference system for international air navigation.'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: True or False: For the vertical reference system, the geoid is defined as the equipotential surface that coincides with undisturbed mean sea level extended continuously through continents.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2.1 Note 1: 'The geoid globally most closely approximates MSL. It is defined as the equipotential surface in the gravity field of the Earth which coincides with the undisturbed MSL extended continuously through the continents.'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: Which temporal reference system shall be used for international air navigation according to section 1.2.3?

options.A: Julian calendar and local solar time
options.B: Gregorian calendar and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC)
options.D: Chinese lunar calendar and Beijing time
options.F: Gregorian calendar and local mean time

questions.explanation: Section 1.2.3.1: 'The Gregorian calendar and Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) shall be used as the temporal reference system for international air navigation.'

questions.question: According to section 1.3.1, aeronautical information products intended for international distribution shall include text in which language for parts expressed in plain language?
questions.explanation: Section 1.3.1: 'Aeronautical information products intended for international distribution shall include English text for those parts expressed in plain language.'

questions.question: True or False: According to section 1.3.2, place names shall be spelt in conformity with local usage and transliterated into the ISO-Basic Latin alphabet when necessary.
questions.explanation: Section 1.3.2: 'Place names shall be spelt in conformity with local usage, transliterated, when necessary, into the ISO-Basic Latin alphabet.'

questions.question: What does the term 'integrity classification' for aeronautical data include, according to Chapter 1.1?

options.B: Routine data, essential data, critical data

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 under 'Integrity classification (aeronautical data)' states: 'a) routine data; b) essential data; c) critical data' with descriptions of associated risk.

questions.question: What does the term 'Bare Earth' mean in the definitions of Chapter 1.1?

options.A: The surface of the Earth including all buildings and vegetation
options.B: Surface of the Earth including bodies of water and permanent ice and snow, excluding vegetation and manmade objects
options.C: Only the soil surface without any water
options.D: The top layer of soil only

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'Bare Earth. Surface of the Earth including bodies of water and permanent ice and snow, and excluding vegetation and manmade objects.'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: True or False: According to section 1.2.2.2, the Earth Gravitational Model — 1996 (EGM-96) shall be used as the global gravity model for international air navigation.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2.2: 'The Earth Gravitational Model — 1996 (EGM-96) shall be used as the global gravity model for international air navigation.'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: According to section 1.2.2.3, when a geoid model other than EGM-96 is used, what must be provided in the AIP?

options.A: Only the name of the model
options.B: A description of the model used, including parameters required for height transformation between the model and EGM-96
options.D: The software used to compute the model

questions.explanation: Section 1.2.2.3: 'When a geoid model other than the EGM-96 model is used, a description of the model used, including the parameters required for height transformation between the model and EGM-96, shall be provided in the Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP).'

questions.sub_chapter: 1.2 Common reference system for air navigation
questions.question: True or False: Section 1.2.3.2 states that if a different temporal reference system is used, no description is required.
questions.explanation: Section 1.2.3.2: 'When a different temporal reference system is used for some applications, the feature catalogue, or the metadata associated with an application schema or a data set, as appropriate shall include a description of that system or a citation for a document that describes that temporal reference system.' Thus a description is required.

questions.question: What does the definition of 'Aeronautical information' in Chapter 1.1 specify?

options.B: Information resulting from the assembly, analysis and formatting of aeronautical data

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'Aeronautical information. Information resulting from the assembly, analysis and formatting of aeronautical data.'

questions.question: According to Chapter 1.1, what is a 'Feature type'?

options.B: Class of real world phenomena with common properties
options.D: A relationship between two data sets

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Feature type. Class of real world phenomena with common properties (ISO 19110).'

questions.question: What is 'Geoid undulation' as defined in Chapter 1.1?

options.A: The height of a point above mean sea level
options.B: The distance of the geoid above or below the mathematical reference ellipsoid
options.E: The height of terrain above geoid

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Geoid undulation. The distance of the geoid above (positive) or below (negative) the mathematical reference ellipsoid.'

questions.question: True or False: According to Chapter 1.1, a 'Heliport' is defined as only a defined area on water intended for helicopters.
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'Heliport. An aerodrome or a defined area on a structure intended to be used wholly or in part for the arrival, departure and surface movement of helicopters.' It includes aerodromes and structures, not only water.

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Pre-flight information bulletin (PIB)' in Chapter 1.1?

options.B: A presentation of current NOTAM information of operational significance, prepared prior to flight
options.D: A digital data set for navigation
options.E: A list of all active AIC

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines: 'Pre-flight information bulletin (PIB). A presentation of current NOTAM information of operational significance, prepared prior to flight.'

questions.question: According to Chapter 1.1, what is 'Station declination'?

options.A: The difference between magnetic north and true north
options.B: An alignment variation between the zero degree radial of a VOR and true north, determined at calibration
options.C: The angle between the runway and the north
options.F: The offset of an ILS localizer

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Station declination. An alignment variation between the zero degree radial of a VOR and true north, determined at the time the VOR station is calibrated.'

questions.question: What does the definition of 'Validation' in Chapter 1.1 refer to?

options.A: Checking that specified requirements have been fulfilled
options.B: Confirmation that requirements for a specific intended use or application have been fulfilled
options.C: Ensuring data is free of errors

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Validation. Confirmation, through the provision of objective evidence, that the requirements for a specific intended use or application have been fulfilled (ISO 9000).'

questions.question: True or False: The definition of 'Verification' in Chapter 1.1 is the same as 'Validation'.
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1 defines Verification as 'Confirmation, through the provision of objective evidence, that specified requirements have been fulfilled' while Validation refers to 'requirements for a specific intended use or application'. They are distinct.

questions.question: What does the definition of 'Cyclic redundancy check (CRC)' in Chapter 1.1 refer to?

options.B: A mathematical algorithm applied to digital data to provide assurance against loss or alteration
options.E: A method for error correction in voice communications

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Cyclic redundancy check (CRC). A mathematical algorithm applied to the digital expression of data that provides a level of assurance against loss or alteration of data.'

questions.question: According to the definition of 'Data quality' in Chapter 1.1, which elements are included?

options.B: Accuracy, resolution, integrity, traceability, timeliness, completeness and format

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Data quality. A degree or level of confidence that the data provided meet the requirements of the data user in terms of accuracy, resolution, integrity (or equivalent assurance level), traceability, timeliness, completeness and format.'

questions.question: True or False: Chapter 1.1 defines 'Data traceability' as the degree that a system can provide a record of changes to a product enabling an audit trail from end-user to originator.
questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Data traceability. The degree that a system or a data product can provide a record of the changes made to that product and thereby enable an audit trail to be followed from the end-user to the originator.'

questions.question: According to the definition of 'International airport' in Chapter 1.1, what formalities are carried out there?

options.B: Customs, immigration, public health, animal and plant quarantine and similar procedures

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'International airport. Any airport designated by the Contracting State ... as an airport of entry and departure for international air traffic, where the formalities incident to customs, immigration, public health, animal and plant quarantine and similar procedures are carried out.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Manoeuvring area' in Chapter 1.1?

options.B: That part of an aerodrome to be used for take-off, landing and taxiing of aircraft, excluding aprons

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Manoeuvring area. That part of an aerodrome to be used for the take-off, landing and taxiing of aircraft, excluding aprons.'

questions.question: What does Note 4 in Chapter 1.1 of Annex 15 indicate about guidance material on AIS organization?

options.A: It is contained in the Aeronautical Information Services Manual (Doc 8126)

questions.explanation: Chapter 1, Note 4: 'Guidance material on the organization and operation of the AIS is contained in the Aeronautical Information Services Manual (Doc 8126).'

questions.question: According to the definition of 'Next intended user' in Chapter 1.1, who is the next intended user?

options.B: The entity that receives the aeronautical data or information from the AIS
options.C: The end customer such as a pilot

questions.explanation: Chapter 1.1: 'Next intended user. The entity that receives the aeronautical data or information from the aeronautical information service.'

questions.question: What is the responsibility of each Contracting State regarding the provision of aeronautical information service (AIS) according to section 2.1.1?

options.A: Must provide AIS exclusively through a government agency
options.B: Must provide AIS or agree with another State for joint service or delegate to a non‑governmental agency provided Standards are met
options.C: Must outsource AIS to a private company without oversight
options.D: Must not share AIS with other States
options.E: Must charge all users for AIS
options.F: Must provide AIS only for domestic flights

questions.explanation: Section 2.1.1: 'Each Contracting State shall: a) provide an aeronautical information service (AIS); or b) agree with one or more other Contracting State(s) for the provision of a joint service; or c) delegate the authority for the provision of the service to a non-governmental agency, provided the Standards and Recommended Practices of this Annex are adequately met.'

questions.question: According to section 2.1.2, a Contracting State shall ensure provision of aeronautical data and information covering its own territory and what other areas?

options.B: Areas over the high seas for which it is responsible for providing air traffic services
options.C: Only within 100 nautical miles of its coastline
options.E: Only the territory of the State
options.F: Areas under the control of other States by agreement

questions.explanation: Section 2.1.2: 'Each Contracting State shall ensure that the provision of aeronautical data and aeronautical information covers its own territory and those areas over the high seas for which it is responsible for the provision of air traffic services (ATS).'

questions.question: True or False: An AIS must ensure that aeronautical data and information are made available in a form suitable only for air traffic control units, not for flight crews.
questions.explanation: Section 2.2.1 states the AIS shall make data available for the ATM community including 'those involved in flight operations, including flight crews, flight planning and flight simulators; and the ATS unit responsible for flight information service'.

questions.question: If an AIS does not provide 24-hour service, how long before and after a flight in its area of responsibility must service be available?

options.A: One hour before and one hour after
options.B: Two hours before and two hours after
options.C: At least two hours before and two hours after such period

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.3: 'Where 24-hour service is not provided, service shall be available during the whole period an aircraft is in flight in the area of responsibility of the AIS, plus a period of at least two hours before and after such a period.'

questions.question: When an AIS distributes aeronautical data obtained from another State's AIS, how must it be identified?

options.A: As having been verified by the distributing AIS
options.B: As having the authority of the originating State
options.D: As the property of the distributing State

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.5: 'Aeronautical data and aeronautical information obtained under 2.2.4 a) shall, when distributed, be clearly identified as having the authority of the originating State.'

questions.sub_chapter: 2.3 Exchange of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.question: According to section 2.3.6, which aeronautical information products shall be made available by the originating State without charge to a requesting State's AIS?

options.B: AIP, AIC, NOTAM, and aeronautical charts
options.D: All products including aeronautical charts but not AIP

questions.explanation: Section 2.3.6 lists: 'a) Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP), including Amendments and Supplements; b) Aeronautical Information Circulars (AIC); c) NOTAM; and d) aeronautical charts.' These shall be provided without charge.

questions.question: True or False: According to section 2.4.1, a third party receiving a copyright-protected aeronautical information product from a State must be made aware of the copyright and the product appropriately annotated.
questions.explanation: Section 2.4.1: 'Any aeronautical information product which has been granted copyright protection by the originating State and provided to another State ... shall only be made available to a third party on the condition that the third party is made aware that the product is copyright protected and provided that it is appropriately annotated that the product is subject to copyright by the originating State.'

questions.question: According to section 2.1.3, who remains responsible for aeronautical data and information provided under the State's authority?
questions.explanation: Section 2.1.3: 'The State concerned shall remain responsible for the aeronautical data and aeronautical information provided in accordance with 2.1.2. Aeronautical data and aeronautical information provided for and on behalf of a State shall clearly indicate that they are provided under the authority of that State, irrespective of the format in which they are provided.'

questions.question: What must each Contracting State ensure regarding formal arrangements between originators and the AIS?

options.A: That originators are paid for their data
options.B: That formal arrangements are established in relation to the timely and complete provision of aeronautical data and information
options.C: That all data is provided free of charge
options.E: That only digital data is exchanged
options.F: That arrangements are reviewed annually by ICAO

questions.explanation: Section 2.1.5: 'Each Contracting State shall ensure that formal arrangements are established between originators of aeronautical data and aeronautical information and the AIS in relation to the timely and complete provision of aeronautical data and aeronautical information.'

questions.question: According to section 2.2.6, if aeronautical data obtained from other sources cannot be verified before distribution, how must it be identified?

options.A: As having the authority of the originating State

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.6: 'Aeronautical data and aeronautical information obtained under 2.2.4 b) shall, if possible, be verified before distribution and if not verified shall, when distributed, be clearly identified as such.'

questions.sub_chapter: 2.3 Exchange of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.question: True or False: Section 2.3.2 recommends that formal arrangements should be established between parties providing aeronautical data on behalf of States and their users.
questions.explanation: Section 2.3.2: 'Recommendation. - Formal arrangements should be established between those parties providing aeronautical data and aeronautical information on behalf of the States and their users in relation to the provision of the service.'

questions.sub_chapter: 2.3 Exchange of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.question: According to section 2.3.8, when are digital data sets provided to another State's AIS?

options.B: On the basis of agreement between the Contracting States concerned

questions.explanation: Section 2.3.8: 'When aeronautical data and aeronautical information are provided in the form of digital data sets to be used by the AIS, they shall be provided on the basis of agreement between the Contracting States concerned.'

questions.question: What does section 2.5 recommend regarding cost recovery for collecting and compiling aeronautical data?

options.B: The overhead cost should be included in the cost basis for airport and air navigation services charges as appropriate
options.C: Costs are borne entirely by the State
options.D: Costs are recovered through a separate tax

questions.explanation: Section 2.5: 'Recommendation. The overhead cost of collecting and compiling aeronautical data and aeronautical information should be included in the cost basis for airport and air navigation services charges as appropriate in accordance with the principles contained in ICAO's Policies on Charges for Airports and Air Navigation Services (Doc 9082).'

questions.question: According to section 2.2.1, the AIS shall make aeronautical data available to which of the following?

options.B: Those involved in flight operations including flight crews, flight planning and flight simulators, and the ATS unit responsible for flight information service

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.1: 'An AIS shall ensure that aeronautical data and aeronautical information ... are made available in a form suitable for the operational requirements of the air traffic management (ATM) community, including: a) those involved in flight operations, including flight crews, flight planning and flight simulators; and b) the ATS unit responsible for flight information service and the services responsible for pre-flight information.'

questions.sub_chapter: 2.3 Exchange of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.question: According to section 2.3.10, what shall be used for the provision of data sets?

options.B: Globally interoperable aeronautical data and aeronautical information exchange models

questions.explanation: Section 2.3.10: 'Globally interoperable aeronautical data and aeronautical information exchange models shall be used for the provision of data sets.'

questions.question: True or False: Section 2.4.2 allows the receiving State to provide digital data sets of the providing State to any third party without consent.
questions.explanation: Section 2.4.2: 'When aeronautical data and aeronautical information are provided to a State in accordance with 2.3.8, the receiving State shall not provide the digital data sets of the providing State to any third party without the consent of the providing State.'

questions.question: According to section 2.2.2, what shall an AIS do with aeronautical data and information concerning the entire territory of the State?

options.A: Only distribute it to domestic users
options.B: Receive, collate or assemble, edit, format, publish/store and distribute as aeronautical information products
options.C: Only store it for future use

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.2: 'An AIS shall receive, collate or assemble, edit, format, publish/store and distribute aeronautical data and aeronautical information concerning the entire territory of the State as well as those areas over the high seas for which the State is responsible for the provision of ATS. Aeronautical data and aeronautical information shall be provided as aeronautical information products.'

questions.question: What does section 2.2.7 require an AIS to do?

options.A: Charge other States for aeronautical information
options.B: Promptly make available to the AIS of other States any aeronautical data and information necessary for safety, regularity or efficiency of air navigation required by them
options.C: Withhold information from other States for national security
options.D: Only exchange data with neighboring States

questions.explanation: Section 2.2.7: 'An AIS shall promptly make available to the AIS of other States any aeronautical data and aeronautical information necessary for the safety, regularity or efficiency of air navigation required by them, to enable them to comply with 2.2.1.'

questions.question: According to section 3.2.3.2, for essential data, what must be assured?

options.A: Only that corruption is avoided during processing
options.B: That corruption does not occur at any stage of the entire process, including additional processes to address potential risks
options.C: That data is backed up daily
options.D: That only authorized personnel have access
options.F: That data is never changed after origination

questions.explanation: Section 3.2.3.2 b): 'for essential data: assure corruption does not occur at any stage of the entire process and include additional processes as needed to address potential risks in the overall system architecture to further assure data integrity at this level'.

questions.sub_chapter: 3.3 Aeronautical data and aeronautical information verification and validation
questions.question: True or False: According to section 3.3.2, an AIS must establish verification and validation procedures to ensure quality requirements are met upon receipt of aeronautical data.
questions.explanation: Section 3.3.2: 'An AIS shall establish verification and validation procedures which ensure that upon receipt of aeronautical data and aeronautical information, quality requirements are met.'

questions.question: What does section 3.5.1 say about automation in AIS?

options.B: Automation shall be applied to ensure quality, efficiency and cost-effectiveness
options.C: Automation is prohibited due to integrity risks
options.F: Automation applies only to NOTAM distribution

questions.explanation: Section 3.5.1: 'Automation shall be applied in order to ensure the quality, efficiency and cost-effectiveness of aeronautical information services.'

questions.question: According to section 3.6.4, what must be done regarding personnel competencies within the quality management system?

options.A: No training is required if personnel have experience
options.B: Competencies must be identified, personnel trained, and periodic assessments established

questions.explanation: Section 3.6.4: '...competencies and the associated knowledge, skills and abilities required for each function shall be identified, and personnel assigned to perform those functions shall be appropriately trained. ... Initial and periodic assessments shall be established that require personnel to demonstrate the required competencies.'

questions.question: True or False: Section 3.7.1 states that human factors principles must be taken into consideration in the organization, design, processing and distribution of aeronautical data and information.
questions.explanation: Section 3.7.1: 'The organization of an AIS as well as the design, contents, processing and distribution of aeronautical data and aeronautical information shall take into consideration human factors principles which facilitate their optimum utilization.'

questions.question: According to section 3.2.1, the order of accuracy for aeronautical data shall be in accordance with what?
questions.explanation: Section 3.2.1: 'The order of accuracy for aeronautical data shall be in accordance with its intended use.'

questions.question: True or False: Section 3.2.2 states that the resolution of aeronautical data can be coarser than the actual data accuracy.
questions.explanation: Section 3.2.2: 'The order of resolution of aeronautical data shall be commensurate with the actual data accuracy.' 'Commensurate' means consistent with, not coarser.

questions.question: What does section 3.2.4 require regarding traceability of aeronautical data?

options.A: Traceability is required only for critical data
options.B: Traceability shall be ensured and retained as long as the data is in use
options.D: Traceability must be removed after 5 years
options.F: Traceability applies only to obstacle data

questions.explanation: Section 3.2.4: 'Traceability of aeronautical data shall be ensured and retained as long as the data is in use.'

questions.question: According to section 3.4.1, when must digital data error detection techniques be used?

options.C: During transmission and/or storage of aeronautical data and digital data sets

questions.explanation: Section 3.4.1: 'Digital data error detection techniques shall be used during the transmission and/or storage of aeronautical data and digital data sets.'

questions.question: True or False: Section 3.6.2 recommends that quality management should be applicable only to the distribution part of the aeronautical data chain.
questions.explanation: Section 3.6.2: 'Recommendation. Quality management should be applicable to the whole aeronautical data chain from data origination to distribution to the next intended user, taking into consideration the intended use of data.'

questions.question: According to section 3.6.6, what must the quality management system provide to users?

options.B: The necessary assurance and confidence that distributed data satisfies quality requirements

questions.explanation: Section 3.6.6: 'The established quality management system shall provide users with the necessary assurance and confidence that distributed aeronautical data and aeronautical information satisfy the aeronautical data quality requirements.'

questions.question: How shall demonstration of compliance of the quality management system be achieved according to section 3.6.8?
questions.explanation: Section 3.6.8: 'Demonstration of compliance of the quality management system applied shall be by audit. If nonconformity is identified, initiating action to correct its cause shall be determined and taken without undue delay.'

questions.sub_chapter: 3.3 Aeronautical data and aeronautical information verification and validation
questions.question: According to section 3.3.1, what must be done with material to be issued as part of an aeronautical information product before it is submitted to the AIS?

options.A: It must be approved by the State
options.B: It must be thoroughly checked to ensure all necessary information is included and correct in detail
options.C: It must be translated into English
options.E: It must be stored in a database
options.F: It must be converted to digital format

questions.explanation: Section 3.3.1: 'Material to be issued as part of an aeronautical information product shall be thoroughly checked before it is submitted to the AIS in order to ensure that all necessary information has been included and that it is correct in detail.'

questions.question: What does section 3.5.3 require automation to enable?

options.B: Digital aeronautical data exchange between parties involved in the data processing chain and use of globally interoperable exchange models

questions.explanation: Section 3.5.3: 'In order to meet the data quality requirements, automation shall: a) enable digital aeronautical data exchange between the parties involved in the data processing chain; and b) use aeronautical information exchange models and data exchange models designed to be globally interoperable.'

questions.question: According to section 3.6.3, what does the quality management system recommended to follow?

options.B: ISO 9000 series of quality assurance standards and be certified by an accredited certification body

questions.explanation: Section 3.6.3: 'Recommendation. The quality management system established in accordance with 3.6.1 should follow the ISO 9000 series of quality assurance standards and be certified by an accredited certification body.'

questions.question: According to section 3.2.5, how is timeliness of aeronautical data ensured?

options.B: By including limits on the effective period of the data elements
options.D: By automatic deletion of old data

questions.explanation: Section 3.2.5: 'Timeliness of aeronautical data shall be ensured by including limits on the effective period of the data elements.'

questions.topic: Quality management - Error correction and communication
questions.question: True or False: Section 3.6.5 requires that the quality management system include policies to allow data anomalies to be corrected and communicated to affected users.
questions.explanation: Section 3.6.5: 'Each quality management system shall include the necessary policies, processes and procedures, including those for the use of metadata, to ensure and verify that aeronautical data is traceable throughout the aeronautical information data chain so as to allow any data anomalies or errors detected in use to be identified by root cause, corrected and communicated to affected users.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 4. AERONAUTICAL DATA AND AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION SCOPE AND METADATA
questions.sub_chapter: 4.1 Scope of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.question: According to section 4.1.1, which of the following is NOT listed as a sub-domain of aeronautical data to be managed by the AIS?
questions.explanation: Section 4.1.1 lists: a) national regulations, rules and procedures; b) aerodromes and heliports; c) airspace; d) ATS routes; e) instrument flight procedures; f) radio navigation aids/systems; g) obstacles; h) terrain; and i) geographic information. Aircraft registration numbers are not included.

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 4. AERONAUTICAL DATA AND AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION SCOPE AND METADATA
questions.question: What does section 4.2 require regarding metadata?

options.A: Metadata is optional for digital data
options.B: Metadata shall be collected for aeronautical data processes and exchange points throughout the data chain
options.C: Metadata only applies to obstacle data
options.D: Metadata is required only for AIP
options.E: Metadata is defined as the data itself
options.F: Metadata is prohibited for security reasons

questions.explanation: Section 4.2.1: 'Metadata shall be collected for aeronautical data processes and exchange points.' Section 4.2.2: 'Metadata collection shall be applied throughout the aeronautical information data chain, from origination to distribution to the next intended user.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 4. AERONAUTICAL DATA AND AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION SCOPE AND METADATA
questions.sub_chapter: 4.1 Scope of aeronautical data and aeronautical information
questions.topic: Data determination - Accuracy and integrity classification
questions.question: According to section 4.1.2, determination and reporting of aeronautical data shall be in accordance with what?

options.B: The accuracy and integrity classification required to meet the needs of the end-user
options.D: The type of aircraft using the data
options.F: The format of the data product

questions.explanation: Section 4.1.2: 'Determination and reporting of aeronautical data shall be in accordance with the accuracy and integrity classification required to meet the needs of the end-user of aeronautical data.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: According to section 5.1.2, when aeronautical data are provided in multiple formats, processes must ensure consistency between formats.
questions.explanation: Section 5.1.2: 'When aeronautical data and aeronautical information are provided in multiple formats, processes shall be implemented to ensure data and information consistency between formats.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.sub_chapter: 5.2 Aeronautical information in a standardized presentation
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid use of an Aeronautical Information Circular (AIC) according to section 5.2.4.1?

options.A: Long-term forecast of major changes in legislation
options.B: Information of an explanatory or advisory nature liable to affect flight safety
options.C: Immediate notification of a temporary runway closure (short duration)
options.D: Information of an explanatory nature concerning technical matters
options.E: Information of an advisory nature concerning legislative matters

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.4.1 lists allowed uses, and section 5.2.4.2 says 'An AIC shall not be used for information that qualifies for inclusion in AIP and NOTAM.' Temporary runway closure is NOTAM information. Also 5.2.4.1 does not include immediate short-duration operational changes.

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.2.5.1, which aeronautical chart may be included in the AIP using a page pocket on appropriate electronic media?

options.B: Aerodrome Terrain and Obstacle Chart ICAO (Electronic)

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.5.1 lists chart types and a note: 'A page pocket may be used in the AIP to include the Aerodrome Terrain and Obstacle Chart ICAO (Electronic) on appropriate electronic media.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: A checklist of valid NOTAM shall be regularly provided according to section 5.2.6.
questions.explanation: Section 5.2.6 states: 'A checklist of valid NOTAM shall be regularly provided.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: Which digital data sets are listed in section 5.3.1.1?

options.A: AIP data set, terrain data sets, obstacle data sets, aerodrome mapping data sets, instrument flight procedure data sets
options.B: Weather data sets, traffic flow data sets, radar data sets
options.C: Aircraft performance data sets, fuel data sets, weight and balance data sets
options.D: NOTAM data sets, AIC data sets, chart data sets
options.E: Communication data sets, navigation data sets, surveillance data sets
options.F: Airport diagram data sets, approach lighting data sets, runway friction data sets

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.1.1: 'Digital data shall be in the form of the following data sets: a) AIP data set; b) terrain data sets; c) obstacle data sets; d) aerodrome mapping data sets; and e) instrument flight procedure data sets.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.1, Area 1 for terrain and obstacle data sets covers what?

options.A: The area within 45 km of an aerodrome
options.B: The entire territory of a State
options.C: The movement area of an aerodrome
options.D: Areas bordering the aerodrome movement area
options.E: The area extending 900 m prior to runway threshold
options.F: The take‑off flight path area only

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.1: 'The coverage areas for terrain and obstacle data sets shall be specified as: - Area 1: the entire territory of a State;'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.3.3.3.2 requires terrain data to be provided for Area 1 only if the State chooses to do so.
questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.3.2 states: 'Terrain data shall be provided for Area 1.' This is a mandatory requirement, not optional.

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.4.2, how should obstacle data be treated relative to terrain data sets?

options.A: Obstacle data may be combined with terrain data sets
options.B: Obstacle data shall not be included in terrain data sets
options.C: Obstacle data must be stored in the same file as terrain data
options.D: Obstacle data is optional for terrain data sets
options.E: Terrain data sets must include all obstacles

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.2: 'Obstacle data shall not be included in terrain data sets.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What is the requirement for obstacle data in Area 1 according to section 5.3.3.4.3?

options.B: Obstacles whose height is 100 m or higher above ground

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.3: 'Obstacle data shall be provided for obstacles in Area 1 whose height is 100 m or higher above ground.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.4.1.3, what is recommended for the provision of aeronautical information products?

options.B: Global communication networks such as the Internet should be employed whenever practicable
options.C: Exclusive use of the aeronautical fixed service

questions.explanation: Section 5.4.1.3: 'Recommendation. - Global communication networks such as the Internet should, whenever practicable, be employed for the provision of aeronautical information products.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: According to section 5.5.1, pre-flight aeronautical information must be made available only to flight crews, not to flight operations personnel.
questions.explanation: Section 5.5.1: 'aeronautical information relative to the route stages originating at the aerodrome/heliport shall be made available to flight operations personnel, including flight crews and services responsible for pre-flight information.' Thus it includes flight operations personnel.

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.6.1, what type of information shall be received from flight crews regarding air navigation facilities?

options.B: Information concerning the state and operation of air navigation facilities or services

questions.explanation: Section 5.6.1: 'For any aerodrome/heliport used for international air operations, arrangements shall be made to receive information concerning the state and operation of air navigation facilities or services noted by flight crews.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.sub_chapter: 5.2.1 Aeronautical information in a standardized presentation
questions.question: According to section 5.2.1.1, in what forms shall the AIP, AIP Amendment, AIP Supplement and AIC be provided?

options.C: On paper and/or as an electronic document
options.D: As a digital data set only

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.1.1: 'The AIP, AIP Amendment, AIP Supplement and AIC shall be provided on paper and/or as an electronic document.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.2.2 requires that the AIP include a statement of the competent authority responsible for the air navigation facilities, services or procedures covered.
questions.explanation: Section 5.2.2 lists what the AIP shall include, including 'a) a statement of the competent authority responsible for the air navigation facilities, services or procedures covered by the AIP;'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.2.4.3, how often shall the validity of AIC currently in force be reviewed?

options.F: Only when a new AIC is issued

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.4.3: 'The validity of AIC currently in force shall be reviewed at least once a year.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.2.5.5 say about chart resolution of aeronautical data?

options.A: It is the same for all charts
options.B: It shall be that as specified for a particular chart
options.C: It is determined by the AIS
options.E: It must be the highest possible resolution
options.F: It is the same as data resolution

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.5.5: 'The chart resolution of aeronautical data shall be that as specified for a particular chart.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.1.2, what must be provided together with each data set to the next intended user?

options.A: A paper copy of the data
options.B: At least the minimum set of metadata that ensures traceability

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.1.2: 'Each data set shall be provided to the next intended user together with at least the minimum set of metadata that ensures traceability.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.3.2.1 recommends that an AIP data set should be provided covering the extent of information as provided in the AIP.
questions.explanation: Section 5.3.2.1: 'Recommendation. An AIP data set should be provided covering the extent of information as provided in the AIP.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.1, Area 2c for terrain and obstacle data sets is defined as:

options.A: An area extending from the ends of Area 2a with a length of 10 km
options.B: An area extending outside Area 2a and Area 2b at a distance of not more than 10 km from the boundary of Area 2a
options.C: The entire territory of a State
options.D: The area bordering the movement area extending 90 m from runway centre line
options.E: The area extending 900 m prior to runway threshold
options.F: An area up to 45 km from the aerodrome reference point

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.1: 'Area 2c: an area extending outside Area 2a and Area 2b at a distance of not more than 10 km from the boundary of Area 2a;'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.topic: Terrain data - Area 4 for Cat II/III
questions.question: For which runways must terrain data be provided for Area 4 according to section 5.3.3.3.8?

options.B: Runways where precision approach Category II or III operations have been established
options.E: Only runways used by international flights
options.F: All runways with a length over 2000 m

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.3.8: 'For aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation, terrain data shall be provided for Area 4 for all runways where precision approach Category II or III operations have been established and where detailed terrain information is required by operators to enable them to assess the effect of terrain on decision height determination by use of radio altimeters.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.4.5 a), what is the height of the Area 2a obstacle collection surface?

options.A: 2 m above the nearest runway elevation
options.B: 3 m above the nearest runway elevation measured along the runway centre line

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.5 a): 'The Area 2a obstacle collection surface shall have a height of 3m above the nearest runway elevation measured along the runway centre line, and for those portions related to a clearway, if one exists, at the elevation of the nearest runway end;'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.3.4.2 recommends that aerodrome mapping data sets should be made available for all aerodromes, regardless of international use.
questions.explanation: Section 5.3.4.2: 'Recommendation. Aerodrome mapping data sets should be made available for aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation.' Not for all aerodromes.

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.4.2.3, what shall be employed for NOTAM distribution whenever practicable?
questions.explanation: Section 5.4.2.3: 'The aeronautical fixed service (AFS) shall, whenever practicable, be employed for NOTAM distribution.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: When a NOTAM is sent by means other than the AFS, what must be used preceding the text?

options.B: A six-digit date-time group indicating the date and time of NOTAM origination, and the identification of the originator

questions.explanation: Section 5.4.2.4: 'When a NOTAM is sent by means other than the AFS, a six-digit date-time group indicating the date and time of NOTAM origination, and the identification of the originator shall be used, preceding the text.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.5.2, what form may be used to make available recapitulation of valid NOTAM of operational significance?
questions.explanation: Section 5.5.2 Note 2: 'A recapitulation of valid NOTAM of operational significance and other information of urgent character may be made available to flight crews in the form of plain-language pre-flight information bulletins (PIB).'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.6.3 requires arrangements to receive information about wildlife hazards from flight crews.
questions.explanation: Section 5.6.3: 'For any aerodrome/heliport used for international air operations, arrangements shall be made to receive information concerning the presence of wildlife hazards observed by flight crews.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: According to section 5.2.4.2, an AIC may be used for information that qualifies for inclusion in the AIP.
questions.explanation: Section 5.2.4.2: 'An AIC shall not be used for information that qualifies for inclusion in AIP and NOTAM.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.topic: Obstacle data - Area 4 for Cat II/III
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.4.10, for which runways must obstacle data be provided for Area 4?

options.B: Runways where precision approach Category II or III operations have been established

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.10: 'For aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation, obstacle data shall be provided for Area 4 for all runways where precision approach Category II or III operations have been established.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.4.2.6 say about distribution of NOTAM series other than those distributed internationally?

options.B: The originating State shall, upon request, grant distribution
options.F: They are only for domestic use and cannot be shared

questions.explanation: Section 5.4.2.6: 'The originating State shall, upon request, grant distribution of NOTAM series other than those distributed internationally.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.2, when should the length of Area 4 be extended beyond 900 m from the runway threshold?

options.A: Always for all precision approach runways
options.B: Where the terrain at a distance greater than 900 m from the runway threshold is mountainous or otherwise significant, up to a distance not exceeding 2000 m
options.F: For all runways with instrument approaches

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.2: 'Recommendation.—Where the terrain at a distance greater than 900 m (3 000 ft) from the runway threshold is mountainous or otherwise significant, the length of Area 4 should be extended to a distance not exceeding 2 000 m (6 500 ft) from the runway threshold.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.3.3.3.7 recommend regarding terrain data for Area 3?

options.A: Terrain data should be provided for Area 3 for aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation
options.B: Terrain data is mandatory for Area 3 for all aerodromes
options.C: Terrain data shall not be provided for Area 3
options.D: Area 3 terrain data is only for military aerodromes
options.E: Terrain data for Area 3 is optional for all aerodromes
options.F: Area 3 terrain data is replaced by obstacle data

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.3.7: 'Recommendation.—For aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation, terrain data should be provided for Area 3.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.3.3.3.9 recommends that if additional terrain data is collected to meet other aeronautical requirements, the terrain data sets should be expanded to include this additional data.
questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.3.9: 'Recommendation.—Where additional terrain data is collected to meet other aeronautical requirements, the terrain data sets should be expanded to include this additional data.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.3.3.4.6, what is the height threshold below which obstacle data need not be collected in Area 2c?

options.F: No threshold, all obstacles must be collected

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.6: '...except that data need not be collected for obstacles less than a height of 3 m above ground in Area 2b and less than a height of 15 m above ground in Area 2c.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.3.3.4.9 recommend for obstacle data in Area 3?

options.A: Obstacle data should be provided for obstacles that penetrate a collection surface extending 0.5 m above the horizontal plane through the nearest point on the movement area
options.B: No obstacle data is needed in Area 3
options.C: Only obstacles higher than 3 m need to be collected
options.D: Obstacle data is mandatory for all obstacles in Area 3
options.E: Area 3 obstacle data is only for temporary obstacles
options.F: Obstacle data should be provided for obstacles that penetrate 1.2% slope

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.3.4.9: 'Recommendation.—For aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation, obstacle data should be provided for Area 3 for obstacles that penetrate the relevant obstacle data collection surface extending a half-metre (0.5 m) above the horizontal plane passing through the nearest point on the aerodrome movement area.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.4.1.2, how shall AIP, AIP Amendments, AIP Supplements and AIC be made available?
questions.explanation: Section 5.4.1.2: 'AIP, AIP Amendments, AIP Supplements and AIC shall be made available by the most expeditious means.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.4.2.7 recommends that selective distribution lists should be used when practicable for NOTAM.
questions.explanation: Section 5.4.2.7: 'Recommendation. - Selective distribution lists should be used when practicable.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.2.3 require regarding AIP Supplements?

options.A: A checklist of valid AIP Supplements shall be regularly provided
options.C: AIP Supplements must be reissued annually
options.D: Only one AIP Supplement is allowed at a time
options.E: AIP Supplements are not required for international distribution

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.3: 'A checklist of valid AIP Supplements shall be regularly provided.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: According to section 5.2.4.4, a checklist of currently valid AIC shall be provided. How often?

options.E: Only when a new AIC is issued

questions.explanation: Section 5.2.4.4: 'A checklist of currently valid AIC shall be regularly provided. Note.- Detailed specifications concerning the frequency for providing checklists of valid AIC are contained in the PANS-AIM (Doc 10066).'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: True or False: Section 5.3.1.3 requires that a checklist of valid data sets shall be regularly provided.
questions.explanation: Section 5.3.1.3: 'A checklist of valid data sets shall be regularly provided.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.topic: Instrument flight procedure data sets - Recommendation
questions.question: According to section 5.3.5.2, for which aerodromes should instrument flight procedure data sets be made available?

options.B: Aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation
options.F: Only aerodromes with Category II/III approaches

questions.explanation: Section 5.3.5.2: 'Recommendation. - Instrument flight procedure data sets should be made available for aerodromes regularly used by international civil aviation.'

questions.chapter: CHAPTER 5. AERONAUTICAL INFORMATION PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
questions.question: What does section 5.5.2 Note 1 indicate about pre-flight information from adjacent States?

options.A: It must be provided regardless of cost
options.B: The elements of aeronautical information products may be limited to national publications and when practicable, those of adjacent States, provided a complete library is available centrally with direct communications
options.C: Adjacent States' information is never required
options.D: Only NOTAM from adjacent States is needed
options.E: Adjacent States must provide their AIP free of charge
options.F: Pre-flight information must include all States worldwide

questions.explanation: Section 5.5.2 Note 1: 'The elements of aeronautical information products may be limited to national publications and when practicable, those of adjacent States, provided a complete library of aeronautical information is available at a central location and means of direct communications are available with that library.'

questions.question: What is the interval of common effective dates for the AIRAC system according to section 6.2.1?
questions.explanation: Section 6.2.1: '...basing establishment, withdrawal or significant changes upon a series of common effective dates at intervals of 28 days, including 8 November 2018.'

questions.question: How many days in advance of the effective date must AIRAC information be made available to recipients?
questions.explanation: Section 6.2.3: 'Information provided under the AIRAC system shall be made available by the aeronautical information service (AIS) so as to reach recipients at least 28 days in advance of the effective date.' Note: AIS distributes at least 42 days in advance to achieve this, but the requirement for receipt is 28 days.

questions.question: According to section 6.3.1.3, how should temporary changes of long duration (three months or longer) be published?

options.F: As a digital data set only

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.1.3: 'Temporary changes of long duration (three months or longer) and information of short duration which contains extensive text and/or graphics shall be published as AIP Supplements.'

questions.question: What is a Trigger NOTAM according to section 6.3.2.1?

options.A: A NOTAM that warns of military exercises
options.B: A NOTAM originated when an AIP Amendment or AIP Supplement is published in accordance with AIRAC procedures
options.C: A NOTAM that cancels all previous NOTAM
options.D: A NOTAM that is issued only for volcanic ash
options.E: A NOTAM that triggers automatic airport lighting
options.F: A NOTAM that is never used internationally

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.1: 'When an AIP Amendment or an AIP Supplement is published in accordance with AIRAC procedures, a Trigger NOTAM shall be originated.'

questions.question: True or False: According to section 6.3.2.4, a NOTAM may be used to notify routine maintenance work on aprons that does not affect safe movement of aircraft.
questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.4 lists information that shall NOT be notified by NOTAM, including 'a) routine maintenance work on aprons and taxiways which does not affect the safe movement of aircraft;'

questions.question: According to section 6.3.3.5, what must be synchronized?

options.B: AIP and digital data set updates

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.3.5: 'Updates to AIP and digital data sets shall be synchronized.'

questions.question: According to section 6.2.2, after an AIRAC effective date, when can the information be changed?

options.B: Not for at least another 28 days, unless the circumstance is temporary and would not persist for the full period

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.2: 'The information notified under the AIRAC system shall not be changed further for at least another 28 days after the effective date, unless the circumstance notified is of a temporary nature and would not persist for the full period.'

questions.question: When information has not been submitted by the AIRAC date, what must be distributed according to section 6.2.4?

options.B: A NIL notification not later than one cycle before the AIRAC effective date concerned
options.F: A NOTAM with the missing information

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.4: 'When information has not been submitted by the AIRAC date, a NIL notification shall be distributed not later than one cycle before the AIRAC effective date concerned.'

questions.question: According to section 6.2.5, what shall not be used for pre-planned operationally significant changes requiring cartographic work or updating of navigation databases?

options.B: Implementation dates other than AIRAC effective dates

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.5: 'Implementation dates other than AIRAC effective dates shall not be used for pre-planned operationally significant changes requiring cartographic work and/or for updating of navigation databases.'

questions.question: What does section 6.2.7 recommend regarding advance notice for major changes?

options.A: Information should be made available at least 28 days in advance
options.B: Information should be made available at least 56 days in advance
options.C: Information should be made available at least 90 days in advance
options.E: Information should be made available on the effective date
options.F: Information should be made available at least 14 days in advance

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.7: 'Recommendation. Whenever major changes are planned and where advance notice is desirable and practicable, information should be made available by the AIS so as to reach recipients at least 56 days in advance of the effective date.'

questions.question: According to section 6.3.2.3 m), what type of hazards affecting air navigation must be notified by NOTAM?

options.B: Hazards not otherwise promulgated, including obstacles, military exercises, radio frequency interferences, rocket launches, displays, fireworks, sky lanterns, rocket debris, races and major parachuting events

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.3 m): 'presence of hazards not otherwise promulgated, which affect air navigation (including obstacles, military exercises and operations, intentional and unintentional radio frequency interferences, rocket launches, displays, fireworks, sky lanterns, rocket debris, races and major parachuting events);'

questions.question: True or False: Section 6.3.2.4 states that partial temporary failure of air-ground communications when suitable alternative frequencies are known and operative shall be notified by NOTAM.
questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.4 e): 'partial temporary failure of air-ground communications when suitable alternative frequencies are known to be available and are operative' is listed as information that shall NOT be notified by NOTAM.

questions.question: According to section 6.3.3.2, how shall permanent changes and temporary changes of long duration (three months or longer) be issued as digital data?

options.A: Only as a complete data set
options.B: Only as a subset that includes only the differences
options.C: In the form of a complete data set or a subset that includes only the differences from the previously issued complete data set

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.3.2: 'Permanent changes and temporary changes of long duration (three months or longer) made available as digital data shall be issued in the form of a complete data set or a subset that includes only the differences from the previously issued complete data set.'

questions.question: True or False: According to section 6.2.6, the AIRAC system should be used for the provision of information relating to temporary danger areas and navigational hazards.
questions.explanation: Section 6.2.6: 'Recommendation. The regulated system (AIRAC) should be used for the provision of information relating to the establishment and withdrawal of, and premeditated significant changes in, the circumstances listed below: ... d) temporary danger, prohibited and restricted areas and navigational hazards, military exercises and mass movements of aircraft;'

questions.question: According to section 6.3.2.3, which of the following must be notified by NOTAM?

options.A: Routine maintenance work on aprons that does not affect safe movement
options.B: Establishment, withdrawal or significant changes in operation of aerodrome(s) or runways
options.C: Partial failure of aerodrome lighting that does not directly affect aircraft operations
options.E: Unserviceability of location signs on movement area
options.F: Parachuting in uncontrolled airspace under VFR

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.3 a): 'establishment, closure or significant changes in operation of aerodrome(s) or heliport(s) or runways' must be notified by NOTAM. Options A, C, D, E, F are listed in 6.3.2.4 as information that shall NOT be notified by NOTAM.

questions.topic: AIRAC - 56-day advance notice for major changes
questions.question: According to section 6.2.7, which of the following is listed as a circumstance where information should be made available at least 56 days in advance?

options.B: New aerodromes for international IFR operations

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.7 lists: 'a) new aerodromes for international instrument flight rules (IFR) operations; b) new runways for IFR operations at international aerodromes; c) design and structure of the ATS route network; d) design and structure of a set of terminal procedures; e) circumstances listed in 6.2.1 if the entire State or any significant portion thereof is affected or if cross-border coordination is required.'

questions.question: What does section 6.3.1.2 state about permanent changes to the AIP?

options.A: They shall be published as AIP Supplements
options.B: They shall be published as AIP Amendments
options.C: They shall be published as NOTAM
options.D: They shall be published as AIC
options.E: They shall be published only in digital format
options.F: They require no publication if minor

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.1.2: 'Permanent changes to the AIP shall be published as AIP Amendments.'

questions.question: According to section 6.3.2.3, when must a NOTAM be originated concerning conflict zones?

options.B: Conflict zones which affect air navigation, including information as specific as possible about the nature and extent of threats and consequences for civil aviation
options.C: Only for zones designated by ICAO
options.D: Never, conflict zones are notified by other means
options.E: Only after a formal declaration of war
options.F: Only for zones over high seas

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.3 n): 'conflict zones which affect air navigation (to include information that is as specific as possible regarding the nature and extent of threats of that conflict and its consequences for civil aviation);'

questions.topic: NOTAM - Back-up systems with operational impact
questions.question: True or False: Section 6.3.2.3 states that unavailability of back-up and secondary systems having a direct operational impact shall be notified by NOTAM.
questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.3 d): 'unavailability of back-up and secondary systems, having a direct operational impact' shall be notified by NOTAM. (Compare with 6.3.2.4 j which excludes back-up systems without operational impact.)

questions.question: According to section 6.3.2.4, which of the following is NOT permitted to be notified by NOTAM?

options.A: Closure of movement area parts for work locally coordinated of duration less than one hour
options.B: Establishment of a new prohibited area
options.C: Volcanic ash cloud affecting flight levels
options.D: Change in rescue and firefighting category
options.E: Outbreak of epidemics necessitating quarantine changes

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.4 q): 'closure of movement area parts in connection with planned work locally coordinated of duration of less than one hour' is listed as information that shall NOT be notified by NOTAM. The other options are listed in 6.3.2.3 as requiring NOTAM.

questions.topic: Data set updates - Indication of differences
questions.question: What does section 6.3.3.3 recommend regarding reissued data sets?

options.A: The differences from the previously issued complete data set should be indicated
options.B: Only the complete data set should be provided without changes marked
options.C: No indication of changes is required
options.D: Changes must be highlighted in a separate NOTAM
options.F: The previous data set must be retained

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.3.3: 'Recommendation. When made available as a completely reissued data set, the differences from the previously issued complete data set should be indicated.'

questions.topic: Digital NOTAM - Same model as data set
questions.question: According to section 6.3.3.4, what should temporary changes of short duration made available as digital data use?

options.A: A different model from the complete data set
options.B: The same aeronautical information model as the complete data set
options.E: A special digital NOTAM format not aligned with the data set

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.3.4: 'Recommendation. When temporary changes of short duration are made available as digital data (digital NOTAM), they should use the same aeronautical information model as the complete data set.'

questions.question: According to section 6.1, what is the general specification for aeronautical data and information?

options.A: They shall be kept up to date
options.B: They shall be archived after 10 years
options.D: They shall be replaced every 5 years
options.E: They shall be stored in multiple locations

questions.explanation: Section 6.1: 'Aeronautical data and aeronautical information shall be kept up to date.'

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed in section 6.2.1 as a circumstance requiring distribution under the AIRAC system?

options.B: Positions and frequencies of radio navigation aids
options.C: Aerodrome ground operating procedures including low visibility procedures
options.D: Temporary danger areas of short duration (less than 28 days)

questions.explanation: Section 6.2.1 lists permanent or significant changes to various elements. Temporary danger areas are listed in 6.2.6 as a recommendation but not in the mandatory list of 6.2.1. Also, temporary short-duration items are typically NOTAM, not AIRAC.

questions.question: True or False: According to section 6.3.2.3, a NOTAM shall be originated for the allocation, cancellation or change of location indicators.
questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.3 s): 'allocation, cancellation or change of location indicators' is listed as information that shall be originated and issued as NOTAM.

questions.question: According to section 6.3.2.4, which of the following information shall NOT be notified by NOTAM?

options.A: General reminders on already published information
options.C: Change in rescue and firefighting category
options.F: Presence of obstacles affecting air navigation

questions.explanation: Section 6.3.2.4 n): 'general reminders on already published information' is listed as information that shall NOT be notified by NOTAM. The other options are listed in 6.3.2.3 as requiring NOTAM.

questions.question: According to the document, what is the meaning of the term 'Accepting unit/controller'?

options.A: Air traffic control unit/air traffic controller next to take control of an aircraft
options.B: Air traffic control unit/air traffic controller that transfers responsibility for an aircraft
options.C: Air traffic control unit that provides flight information service
options.D: Air traffic control unit that issues air traffic control clearances
options.E: The unit responsible for alerting service
options.F: The unit that accepts flight plans

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Accepting unit/controller. Air traffic control unit/air traffic controller next to take control of an aircraft.'

questions.question: What is an ADS-C agreement?

options.A: A contract between two air traffic control units for the transfer of radar data
options.B: A reporting plan which establishes the conditions of ADS-C data reporting
options.C: An agreement between a pilot and a controller for voice communications
options.D: A contract for the use of secondary surveillance radar codes
options.E: An agreement for the provision of air traffic advisory service
options.F: A plan for the strategic lateral offset of aircraft

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'ADS-C agreement. A reporting plan which establishes the conditions of ADS-C data reporting (i.e. data required by the air traffic services unit and frequency of ADS-C reports which have to be agreed to prior to using ADS-C in the provision of air traffic services).'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Advisory airspace'?

options.A: An airspace where air traffic control service is provided to all flights
options.B: An airspace of defined dimensions within which air traffic advisory service is available
options.C: An airspace reserved for military operations
options.D: An airspace where only VFR flights are permitted
options.E: An airspace where no air traffic services are provided
options.F: An airspace designated for supersonic flight

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Advisory airspace. An airspace of defined dimensions, or designated route, within which air traffic advisory service is available.'

questions.question: According to the document, which aircraft types are classified as 'SUPER' for wake turbulence?

options.A: Aircraft types of 136,000 kg or more
options.B: Aircraft types specified as such in Doc 8643, Aircraft Type Designators
options.C: Aircraft types with a wing span greater than 80 m
options.E: Aircraft types with maximum take-off mass less than 7,000 kg
options.F: Aircraft types with maximum take-off mass between 7,000 kg and 136,000 kg

questions.explanation: The document states: 'SUPER (J) - aircraft types specified as such in Doc 8643, Aircraft Type Designators.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Aerodrome control tower'?

options.A: A unit that provides approach control service
options.B: A unit established to provide air traffic control service to aerodrome traffic
options.C: A unit that provides area control service
options.D: A unit that provides flight information service
options.E: A unit that provides alerting service only
options.F: A unit that coordinates military traffic

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Aerodrome control tower. A unit established to provide air traffic control service to aerodrome traffic.'

questions.question: What constitutes 'Aerodrome traffic'?

options.A: Only aircraft landing and taking off
options.B: All traffic on the manoeuvring area of an aerodrome and all aircraft flying in the vicinity of an aerodrome
options.C: Only aircraft in the traffic circuit
options.E: Only IFR flights within 5 NM of the aerodrome
options.F: Only military aircraft operating at the aerodrome

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aerodrome traffic. All traffic on the manoeuvring area of an aerodrome and all aircraft flying in the vicinity of an aerodrome.'

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Aircraft proximity (AIRPROX) risk classification: Risk of collision'?

options.B: Serious risk of collision has existed
options.C: No risk of collision has existed
options.D: Safety of the aircraft may have been compromised
options.E: The aircraft have passed within 500 feet vertically
options.F: The aircraft have passed within 1 NM horizontally

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Risk of collision. The risk classification of an aircraft proximity in which serious risk of collision has existed.'

questions.question: What is AIRMET information?

options.B: Information concerning the occurrence or expected occurrence of specified en-route weather phenomena which may affect the safety of low-level aircraft operations
options.D: Information about moderate or severe turbulence at high levels

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'AIRMET information. Information issued by a meteorological watch office concerning the occurrence or expected occurrence of specified en-route weather phenomena which may affect the safety of low-level aircraft operations and which was not already included in the forecast issued for low-level flights in the flight information region concerned or sub-area thereof.'

questions.question: What is 'Air-taxiing' as defined in the document?

options.A: Movement of a helicopter on the ground under its own power
options.B: Movement of a helicopter/VTOL above the surface of an aerodrome, normally in ground effect and at a ground speed normally less than 37 km/h (20 kt)
options.C: Movement of an aircraft on the apron using a tug
options.D: Taxiing of aircraft on a runway
options.F: Movement of aircraft between parking positions

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Air-taxiing. Movement of a helicopter/VTOL above the surface of an aerodrome, normally in ground effect and at a ground speed normally less than 37 km/h (20 kt).'

questions.question: What are the objectives of Air Traffic Control service?
