options.A: Preventing collisions between aircraft and expediting and maintaining an orderly flow of air traffic

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Air traffic control service. A service provided for the purpose of: a) preventing collisions: 1) between aircraft, and 2) on the manoeuvring area between aircraft and obstructions; and b) expediting and maintaining an orderly flow of air traffic.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Air traffic flow management (ATFM)'?

options.A: A service that provides weather forecasts to aircraft
options.B: A service established to contribute to a safe, orderly and expeditious flow of air traffic by ensuring ATC capacity is utilized to the maximum extent possible
options.C: A service that separates aircraft in the en-route phase
options.D: A service that provides alerting for search and rescue
options.E: A service that manages airport ground movements
options.F: A service that coordinates military airspace reservations

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Air traffic flow management (ATFM). A service established with the objective of contributing to a safe, orderly and expeditious flow of air traffic by ensuring that ATC capacity is utilized to the maximum extent possible, and that the traffic volume is compatible with the capacities declared by the appropriate ATS authority.'

questions.question: What is an 'Alternate aerodrome'?

options.A: An aerodrome to which an aircraft may proceed when it becomes impossible or inadvisable to proceed to or land at the aerodrome of intended landing
options.B: An aerodrome used only for training flights
options.C: An aerodrome designated for military use only
options.D: An aerodrome that has no control tower
options.E: An aerodrome that is always available for emergency landings
options.F: An aerodrome that is used as a hub for connecting flights

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Alternate aerodrome. An aerodrome to which an aircraft may proceed when it becomes either impossible or inadvisable to proceed to or to land at the aerodrome of intended landing where the necessary services and facilities are available, where aircraft performance requirements can be met and which is operational at the expected time of use.'

questions.question: What is the difference between altitude and height?

options.A: Altitude is measured from mean sea level, height is measured from a specified datum
options.B: Altitude is measured from ground level, height is measured from sea level
options.C: Altitude is used only for IFR flights, height for VFR flights
options.D: Altitude is expressed in feet, height in meters
options.E: Altitude is a vertical distance above a reference point, height is a horizontal distance
options.F: There is no difference; they are synonymous

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Altitude. The vertical distance of a level, a point or an object considered as a point, measured from mean sea level (MSL).' And: 'Height. The vertical distance of a level, a point or an object considered as a point, measured from a specified datum.'

questions.question: What is an 'Appropriate authority' for flight over the high seas?

options.A: The relevant authority of the State having sovereignty over the territory being overflown
options.B: The relevant authority of the State of Registry
options.D: The air traffic control unit providing service

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Appropriate authority. a) Regarding flight over the high seas: The relevant authority of the State of Registry.'

questions.question: What is Area navigation (RNAV)?

options.A: Navigation using only ground-based VOR and DME
options.B: A method of navigation which permits aircraft operation on any desired flight path within the coverage of ground- or space-based navigation aids or within the limits of self-contained aids
options.C: Navigation using only inertial reference systems
options.D: Navigation that requires flying directly over ground-based navigation aids
options.F: Navigation using only GPS without any augmentation

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Area navigation (RNAV). A method of navigation which permits aircraft operation on any desired flight path within the coverage of ground- or space-based navigation aids or within the limits of the capability of self-contained aids, or a combination of these.'

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'ATIS'?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'ATIS. The symbol used to designate automatic terminal information service.'

questions.question: What is a 'Blind transmission'?

options.A: A transmission that is intentionally not acknowledged
options.B: A transmission from one station to another station in circumstances where two-way communication cannot be established but where it is believed that the called station is able to receive the transmission
options.D: A transmission on an emergency frequency
options.E: A transmission that is broadcast to all stations
options.F: A transmission made without using standard phraseology

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Blind transmission. A transmission from one station to another station in circumstances where two-way communication cannot be established but where it is believed that the called station is able to receive the transmission.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Ceiling'?

options.A: The highest altitude at which an aircraft can fly
options.B: The height above the ground or water of the base of the lowest layer of cloud below 6000 m (20000 ft) covering more than half the sky
options.C: The maximum altitude permitted by ATC
options.E: The height of the cloud base above mean sea level
options.F: The altitude at which an aircraft must switch to flight levels

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Ceiling. The height above the ground or water of the base of the lowest layer of cloud below 6000 m (20000 ft) covering more than half the sky.'

questions.question: What is a 'Controlled aerodrome'?

options.A: An aerodrome with a control zone
options.B: An aerodrome at which air traffic control service is provided to aerodrome traffic
options.C: An aerodrome that has radar coverage
options.D: An aerodrome that is open 24 hours
options.E: An aerodrome that has an instrument landing system
options.F: An aerodrome that requires a flight plan for all operations

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Controlled aerodrome. An aerodrome at which air traffic control service is provided to aerodrome traffic.'

questions.question: What is 'Cruise climb'?

options.A: A climb at maximum rate to cruising level
options.B: An aeroplane cruising technique resulting in a net increase in altitude as the aeroplane mass decreases
options.C: A climb performed only during take-off
options.E: A descent technique to save fuel

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Cruise climb. An aeroplane cruising technique resulting in a net increase in altitude as the aeroplane mass decreases.'

questions.question: What is 'Decision altitude (DA) or decision height (DH)'?

options.A: The altitude at which a missed approach is required if the runway is not in sight
options.B: A specified altitude or height in a 3D instrument approach operation at which a missed approach must be initiated if the required visual reference to continue the approach has not been established
options.C: The altitude at which the pilot decides to continue the approach
options.D: The minimum altitude for an instrument approach
options.E: The altitude at which the aircraft must be configured for landing
options.F: The altitude at which the approach is terminated

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Decision altitude (DA) or decision height (DH). A specified altitude or height in a 3D instrument approach operation at which a missed approach must be initiated if the required visual reference to continue the approach has not been established.'

questions.question: What is a 'Discrete code' in SSR?

options.A: A four-digit SSR code with the last two digits not being '00'
options.B: A code assigned to military aircraft only
options.C: A code that is never changed during flight
options.D: A code that is broadcast continuously
options.E: A code that indicates an emergency
options.F: A code that is used only for VFR flights

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Discrete code. A four-digit SSR code with the last two digits not being '00'.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Distress phase'?

options.A: A situation wherein apprehension exists as to the safety of an aircraft
options.B: A situation wherein there is reasonable certainty that an aircraft and its occupants are threatened by grave and imminent danger or require immediate assistance
options.C: A situation wherein uncertainty exists as to the safety of an aircraft
options.D: A situation where an aircraft has declared minimum fuel
options.E: A situation where an aircraft has communication failure
options.F: A situation where an aircraft is unlawfully interfered with

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Distress phase. A situation wherein there is reasonable certainty that an aircraft and its occupants are threatened by grave and imminent danger or require immediate assistance.'

questions.question: What is 'Expected approach time'?

options.A: The time at which an aircraft is expected to land
options.B: The time at which ATC expects that an arriving aircraft, following a delay, will leave the holding fix to complete its approach for a landing
options.C: The estimated time of arrival at the destination aerodrome
options.D: The time at which an aircraft will cross the final approach fix
options.E: The time at which an aircraft should start its descent
options.F: The time at which an aircraft will be cleared for take-off

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Expected approach time. The time at which ATC expects that an arriving aircraft, following a delay, will leave the holding fix to complete its approach for a landing.'

questions.question: What is a 'Flight level'?

options.A: A surface of constant atmospheric pressure which is related to a specific pressure datum, 1013.2 hectopascals (hPa)
options.B: The altitude above mean sea level
options.C: The height above the aerodrome elevation
options.D: The vertical position expressed in feet above ground
options.E: The level at which an aircraft cruises
options.F: The minimum altitude for IFR flight

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Flight level. A surface of constant atmospheric pressure which is related to a specific pressure datum, 1013.2 hectopascals (hPa), and is separated from other such surfaces by specific pressure intervals.'

questions.question: What is a 'Hot spot' on an aerodrome?

options.A: A location with high traffic density
options.B: A location on an aerodrome movement area with a history or potential risk of collision or runway incursion
options.C: A location where aircraft frequently experience wake turbulence
options.D: A location where noise abatement procedures are applied
options.E: A location designated for military aircraft only
options.F: A location where fuel is stored

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Hot spot. A location on an aerodrome movement area with a history or potential risk of collision or runway incursion, and where heightened attention by pilots/drivers is necessary.'

questions.question: What does the code word 'INCERFA' designate?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'INCERFA. The code word used to designate an uncertainty phase.'

questions.question: What is 'Independent parallel approaches'?

options.A: Approaches to parallel runways where vertical separation is required
options.B: Simultaneous approaches to parallel or near-parallel instrument runways where ATS surveillance system separation minima between aircraft on adjacent extended runway centre lines are not prescribed
options.C: Approaches where one runway is used for arrivals and the other for departures
options.D: Approaches that require radar monitoring by a single controller
options.E: Approaches where aircraft are separated by time only

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Independent parallel approaches. Simultaneous approaches to parallel or near-parallel instrument runways where ATS surveillance system separation minima between aircraft on adjacent extended runway centre lines are not prescribed.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Instrument approach operations'?

options.A: An approach using only visual reference
options.B: An approach and landing using instruments for navigation guidance based on an instrument approach procedure
options.C: An approach performed only in IMC
options.D: An approach that requires a clearance from ATC
options.E: An approach that is always conducted at night
options.F: An approach that uses only radar vectors

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Instrument approach operations. An approach and landing using instruments for navigation guidance based on an instrument approach procedure.'

questions.question: What is 'Minimum fuel'?

options.A: A situation where an aircraft has less than 30 minutes of fuel remaining
options.B: A situation in which an aircraft's fuel supply has reached a state where the flight is committed to land at a specific aerodrome and no additional delay can be accepted
options.C: A situation where an aircraft must declare an emergency
options.D: A situation where an aircraft cannot reach its alternate
options.E: A situation where an aircraft has only enough fuel for one approach
options.F: A situation where an aircraft's fuel is below legal requirements

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Minimum fuel. The term used to describe a situation in which an aircraft's fuel supply has reached a state where the flight is committed to land at a specific aerodrome and no additional delay can be accepted.'

questions.question: What is a 'No transgression zone (NTZ)' in the context of independent parallel approaches?

options.A: An area where aircraft are not permitted to fly under any circumstances
options.B: A corridor of airspace of defined dimensions located centrally between the two extended runway centre lines, where a penetration by an aircraft requires a controller intervention to manoeuvre any threatened aircraft on the adjacent approach
options.C: A zone where only departing aircraft are allowed
options.D: An area where wake turbulence separation is not required
options.E: A buffer zone between parallel taxiways
options.F: An area where VFR flights are prohibited

questions.explanation: The document states: 'No transgression zone (NTZ). In the context of independent parallel approaches, a corridor of airspace of defined dimensions located centrally between the two extended runway centre lines, where a penetration by an aircraft requires a controller intervention to manoeuvre any threatened aircraft on the adjacent approach.'

questions.question: What does NOTAM stand for?

options.C: Notice distributed by means of telecommunication containing information concerning the establishment, condition or change in any aeronautical facility, service, procedure or hazard
options.D: Navigation Operational Telecommunication and Aeronautical Message
options.F: Notice of Aeronautical Terrain and Mapping

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'NOTAM. A notice distributed by means of telecommunication containing information concerning the establishment, condition or change in any aeronautical facility, service, procedure or hazard, the timely knowledge of which is essential to personnel concerned with flight operations.'

questions.question: What is 'Operational control'?

options.A: Control exercised by ATC over aircraft
options.B: The exercise of authority over the initiation, continuation, diversion or termination of a flight in the interest of the safety of the aircraft and the regularity and efficiency of the flight
options.D: The control of ground movements at an aerodrome
options.E: The management of air traffic flow

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Operational control. The exercise of authority over the initiation, continuation, diversion or termination of a flight in the interest of the safety of the aircraft and the regularity and efficiency of the flight.'

questions.question: What is 'Performance-based navigation (PBN)'?

options.B: Area navigation based on performance requirements for aircraft operating along an ATS route, on an instrument approach procedure or in a designated airspace
options.E: Navigation that is independent of aircraft equipment
options.F: Navigation that uses only visual references

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Performance-based navigation (PBN). Area navigation based on performance requirements for aircraft operating along an ATS route, on an instrument approach procedure or in a designated airspace.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Radar contact'?

options.A: When an aircraft is seen on radar and identified
options.B: The situation which exists when the radar position of a particular aircraft is seen and identified on a situation display
options.C: When an aircraft responds to a radar interrogation
options.D: When a controller establishes two-way communication with an aircraft
options.E: When an aircraft is within radar coverage
options.F: When a pilot reports radar contact

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Radar contact. The situation which exists when the radar position of a particular aircraft is seen and identified on a situation display.'

questions.question: What is a 'Repetitive flight plan (RPL)'?

options.A: A flight plan filed repeatedly for the same flight
options.B: A flight plan related to a series of frequently recurring, regularly operated individual flights with identical basic features, submitted by an operator for retention and repetitive use by ATS units
options.C: A flight plan that is automatically generated
options.D: A flight plan that is valid for multiple days
options.E: A flight plan that does not require ATC clearance
options.F: A flight plan used only for military operations

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Repetitive flight plan (RPL). A flight plan related to a series of frequently recurring, regularly operated individual flights with identical basic features, submitted by an operator for retention and repetitive use by ATS units.'

questions.question: What is 'RNP type'?

options.A: A classification of aircraft based on navigation accuracy
options.B: A containment value expressed as a distance in nautical miles from the intended position within which flights would be for at least 95 per cent of the total flying time
options.C: The required navigation performance for a specific airspace
options.D: A type of area navigation specification
options.E: A measure of vertical navigation accuracy
options.F: A category of instrument approach procedure

questions.explanation: The document states: 'RNP type. A containment value expressed as a distance in nautical miles from the intended position within which flights would be for at least 95 per cent of the total flying time.'

questions.question: What is a 'Runway incursion'?

options.A: Any occurrence at an aerodrome involving the incorrect presence of an aircraft, vehicle or person on the protected area of a surface designated for the landing and take-off of aircraft
options.B: A collision between two aircraft on the runway
options.D: A vehicle entering the runway without authorization
options.E: Any deviation from the assigned runway
options.F: A failure to vacate the runway after landing

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Runway incursion. Any occurrence at an aerodrome involving the incorrect presence of an aircraft, vehicle or person on the protected area of a surface designated for the landing and take-off of aircraft.'

questions.question: What is 'Shoreline' as defined for ATS purposes?

options.A: The line of the coast at mean high water
options.B: A line following the general contour of the shore, except that in cases of inlets or bays less than 30 nautical miles in width, the line shall pass directly across the inlet or bay to intersect the general contour on the opposite side
options.D: The line where land meets water at low tide
options.E: A line used for defining FIR boundaries
options.F: A line for search and rescue coordination

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Shoreline. A line following the general contour of the shore, except that in cases of inlets or bays less than 30 nautical miles in width, the line shall pass directly across the inlet or bay to intersect the general contour on the opposite side.'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Stopway'?

options.A: A defined rectangular area on the ground at the end of take-off run available prepared as a suitable area in which an aircraft can be stopped in the case of an abandoned take-off
options.B: A taxiway used for aircraft parking
options.D: An area where aircraft can hold before take-off
options.E: A designated area for emergency stopping after landing
options.F: A paved area for aircraft maintenance

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Stopway. A defined rectangular area on the ground at the end of take-off run available prepared as a suitable area in which an aircraft can be stopped in the case of an abandoned take-off.'

questions.question: What is 'Time difference of arrival (TDOA)'?

options.A: The difference in time between an aircraft's estimated and actual arrival
options.B: The difference in relative time that a transponder signal from the same aircraft (or ground vehicle) is received at different receivers
options.C: The time difference between two aircraft passing a fix
options.E: The difference in UTC time between two locations
options.F: The time required for radar signal to return

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Time difference of arrival (TDOA). The difference in relative time that a transponder signal from the same aircraft (or ground vehicle) is received at different receivers.'

questions.question: What is 'Total estimated elapsed time' for IFR flights?

options.A: The estimated time from take-off to landing
options.B: The estimated time from take-off to arrive over that designated point from which an instrument approach procedure will be commenced
options.C: The estimated time from departure gate to arrival gate
options.D: The estimated time from take-off to the destination aerodrome
options.E: The estimated time from departure to the first holding fix
options.F: The estimated time from take-off to the final approach fix

questions.explanation: The document states: 'total estimated elapsed time. For IFR flights, the estimated time required from take-off to arrive over that designated point, defined by reference to navigation aids, from which it is intended that an instrument approach procedure will be commenced, or, if no navigation aid is associated with the destination aerodrome, to arrive over the destination aerodrome.'

questions.question: What is 'Traffic avoidance advice'?

options.A: Information about traffic provided by ATC
options.B: Advice provided by an air traffic services unit specifying manoeuvres to assist a pilot to avoid a collision
options.C: A clearance to deviate from the assigned route
options.D: Instructions to change altitude to avoid traffic
options.E: A warning of potential conflict issued by TCAS

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Traffic avoidance advice. Advice provided by an air traffic services unit specifying manoeuvres to assist a pilot to avoid a collision.'

questions.question: What is the 'Transition layer'?

options.A: The airspace between the transition altitude and the transition level
options.B: The altitude at which pilots change from QNH to standard pressure
options.C: The layer of airspace where VFR flights are not permitted
options.D: The layer between two control areas
options.E: The layer where aircraft change from IFR to VFR
options.F: The layer of airspace with special noise abatement procedures

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Transition layer. The airspace between the transition altitude and the transition level.'

questions.question: According to Chapter 4, what is the maximum speed for air-taxiing of a helicopter?
questions.explanation: The definition of 'Air-taxiing' states: 'Movement of a helicopter/VTOL above the surface of an aerodrome, normally in ground effect and at a ground speed normally less than 37 km/h (20 kt).'

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Fly-by waypoint'?

options.A: A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment
options.B: A waypoint which requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment
options.C: A waypoint that must be overflown before turning
options.D: A waypoint that is only used for departure procedures
options.E: A waypoint that is used for en-route navigation only
options.F: A waypoint that is defined by latitude and longitude

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Fly-by waypoint. A waypoint which requires turn anticipation to allow tangential interception of the next segment of a route or procedure.' (Definitions section)

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Flyover waypoint'?

options.A: A waypoint which requires turn anticipation
options.B: A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment
options.C: A waypoint that must be crossed before turning
options.D: A waypoint used only for arrival procedures
options.E: A waypoint that is not used in RNAV
options.F: A waypoint that is defined by bearing and distance

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Flyover waypoint. A waypoint at which a turn is initiated in order to join the next segment of a route or procedure.'

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'D-ATIS' service?
questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Data link-automatic terminal information service (D-ATIS). The provision of ATIS via data link.' (Definitions section)

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Position symbol'?

options.A: The visual indication of the position of an aircraft on a situation display obtained after automatic processing of positional data
options.E: The map symbol for a waypoint

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Position symbol. The visual indication in symbolic form, on a situation display, of the position of an aircraft, aerodrome vehicle or other object, obtained after automatic processing of positional data derived from any source.' (Definitions section)

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Performance-based communication (PBC)'?

options.B: Communication based on performance specifications applied to the provision of air traffic services
options.E: Communication that is independent of aircraft equipment

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Performance-based communication (PBC). Communication based on performance specifications applied to the provision of air traffic services.' (Definitions section)

questions.question: What is the definition of 'Ground effect' for helicopters?

options.A: The condition of improved performance due to the interference of the surface with the airflow pattern of the rotor system when operating near the ground
options.B: The reduction in lift near the ground
options.C: The effect of ground proximity on navigation
options.D: The interference of terrain with radio signals
options.F: The effect of temperature on rotor efficiency

questions.explanation: The document defines: 'Ground effect. A condition of improved performance (lift) due to the interference of the surface with the airflow pattern of the rotor system when a helicopter or other VTOL aircraft is operating near the ground.' (Definitions section)

questions.question: True or False: 'Air traffic control clearance constitutes authority to violate any applicable regulations for promoting the safety of flight operations.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'ATC clearances do not constitute authority to violate any applicable regulations for promoting the safety of flight operations or for any other purpose; neither do clearances relieve a pilot-in-command of any responsibility whatsoever in connection with a possible violation of applicable rules and regulations.'

questions.question: True or False: 'Flight crew should read back to the air traffic controller all clearances and instructions, including conditional clearances.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The flight crew 'shall' read back to the air traffic controller safety-related parts of ATC clearances and instructions which are transmitted by voice. The following items shall always be read back: a) ATC route clearances; b) clearances and instructions to enter, land on, take off from, hold short of, cross, taxi and backtrack on any runway; and c) runway-in-use, altimeter settings, SSR codes, level instructions, heading and speed instructions and ... transition levels. ... Other clearances or instructions, including conditional clearances, shall be read back or acknowledged in a manner to clearly indicate that they have been understood and will be complied with.' Not all clearances must be read back verbatim; conditional clearances require acknowledgment but not necessarily full readback in the same way. However, the statement says 'all clearances and instructions' which is too broad because safety-related parts are required for readback, but some items like conditional clearances require acknowledgment, not necessarily readback word-for-word. The document says 'shall be read back or acknowledged' - so acknowledgment suffices. Therefore the statement as written is false because it says 'read back' for all, but conditional clearances can be acknowledged. Also, routine non-safety parts may not need readback. To be precise, the statement is false.

questions.question: True or False: 'Speed control instructions shall not be applied to aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Speed control shall not be applied to aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern.'

questions.sub_chapter: 4.8 Change from IFR to VFR flight
questions.question: True or False: 'Change from IFR flight to VFR flight can be initiated by ATC if weather conditions permit.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Change from instrument flight rules (IFR) flight to visual flight rules (VFR) flight is only acceptable when a message initiated by the pilot-in-command ... is received by an air traffic services unit. No invitation to change from IFR flight to VFR flight is to be made either directly or by inference.'

questions.question: True or False: 'Wake turbulence separation minima based on GROUPS use 'SUPER', 'HEAVY', 'MEDIUM', 'LIGHT' categories.'
questions.explanation: The document describes two separate systems: wake turbulence categories (SUPER, HEAVY, MEDIUM, LIGHT) based on mass, and wake turbulence groups (A through G) based on mass and wing span. The statement incorrectly combines them. Groups are not the same as categories.

questions.question: True or False: 'For flights en route, the vertical position of aircraft shall be expressed in terms of altitudes at or above the lowest usable flight level and flight levels below that level.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'For flights en route, the vertical position of aircraft shall be expressed in terms of: a) flight levels at or above the lowest usable flight level; and b) altitudes below the lowest usable flight level;' The statement reverses the assignment.

questions.question: Who provides area control service?

options.B: Area control centre (ACC) or, in limited areas, the unit providing approach control service when no ACC is established

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Area control service shall be provided: a) by an area control centre (ACC); or b) by the unit providing approach control service in a control zone or in a control area of limited extent which is designated primarily for the provision of approach control service, when no ACC is established.'

questions.question: When should a flight plan be amended or a new flight plan submitted in case of delay?

options.B: Delay of 30 minutes in excess of estimated off-block time for a controlled flight or one hour for an uncontrolled flight
options.C: Delay of 1 hour for any flight
options.F: Delay of 45 minutes for controlled flights

questions.explanation: The document states: 'In the event of a delay of 30 minutes in excess of the estimated off-block time for a controlled flight or a delay of one hour for an uncontrolled flight for which a flight plan has been submitted, the flight plan should be amended or a new flight plan submitted and the old flight plan cancelled, whichever is applicable.'

questions.question: What are the contents of a standard clearance for a departing aircraft?

options.A: Aircraft identification, clearance limit (normally destination), SID designator if applicable, cleared level, allocated SSR code, and any other necessary instructions
options.B: Only aircraft identification and cleared level
options.C: Aircraft type, route, speed, and altitude
options.D: Call sign, take-off time, and destination
options.E: Aircraft identification, runway, and weather information
options.F: Flight rules, type of flight, and endurance

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Standard clearances for departing aircraft shall contain the following items: a) aircraft identification; b) clearance limit, normally destination aerodrome; c) designator of the assigned SID, if applicable; d) cleared level; e) allocated SSR code; f) any other necessary instructions or information not contained in the SID description, e.g. instructions relating to change of frequency.'

questions.question: When shall a departing aircraft be advised of the QNH altimeter setting?

options.A: Only when requested by the pilot
options.B: Prior to taxiing for take-off, and in descent clearances when first cleared to an altitude below the transition level
options.E: Only when transitioning from IFR to VFR
options.F: Only when the transition level changes

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Prior to taxiing for take-off, aircraft shall be advised of ... c) the QNH altimeter setting ...' and 'A QNH altimeter setting shall be included in the descent clearance when first cleared to an altitude below the transition level, in approach clearances or clearances to enter the traffic circuit, and in taxi clearances for departing aircraft, except when it is known that the aircraft has already received the information.'

questions.question: What are the contents of a voice position report?

options.A: Aircraft identification, position, time, flight level or altitude, next position and time over, and ensuing significant point
options.C: Aircraft identification, position, time, and speed
options.E: Aircraft identification, flight level, and endurance
options.F: Position, next position, and estimated time of arrival

questions.explanation: The document states: 'The position reports required by 4.11.1.1 and 4.11.1.2 shall contain the following elements of information, except that elements d), e) and f) may be omitted ... when so prescribed: a) aircraft identification; b) position; c) time; d) flight level or altitude...; e) next position and time over; and f) ensuing significant point.'

questions.question: What is the maximum period before departure that a flight plan may be submitted?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Flight plans shall not be submitted more than 120 hours before the estimated off-block time of a flight.'

questions.question: What action should be taken by the first ATS unit receiving a flight plan?

options.A: Only forward it to the next unit
options.B: Check it for compliance with format and data conventions, completeness and accuracy, take action to make it acceptable, and indicate acceptance to the originator
options.E: Forward it to the destination aerodrome only

questions.explanation: The document states: 'The first ATS unit receiving a flight plan, or change thereto, shall: a) check it for compliance with the format and data conventions; b) check it for completeness and, to the extent possible, for accuracy; c) take action, if necessary, to make it acceptable to the air traffic services; and d) indicate acceptance of the flight plan or change thereto, to the originator.'

questions.question: What is the meaning of the phrase 'cleared flight planned route'?

options.A: The route is identical to that filed in the flight plan
options.B: The route is a standard departure route
options.C: The route is a direct routing
options.D: The route is the most direct routing
options.E: The route is a published arrival route
options.F: The route is a vectoring route

questions.explanation: The document states: 'The phrase "cleared flight planned route" may be used to describe any route or portion thereof, provided the route or portion thereof is identical to that filed in the flight plan and sufficient routing details are given to definitely establish the aircraft on its route.'

questions.question: When may speed control not be applied?

options.B: To aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern
options.E: To aircraft at or above FL 250

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Speed control shall not be applied to aircraft entering or established in a holding pattern.'

questions.question: What are the wake turbulence categories based on maximum certificated take-off mass?

options.F: Both A and E are correct

questions.explanation: The document states: 'SUPER (J), HEAVY (H), MEDIUM (M), LIGHT (L).' So both the names and the letters are correct. The options list both A (names) and E (letters). Since F says both A and E are correct, that is the best answer.

questions.question: What is the transition level?

options.A: The altitude at which pilots change from QFE to QNH
options.B: The lowest flight level available for use above the transition altitude
options.C: The level at which aircraft change from IFR to VFR
options.D: The altitude below which all aircraft must use QNH
options.E: The flight level at which vertical separation changes from 1000 ft to 2000 ft
options.F: The level at which aircraft must report position

questions.explanation: The document states: 'The transition level shall be the lowest flight level available for use above the transition altitude established for the aerodrome(s) concerned.'

questions.question: When shall the initial call to an ATC unit after a change of air-ground voice communication channel contain the word 'super' or 'heavy'?

options.B: Only for aircraft in the SUPER and HEAVY wake turbulence categories
options.D: Only for aircraft with more than 4 engines
options.E: Only for aircraft at or above FL 290

questions.explanation: The document states: 'When so prescribed by the appropriate ATS authority, the initial call to an ATC unit after a change of air-ground voice communication channel shall contain the following elements: ... b) call sign and, for aircraft in the SUPER and HEAVY wake turbulence categories, the word 'super' or 'heavy' respectively;'

questions.question: True or False: 'An ATC unit may request an adjacent ATC unit to clear aircraft to a specified point during a specified period.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'An ATC unit may request an adjacent ATC unit to clear aircraft to a specified point during a specified period.' (Chapter 4, 4.5.6.1.1)

questions.question: True or False: 'An aircraft may be cleared for cruise climb only when specifically requested by the pilot and if traffic conditions permit.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'When so requested by the flight crew, an aircraft shall be cleared for cruise climb whenever traffic conditions and coordination procedures permit.' (Chapter 4, 4.5.6.1.3)

questions.question: What is the minimum distance from a runway threshold at which speed control should not be applied to an aircraft on final approach?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Speed control should not be applied to aircraft after passing a point 7 km (4 NM) from the threshold on final approach.'

questions.question: What is the minimum speed reduction for turbojet aircraft during initial descent from cruising level that requires flight crew concurrence?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Speed reductions to less than 460 km/h (250 kt) IAS for turbojet aircraft during initial descent from cruising level should be applied only with the concurrence of the flight crew.'

questions.question: What is the maximum minor speed adjustment for aircraft on intermediate and final approach?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Only minor speed adjustments not exceeding plus/minus 40 km/h (20 kt) IAS should be used for aircraft on intermediate and final approach.'

questions.question: What is the correct altimeter setting procedure for an aircraft climbing through the transition layer?

options.A: Express vertical position in terms of altitudes
options.B: Express vertical position in terms of flight levels
options.C: Express vertical position in terms of altitudes when climbing and flight levels when descending
options.D: Express vertical position in terms of flight levels when climbing and altitudes when descending
options.E: Express vertical position in QFE only
options.F: Express vertical position in height above aerodrome

questions.explanation: The document states: 'While passing through the transition layer, vertical position shall be expressed in terms of flight levels when climbing and in terms of altitudes when descending.' (Chapter 4, 4.10.1.1)

questions.question: When shall a QFE altimeter setting be provided for a precision approach runway?

options.B: On a regular basis in accordance with local arrangements or on request
options.C: Only when the threshold is 2 m or more below the aerodrome elevation
options.D: Always, as the QFE for the relevant runway threshold

questions.explanation: The document states: 'A QFE altimeter setting shall be provided to aircraft on request or on a regular basis in accordance with local arrangements; it shall be the QFE for the aerodrome elevation except for: a) non-precision approach runways, if the threshold is 2m (7 ft) or more below the aerodrome elevation; and b) precision approach runways; in which cases the QFE for the relevant runway threshold shall be provided.' (Chapter 4, 4.10.4.6)

questions.question: What is the maximum time after the expected time of a position report that a controller should take action to obtain the report if it is likely to affect control of other aircraft?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'If a position report is not received at the expected time, subsequent control shall not be based on the assumption that the estimated time is accurate. Immediate action shall be taken to obtain the report if it is likely to have any bearing on the control of other aircraft.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.1.5)

questions.question: What data block is mandatory in all ADS-C reports?

options.C: Basic ADS-C (latitude, longitude, altitude, time, figure of merit)

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Note.- The basic ADS-C block is mandatory and is included in all ADS-C reports.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.5.1 b) Note)

questions.question: What action should be taken by an ATC unit when receiving special air-reports by voice concerning braking action that is not as good as reported?

options.A: Forward them without delay to the associated meteorological watch office
options.B: Forward them without delay to the appropriate aerodrome operator
options.C: Forward them to the world area forecast centres
options.D: Record them and take no further action
options.F: Forward them to the next ATC unit

questions.explanation: The document states: 'When receiving special air-reports by voice communications concerning braking action encountered that is not as good as reported, air traffic service units shall forward them without delay to the appropriate aerodrome operator.' (Chapter 4, 4.12.7)

questions.question: What is the minimum period for which paper flight progress strips (FPS) must be retained for investigative purposes?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Paper FPS shall be retained for a period of at least 30 days. Electronic flight progress and coordination data shall be recorded and retained for at least the same period of time.' (Chapter 4, 4.13.4)

questions.question: What is the initial action by an aircraft for data link initiation when entering airspace where data link applications are used by the ATS unit?

options.A: Wait for the ATS unit to initiate
options.B: Data link communications shall be initiated by the aircraft, either automatically or by the pilot, or by the ATS unit on address forwarding
options.C: Only the ATS unit can initiate
options.D: Only voice communications are used initially
options.E: The pilot must request via voice first

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Before entering an airspace where data link applications are used by the ATS unit, data link communications shall be initiated between the aircraft and the ATS unit in order to register the aircraft and, when necessary, allow the start of a data link application. This shall be initiated by the aircraft, either automatically or by the pilot, or by the ATS unit on address forwarding.' (Chapter 4, 4.15.1.1)

questions.question: What is the minimum vertical separation between a helicopter and light aircraft when the helicopter is hovering or air taxiing?

options.C: No specific minimum, but helicopters should be kept well clear of light aircraft
options.F: Same as fixed-wing wake turbulence minima

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Helicopters should be kept well clear of light aircraft when hovering or while air taxiing.' (Chapter 4, 4.9.1.3) No specific numerical separation is given; it is a qualitative requirement.

questions.question: What is the required content of a position report for an aircraft on a route not defined by designated significant points?

options.A: Report over each compulsory reporting point
options.B: Report as soon as possible after the first half hour of flight and at hourly intervals thereafter

questions.explanation: The document states: 'On routes not defined by designated significant points, position reports shall be made by the aircraft as soon as possible after the first half hour of flight and at hourly intervals thereafter, except as provided in 4.11.1.3.' (Chapter 4, 4.11.1.2)

questions.question: True or False: 'The vertical separation minimum (VSM) is always 1,000 ft regardless of flight level.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The vertical separation minimum (VSM) shall be: a) a nominal 300 m (1 000 ft) below FL 290 and a nominal 600 m (2 000 ft) at or above this level, except as provided for in b) below; and b) within designated airspace, subject to a regional air navigation agreement: a nominal 300 m (1 000 ft) below FL 410 or a higher level where so prescribed for use under specified conditions, and a nominal 600 m (2 000 ft) at or above this level.'

questions.question: True or False: 'When an aircraft has been cleared into a control area at a cruising level below the established minimum for a subsequent portion of the route, the ATC unit responsible should issue a revised clearance even if the pilot has not requested it.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'When an aircraft has been cleared into a control area at a cruising level which is below the established minimum cruising level for a subsequent portion of the route, the ATC unit responsible for the area should issue a revised clearance to the aircraft even though the pilot has not requested the necessary cruising level change.'

questions.question: True or False: 'Lateral separation using VOR requires aircraft to be established on radials diverging by at least 15 degrees and at least one aircraft at a distance of 15 NM or more from the facility.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'VOR: both aircraft are established on radials diverging by at least 15 degrees and at least one aircraft is at a distance of 28 km (15 NM) or more from the facility.'

questions.question: True or False: 'For aircraft on the same track, a 5-minute longitudinal separation minimum may be applied only if the preceding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 20 kt or more faster than the succeeding aircraft.'
questions.explanation: The document states: '5 minutes in the following cases, provided that in each case the preceding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 37 km/h (20 kt) or more faster than the succeeding aircraft:'

questions.question: True or False: 'When applying DME-based longitudinal separation, direct controller-pilot VHF voice communication is required.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Direct controller-pilot VHF voice communication shall be maintained while such separation is used.'

questions.question: What is the minimum vertical separation in designated RVSM airspace below FL 410?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'within designated airspace, subject to a regional air navigation agreement: a nominal 300 m (1 000 ft) below FL 410 or a higher level where so prescribed for use under specified conditions.'

questions.question: Which lateral separation minimum using VOR requires radials diverging by at least 15 degrees?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'VOR: both aircraft are established on radials diverging by at least 15 degrees and at least one aircraft is at a distance of 28 km (15 NM) or more from the facility.'

questions.question: What is the longitudinal separation minimum for aircraft on the same track at the same level when using 15 minutes?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aircraft flying on the same track: a) 15 minutes' (Figure 5-10).

questions.question: When can a 5-minute longitudinal separation be applied between aircraft on the same track at the same level?

options.B: Only when the preceding aircraft is 20 kt or more faster
options.C: Only when both aircraft are departing from the same aerodrome
options.F: Only when both aircraft are IFR

questions.explanation: The document states: '5 minutes in the following cases, provided that in each case the preceding aircraft is maintaining a true airspeed of 37 km/h (20 kt) or more faster than the succeeding aircraft:'

questions.question: What is the minimum distance for DME-based longitudinal separation between aircraft on the same track at the same level?
questions.explanation: The document states: '37 km (20 NM)' as the basic DME-based separation, and also allows 19 km (10 NM) under certain conditions. The standard minimum is 20 NM.

questions.question: What is the definition of 'same track' for longitudinal separation purposes?

options.A: Tracks with angular difference less than 45 degrees or more than 315 degrees and protected airspaces overlap
options.D: Tracks that intersect at any angle
options.E: Tracks that diverge by more than 45 degrees

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Same track (see Figure 5-7): same direction tracks and intersecting tracks or portions thereof, the angular difference of which is less than 45 degrees or more than 315 degrees, and whose protected airspaces overlap.'

questions.question: What is the RNAV distance-based separation minimum with Mach number technique?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'A 150 km (80 NM) RNAV distance-based separation minimum with Mach number technique may be used on same-direction tracks in lieu of a 10-minute longitudinal separation minimum with Mach number technique.'

questions.question: What is the maximum altitude difference for an ITP climb or descent?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the altitude difference between the ITP aircraft and any reference aircraft shall be 600 m (2 000 ft) or less.'

questions.question: What action should a controller take if an expected ADS-C periodic report is not received within 3 minutes of the time it should have been sent?

options.B: Take action to obtain the report as quickly as possible, normally by ADS-C or CPDLC

questions.explanation: The document states: 'When an ADS-C periodic or waypoint change event report is not received within 3 minutes of the time it should have been sent, the report is considered overdue and the controller shall take action to obtain the report as quickly as possible, normally by ADS-C or CPDLC.'

questions.question: What is the minimum lateral separation between aircraft on parallel tracks when SLOP is not authorized and the required performance is RNAV 10 (RNP 10)?
questions.explanation: Table 5-2 shows that for RNAV 10 (RNP 10) with SLOP not authorized, the minimum spacing between tracks is 93 km (50 NM).

questions.question: What is the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval allowed for a 37 km (20 NM) longitudinal separation minimum under performance-based separation?
questions.explanation: Table in 5.4.2.9.2 shows for 37 km (20 NM) separation, the maximum ADS-C periodic reporting interval is 192 seconds (3.2 minutes).

questions.question: What is the minimum RNAV distance-based separation for aircraft on reciprocal tracks?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aircraft on reciprocal tracks. Aircraft utilizing RNAV may be cleared to climb or descend to or through the levels occupied by other aircraft utilizing RNAV provided it has been positively established by simultaneous RNAV distance readings to or from the same 'on-track' common point that the aircraft have passed each other and are at least 150 km (80 NM) apart.'
questions.image_description: Figure 5-28

questions.question: What is the maximum altitude for applying 5-minute longitudinal separation while vertical separation does not exist for aircraft on the same track?
questions.explanation: The document does not specify an altitude limit for the 5-minute separation while vertical separation does not exist. It only provides conditions: the level change is commenced within 10 minutes of the second aircraft reporting over a common point derived from ground-based aids or GNSS.

questions.question: What is the minimum rate of climb or descent for an ITP manoeuvre?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the climb or descent shall be conducted at a rate of not less than 1.5 m/s (300 ft/min), or any higher rate when specified by the controller.'

questions.question: What is the minimum distance from a common point for applying lateral separation between aircraft using GNSS on intersecting tracks at FL 200?
questions.explanation: Table 5-1 shows for FL200–FL600 and angular difference 15-135 degrees, the distance from a common point is 43 km (23 NM) for GNSS/GNSS or VOR/GNSS.

questions.question: What is the maximum distance from a common point for lateral separation using VOR at FL 100?
questions.explanation: Table 5-1 shows for FL010–FL190, the distance from a common point is 27.8 km (15 NM) for VOR/GNSS separation.

questions.question: What is the maximum allowable closing ground speed between ITP aircraft and reference aircraft when the ITP distance is not less than 37 km (20 NM)?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the ITP distance between the ITP aircraft and the reference aircraft shall be: 1) not less than 28 km (15 NM) with a maximum closing ground speed of 37 km/h (20 kt); or 2) not less than 37 km (20 NM) with a maximum closing ground speed of 56 km/h (30 kt).'

questions.question: What is the minimum distance for GNSS-based lateral separation for an angular difference of 15 degrees at FL 100?
questions.explanation: Table 5-1 shows for FL010–FL190, the distance from a common point is 27.8 km (15 NM) for GNSS-based separation.

questions.question: What is the longitudinal separation minimum for aircraft climbing and on crossing tracks when vertical separation does not exist, using the 15-minute rule?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aircraft on crossing tracks: a) 15 minutes while vertical separation does not exist (see Figures 5-19A and 5-19B).'
questions.image_description: Figures 5-19A and 5-19B

questions.question: What is the minimum distance from a common point for lateral separation using NDB?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'NDB: both aircraft are established on tracks to or from the NDB which are diverging by at least 30 degrees and at least one aircraft is at a distance of 28 km (15 NM) or more from the facility.'

questions.question: What is the minimum distance from a common point for lateral separation using GNSS at FL 300?
questions.explanation: Table 5-1 shows for FL200–FL600, the distance from a common point is 43 km (23 NM) for GNSS/GNSS or VOR/GNSS.

questions.question: What is the maximum distance from a common point for lateral separation using GNSS at FL 300?
questions.explanation: Same as above: Table 5-1 shows 43 km (23 NM) for FL200–FL600.

questions.question: When may a reduced separation minimum be implemented according to Chapter 5?

options.A: Whenever the controller deems it safe
options.B: Provided an appropriate safety risk assessment has shown an acceptable level of safety will be maintained, and after prior consultation with users

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Provided an appropriate safety risk assessment has shown that an acceptable level of safety will be maintained, and after prior consultation with users, the separation minima detailed in 5.4.1 and 5.4.2 may be reduced in the following circumstances:' (Chapter 5, 5.11.1)

questions.question: What is the maximum time allowed for a controller to intervene and resolve a potential conflict using the normal means of communication when applying performance-based longitudinal separation minima?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The communication system provided to enable the application of the separation minima in 5.4.2.9.2 shall allow a controller, within 4 minutes, to intervene and resolve a potential conflict by contacting an aircraft using the normal means of communication.' (Chapter 5, 5.4.2.9.6)

questions.question: What is the minimum horizontal separation between aircraft on parallel tracks when using RNP 4 and SLOP up to 2 NM is authorized?
questions.explanation: Table 5-2 shows for RNP 4 with SLOP up to 2 NM authorized, the minimum spacing between tracks is 42.6 km (23 NM).

questions.question: What is the minimum wake turbulence separation time for a LIGHT aircraft landing behind a HEAVY aircraft?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'LIGHT aircraft landing behind a HEAVY or MEDIUM aircraft — 3 minutes.' (Chapter 5, 5.8.2.1 e))

questions.question: True or False: 'For independent parallel approaches, a single monitoring controller may monitor no more than one runway, and that is mandatory.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the approaches are monitored by: 1) a separate monitoring controller for each runway; or 2) a single monitoring controller for no more than two runways, if determined by a safety risk assessment and approved by the appropriate ATS authority.' Therefore, a single controller may monitor up to two runways under certain conditions.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the term 'Essential local traffic'?

options.A: Any aircraft, vehicle or personnel on or near the runway or in the take-off/climb-out or final approach area that may constitute a collision hazard to a departing or arriving aircraft
options.B: All IFR flights within 5 NM of the aerodrome
options.D: Only vehicles on the manoeuvring area

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Essential local traffic in this context consists of any aircraft, vehicle or personnel on or near the runway to be used, or traffic in the take-off and climb-out area or the final approach area, which may constitute a collision hazard to a departing or arriving aircraft.' (Chapter 6, 6.2.1 Note 1)

questions.question: What is the minimum horizontal separation for dependent parallel approaches between successive aircraft on adjacent final approach courses when runway centre lines are more than 2529 m apart?
questions.explanation: The document states: '3.7 km (2.0 NM) between successive aircraft on adjacent final approach courses or tracks more than 2529 m (8 300 ft) apart (Figure 6-3).'
questions.image_description: Figure showing diagonal separation for distance between centre lines greater than 2529 m

questions.question: For independent parallel approaches, what is the required minimum accuracy for SSR when runway centre line spacing is less than 1310 m but not less than 1035 m?
questions.explanation: Table 6-1 states: 'Less than 1 310 m (4 300 ft) but not less than 1 035 m (3 400 ft) a) a minimum accuracy for an ATS surveillance system as follows: 1) for SSR, an azimuth accuracy of 0.06 degrees (one sigma);'

questions.question: What is the minimum distance for independent parallel departures from runways spaced 760 m apart?

options.A: The runway centre lines must be spaced by at least 760 m
options.C: Spacing must be at least 1525 m
options.D: Spacing must be at least 915 m
options.E: Spacing must be at least 1035 m
options.F: Spacing must be at least 300 m

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Independent IFR departures may be conducted from parallel runways provided: a) the runway centre lines are spaced by a minimum distance of 760 m (2 500 ft)'

questions.question: What is the minimum angle of divergence for nominal departure tracks for independent parallel departures when the turn commences no more than 2 NM from the departure end of the runway?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the nominal departure tracks diverge by at least: 1) 15 degrees immediately after take-off; or 2) 10 degrees, where: i) both aircraft are flying an RNAV or RNP instrument departure; and ii) the turn commences no more than 3.7 km (2.0 NM) from the departure end of the runway.'

questions.question: What is the maximum permissible variation for reporting a revised expected approach time to an aircraft?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'A revised expected approach time shall be transmitted to the aircraft without delay whenever it differs from that previously transmitted by 5 minutes or more, or such lesser period of time as has been established by the appropriate ATS authority or agreed between the ATS units concerned.' (Chapter 6, 6.5.7.1)

questions.question: What is the minimum angle of divergence for missed approach tracks for independent parallel approaches?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'the nominal tracks of the missed approach procedures diverge by at least 30 degrees;' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.2.1 c))

questions.question: When are independent parallel approaches to closely spaced parallel runways (less than 1525 m) suspended?

options.B: Under certain meteorological conditions prescribed by the ATS authority, such as wind shear, turbulence, crosswind, and thunderstorms
options.E: When military operations are in progress

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Independent parallel approaches to parallel runways spaced by less than 1525 m between their centre lines shall be suspended under certain meteorological conditions, as prescribed by the appropriate ATS authority, including wind shear, turbulence, downdrafts, crosswind and significant meteorological conditions such as thunderstorms, which might otherwise increase deviations from the final approach course or track to the extent that safety may be impaired.' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.3)

questions.question: What is the maximum height above the runway threshold elevation below which a heading instruction shall not be issued to an aircraft during a break-out procedure for independent parallel approaches where PAOAS criteria are applied?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Where parallel approach obstacle assessment surfaces (PAOAS) criteria are applied for the obstacle assessment, the monitoring controller shall not issue the heading instruction to the aircraft below 120 m (400 ft) above the runway threshold elevation, and the heading instruction shall not exceed 45 degrees track difference with the final approach course or track.' (Chapter 6, 6.7.3.2.10)

questions.question: True or False: 'Aerodrome control towers are responsible for alerting the rescue and fire fighting services whenever an aircraft accident has occurred on or in the vicinity of the aerodrome.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aerodrome control towers are responsible for alerting the rescue and fire fighting services whenever: a) an aircraft accident has occurred on or in the vicinity of the aerodrome;'

questions.question: True or False: 'Visual observation by an aerodrome controller can be achieved through direct out-of-the-window observation or through indirect observation utilizing a visual surveillance system approved for the purpose.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Visual observation shall be achieved through direct out-of-the-window observation, or through indirect observation utilizing a visual surveillance system which is specifically approved for the purpose by the appropriate ATS authority.'

questions.question: True or False: 'A pilot in doubt as to the position of the aircraft with respect to the manoeuvring area must immediately stop the aircraft and notify ATC, but if on a runway, they may attempt to vacate to a suitable taxiway before stopping.'
questions.explanation: The document states: '7.4.1.5.1 Except as provided for in 7.4.1.5.2, a pilot in doubt as to the position ... shall immediately: a) stop the aircraft; and b) simultaneously notify the appropriate ATS unit... 7.4.1.5.2 In those situations where a pilot is in doubt ... but recognizes that the aircraft is on a runway, the pilot shall immediately: a) notify ... b) if able to locate a nearby suitable taxiway, vacate the runway as expeditiously as possible, unless otherwise instructed ... and then, c) stop the aircraft.'

questions.question: True or False: 'The expression TAKE-OFF may be used in radiotelephony only when an aircraft is cleared for take-off or when cancelling a take-off clearance.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The expression TAKE-OFF shall only be used in radiotelephony when an aircraft is cleared for take-off or when cancelling a take-off clearance.'

questions.question: True or False: 'Reduced runway separation minima may be applied between a departing aircraft and a preceding landing aircraft.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Reduced runway separation minima shall not apply between a departing aircraft and a preceding landing aircraft.'

questions.question: When should a departing aircraft not be permitted to line up and hold on the approach end of a runway?

options.A: Whenever another aircraft is on final approach
options.B: Whenever another aircraft is effecting a landing, until the landing aircraft has passed the point of intended holding
options.D: Whenever visibility is less than 5 km
options.F: Whenever the preceding aircraft is heavy

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Aircraft shall not be permitted to line up and hold on the approach end of a runway-in-use whenever another aircraft is effecting a landing, until the landing aircraft has passed the point of intended holding.'

questions.question: What light signal from aerodrome control means 'Permission to cross landing area or to move onto taxiway'?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Light signal from aerodrome control Meaning Green flashes Permission to cross landing area or to move onto taxiway'

questions.question: What is the function of a stop bar?

options.A: To indicate the runway holding position
options.B: To be switched on to indicate that all traffic shall stop and switched off to indicate that traffic may proceed
options.C: To mark the edge of a taxiway
options.D: To indicate the threshold of a runway
options.E: To guide aircraft during low visibility

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Stop bars shall be switched on to indicate that all traffic shall stop and switched off to indicate that traffic may proceed.'

questions.question: What is the minimum RVR value for special VFR flights to be authorized?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'When the ground visibility is not less than 1500 m, special VFR flights may be authorized...' (Chapter 7, 7.15.1.3)

questions.question: True or False: 'In low visibility operations, the aerodrome control tower shall, prior to the period of application, establish a record of vehicles and persons currently on the manoeuvring area and maintain it during the application.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The aerodrome control tower shall, prior to a period of application of low visibility procedures, establish a record of vehicles and persons currently on the manoeuvring area and maintain this record during the period of application of these procedures to assist in assuring the safety of operations on that area.'

questions.question: What is the maximum descent rate allowed for a helicopter hovering in ground effect or air taxiing?

options.A: Not specified, but caution is advised due to downwash

questions.explanation: The document does not specify a numeric maximum descent rate; it notes that helicopters generate downwash producing high velocity outwash vortices to a distance approximately three times the rotor diameter, and that air-taxiing consumes fuel at a high burn rate. No specific descent rate limit is given.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the light signal 'White flashes' from an aerodrome control tower?

options.C: Move off the landing area or taxiway and watch out for aircraft
options.D: Vacate manoeuvring area in accordance with local instructions

questions.explanation: The document states: 'White flashes Vacate manoeuvring area in accordance with local instructions'

questions.question: What is the minimum visibility for applying reduced runway separation minima?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Reduced runway separation minima shall be subject to the following conditions: ... b) visibility shall be at least 5 km and ceiling shall not be lower than 300 m (1 000 ft).'

questions.question: What is the maximum wind speed tailwind component for applying reduced runway separation minima?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Reduced runway separation minima shall be subject to the following conditions: ... c) tailwind component shall not exceed 5 kt.'

questions.question: What is the maximum crosswind component, including gusts, for which noise abatement shall not be a determining factor in runway nomination?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Noise abatement shall not be a determining factor in runway nomination under the following circumstances: ... e) when the crosswind component, including gusts, exceeds 28 km/h (15 kt), or the tailwind component, including gusts, exceeds 9 km/h (5 kt).' (Chapter 7, 7.2.6 e))

questions.question: What is the correct procedure for a pilot who is in doubt as to the position of the aircraft with respect to the manoeuvring area and recognizes that the aircraft is on a runway?

options.B: Notify ATC, vacate the runway if able to a suitable taxiway, then stop
options.C: Continue taxiing until a safe area is reached
options.E: Turn on all lights and await instructions
options.F: Backtrack on the runway to the last known position

questions.explanation: The document states: 'In those situations where a pilot is in doubt as to the position of the aircraft with respect to the manoeuvring area, but recognizes that the aircraft is on a runway, the pilot shall immediately: a) notify the appropriate ATS unit of the circumstances (including the last known position); b) if able to locate a nearby suitable taxiway, vacate the runway as expeditiously as possible, unless otherwise instructed by the ATS unit; and then, c) stop the aircraft.' (Chapter 7, 7.4.1.5.2)

questions.question: What is the minimum distance that reduced runway separation minima for a Category 1 aircraft landing behind a Category 1 or 2 aircraft requires the preceding aircraft to have passed from the threshold?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'a succeeding landing Category 1 aircraft may cross the runway threshold when the preceding aircraft is a Category 1 or 2 aircraft which either: i) has landed and has passed a point at least 600 m from the threshold of the runway, is in motion and will vacate the runway without backtracking; or ii) is airborne and has passed a point at least 600 m from the threshold of the runway.' (Chapter 7, 7.11.7 a) 1))

questions.question: What is the requirement for vehicle communication at controlled aerodromes?

options.A: All vehicles on the manoeuvring area must be capable of two-way radiocommunication with the tower, except when only occasionally used and accompanied by a vehicle with communication or employed under a pre-arranged plan
options.D: Only vehicles with flashing lights need communication
options.E: Only vehicles towing aircraft need communication
options.F: Vehicles are not permitted on the manoeuvring area

questions.explanation: The document states: 'At controlled aerodromes all vehicles employed on the manoeuvring area shall be capable of maintaining two-way radiocommunication with the aerodrome control tower, except when the vehicle is only occasionally used on the manoeuvring area and is: a) accompanied by a vehicle with the required communications capability; or b) employed in accordance with a pre-arranged plan established with the aerodrome control tower.' (Chapter 7, 7.6.3.2.3.1)

questions.question: What is the primary responsibility of the aerodrome control tower regarding runway incursions?

options.A: To report any occurrence involving an obstruction on the runway or a runway incursion
options.E: To close the runway after an incursion

questions.explanation: The document states: 'Pilots and air traffic controllers shall report any occurrence involving an obstruction on the runway or a runway incursion.' (Chapter 7, 7.4.1.4.2)

questions.question: True or False: 'ADS-B may be used alone for separation between aircraft provided identification is established and maintained, data integrity is adequate, there is no requirement for detection of non-ADS-B aircraft, and no requirement for independent position determination.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'ADS-B may be used alone, including in the provision of separation between aircraft, provided: a) identification of ADS-B-equipped aircraft is established and maintained; b) the data integrity measure in the ADS-B message is adequate to support the separation minimum; c) there is no requirement for detection of aircraft not transmitting ADS-B; and d) there is no requirement for determination of aircraft position independent of the position-determining elements of the aircraft navigation system.'

questions.question: True or False: 'The tolerance value for pressure-altitude-derived level information in RVSM airspace is ±300 ft.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'The tolerance value used to determine that pressure-altitude-derived level information displayed to the controller is accurate shall be ±60 m (±200 ft) in RVSM airspace. In other airspace, it shall be ±90 m (±300 ft).' Therefore in RVSM it is ±200 ft, not ±300 ft.

questions.question: True or False: 'When vectoring an IFR flight, the controller must ensure that the prescribed obstacle clearance will exist at all times.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'When vectoring an IFR flight and when giving an IFR flight a direct routing which takes the aircraft off an ATS route, the controller shall issue clearances such that the prescribed obstacle clearance will exist at all times until the aircraft reaches the point where the pilot will resume own navigation.'

questions.question: True or False: 'The minimum horizontal separation based on radar shall be 5 NM, but may be reduced to 3 NM if radar capabilities permit.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Unless otherwise prescribed ... the horizontal separation minimum based on radar and/or ADS-B and/or MLAT systems shall be 9.3 km (5.0 NM).' and 'The separation minimum ... may, if so prescribed ... be reduced, but not below: a) 5.6 km (3.0 NM) when radar and/or ADS-B and/or MLAT systems' capabilities at a given location so permit;'

questions.question: True or False: 'When a general ADS-B emergency alert is observed, the controller must attempt to establish communication with the aircraft or request a specified manoeuvre if no response.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Whenever a general ADS-B emergency alert is observed ... the controller shall take the following action: a) attempt to establish communication with the aircraft to verify the nature of the emergency; or b) if no response is received from the aircraft, the controller shall attempt to ascertain if the aircraft is able to receive transmissions from the air traffic control unit by requesting it to execute a specified manoeuvre which can be observed on the situation display.'

questions.question: What are the reserved SSR codes internationally?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Codes 7700, 7600 and 7500 shall be reserved internationally for use by pilots encountering a state of emergency, radiocommunication failure or unlawful interference, respectively.'

questions.question: What is the horizontal separation minimum based on radar?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'Unless otherwise prescribed ... the horizontal separation minimum based on radar and/or ADS-B and/or MLAT systems shall be 9.3 km (5.0 NM).'

questions.question: What is the distance-based wake turbulence separation minimum between a HEAVY preceding and a LIGHT succeeding aircraft?
questions.explanation: The document states: 'HEAVY ... LIGHT 11.1 km (6.0 NM)'

questions.question: True or False: 'In the event of a complete ATS surveillance system failure where air-ground communications remain, the controller shall plot the positions of all identified aircraft and establish procedural separation.'
questions.explanation: The document states: 'In the event of complete failure of the ATS surveillance system where air-ground communications remain, the controller shall plot the positions of all aircraft already identified, take the necessary action to establish procedural separation between the aircraft and, if necessary, limit the number of aircraft permitted to enter the area.' (Chapter 8, 8.8.4.1)

questions.question: What is the distance-based wake turbulence separation minimum between a SUPER aircraft and a MEDIUM aircraft?
questions.explanation: Table 8.7.3.4 shows: 'SUPER preceding, MEDIUM succeeding: 13.0 km (7.0 NM)'

questions.question: What is the maximum range scale or condition under which PSR may be used alone for identification?

options.B: Only within 10 NM of the radar site
options.C: Only when the aircraft reports its position over a point shown on the display and the track is consistent
options.D: Only when SSR is also available
