questions.explanation: ATS units determine the lowest usable flight level.

questions.question: The lowest usable flight is that flight level which corresponds to, or is immediately above, the established minimum flight altitude:
questions.explanation: Definition matches the statement.

questions.question: The funnel area shall spread out to maximum width of either side of runway axis at a distance of 12100 ft.
questions.explanation: At 12100 ft from runway end, the funnel width is 2000 ft each side.

questions.question: Flight crew shall be provided with transitional level in due time prior to reaching it during descent.
questions.explanation: Required to be given in advance.

questions.question: Contours are the lines joining the points of equal pressure.
questions.explanation: Contours join points of equal elevation, not pressure. Isobars join equal pressure. (Correction from review.)

questions.question: Except when it is known that aircraft has already received the QNH altimeter setting, it should be included in:

options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: QNH must be included in descent clearance, taxi clearance, and circuit clearance if not already given.

questions.question: Altimeter setting provided to aircraft shall be rounded up to the nearest upper whole hectopascal.
questions.explanation: Altimeter settings are rounded to the nearest whole hectopascal (not necessarily upper).

questions.question: Lines joining the places of equal atmospheric pressure are called Isogonals.
questions.explanation: Correction: Isogonals are lines of equal magnetic variation; isobars are equal pressure.

questions.question: When an aircraft is assigned a speed to maintain by ATC, the flight crew shall include this speed in:
questions.explanation: Assigned speed must be included in position reports.

questions.question: The length of clearway should not exceed half the length of the associated runway.
questions.explanation: Clearway length is limited to half the runway length.

questions.question: ADS reports shall be composed of data blocks. Which of the following is not a data block of an ADS report?
questions.explanation: SSR is not a data block in ADS reports; ADS data blocks include Basic ADS, Ground vector, and Short term intent.

questions.question: A runway turn pad is subjected to slow moving traffic making hard turns and consequently ______ on pavement.
questions.explanation: Hard turns impose higher stresses on pavement.

questions.question: AIP should be published
questions.explanation: AIP is published in loose-leaf form for easy amendment.

questions.question: An AIP shall have
questions.explanation: AIP has three parts: GEN, ENR, AD.

questions.question: The required minimum track separation between two departing aircraft using the same NDB at a distance of 15 NM from NDB is:
questions.explanation: Minimum track separation at NDB is 30° for that distance.

questions.question: The minimum possible separation based on distance for the aircraft on crossing tracks when the preceding aircraft is maintaining TAS 20 Kt or more faster:
questions.explanation: For crossing tracks, 10 NM minimum when preceding is 20 kt faster.

questions.question: For investigation purpose flight progress strips shall be retained at least for a period of:
questions.explanation: Flight progress strips retained for 30 days.

questions.question: In which chapter of Doc 4444/ ATM 501, the Separation Methods And Minima are specified:
questions.explanation: Chapter 5 of Doc 4444 covers separation methods and minima.

questions.question: Separation shall be provided between all flights in:

options.D: In class A and B airspace.

questions.explanation: In Class A and B, separation is provided between all flights. In Class C, VFR are not separated from VFR.

questions.question: Separation shall be provided between IFR flights in airspace of:

options.C: Class A, B, C and D.
options.D: Class A, B, C,D and E.

questions.explanation: IFR separated from IFR in Classes A, B, C, D, and E.

questions.question: Separation shall be provided between IFR and VFR flights in airspace of:

options.C: Class A, B, C and D.

questions.explanation: IFR and VFR separated in Class B and C (and also D? Actually D separates IFR from IFR, and IFR from VFR? Let's check: In Class D, IFR separated from IFR and receive traffic info on VFR, but IFR and VFR are not necessarily separated? Actually ICAO: Class D – IFR and VFR flights are permitted, IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information about VFR flights, VFR receive traffic info about all flights. So separation between IFR and VFR is not provided. So only Class B and C provide separation between IFR and VFR. So answer D is correct.

questions.question: Separation shall be provided between IFR and Special VFR flight:

options.C: In class B and C airspace.
options.D: Irrespective of the class of airspace.

questions.explanation: Special VFR flights are separated from IFR regardless of airspace class.

questions.question: Separation shall be provided between VFR flights in:

options.D: Class A, B, C and D.

questions.explanation: VFR flights are separated from other VFR only in Class B.

questions.question: Under what circumstances, larger separation than the specified minima should be applied:

options.C: In case of technical problems with the flight

questions.explanation: Larger separation may be needed for unlawful interference or navigational difficulties; technical problems may also warrant but not explicitly listed.

questions.question: When one type of separation has failed, action must be taken to establish another type of separation.
questions.explanation: If separation fails, a new type must be applied.

questions.question: ATC shall normally authorize only one level for an aircraft beyond its control area.
questions.explanation: Only one level is authorized beyond control area.

questions.question: When cruise climb has been permitted by appropriate ATS authority, an aircraft shall be authorized to employ cruise climb:
questions.explanation: Cruise climb is authorized between two levels.

questions.question: An aircraft may be cleared to change its level:
questions.explanation: Level change can be specified by time, rate, or place.

questions.question: Cruising levels to the aircraft flying to the same destination shall be assigned in a manner that will be:

options.A: Correct for an approach sequence at the destination.
options.C: In accordance with the procedures at the destination aerodrome.

questions.explanation: Assigned cruising levels should facilitate approach sequence at destination.

questions.question: An aircraft at a cruising level shall normally have priority over other aircraft requesting that level.
questions.explanation: Aircraft already at a level have priority over others requesting it.

questions.question: When two or more aircraft are at the same level, the preceding aircraft shall normally have the priority.
questions.explanation: Preceding aircraft has priority at same level.

questions.question: An aircraft may be cleared to a level previously occupied by another aircraft after the latter has reported vacating it, except when:

options.B: The higher aircraft is effecting cruise climb.
options.C: There is difference in performance of the aircraft involved.

questions.explanation: All three situations may prevent immediate clearance to a vacated level.

questions.question: How two or more aircraft with different performance, established in the same holding pattern, can be given descent.

options.B: Both the aircraft must have each other insight.
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Step descent and restricted rate are used; visual sighting is not required.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation TCAS stands for?

options.B: Traffic Alert and collision avoidance system.

questions.explanation: TCAS stands for Traffic Alert and Collision Avoidance System.

questions.question: What will you understand when an aircraft is said to be established on track from the NDB:

options.A: Aircraft is moving away from the NDB.
options.B: Aircraft is moving towards the NDB.
options.C: Aircraft is established in NDB holding pattern.
options.D: Aircraft is passing abeam an NDB station.

questions.explanation: Established 'on track from' means outbound from the NDB.

questions.question: The larger separation between aircraft shall be provided than the specified minima when there is:
questions.explanation: Wake turbulence requires larger separation; other conditions may not necessarily increase separation minima.

questions.question: The airspace around a fuel dumping aircraft is affected by the fuel release is known as the Vapor Zone and other known traffic shall be separated from the aircraft dumping fuel by:

options.B: 15 minutes flying time or 50 NM behind 1000 ft above and 3000 ft below.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Separation for fuel dumping includes horizontal 10 NM not behind, or 15 min/50 NM behind with vertical buffers.

questions.question: What will you understand when an ATS route is designated as RNP 10?

options.A: Navigation accuracy of plus or minus10 NM on a 95 percent containment basis is required.
options.B: It is 10th version of a RNAV route.
options.C: An aircraft is authorized to alter its headings but shall remain with 10 NM from the center line of the route.

questions.explanation: RNP 10 means 95% containment within 10 NM.

questions.question: Longitudinal separation may be established by requiring aircraft to:

options.B: Arrive over a geographical location at a specified time.
options.C: Hold over a geographical location until a specified time.

questions.explanation: All these methods can establish longitudinal separation.

questions.question: The tracks will be considered as same tracks if the angular difference between tracks is 316 degrees.
questions.explanation: Angular difference of 316° is equivalent to 44° (since 360-316=44) which is less than 45°, so they are considered same track.

questions.question: The opposite tracks and intersecting tracks will be considered as reciprocal tracks if the angular difference of which is:

options.C: a) and b) both are correct

questions.explanation: Reciprocal when angular difference >135° and <225°.

questions.question: If the angular difference between tracks is 50 degrees than the tracks will be considered as:
questions.explanation: 50° is between 45° and 135°, so crossing tracks.

questions.question: Irrespective of the speeds and availability of navigational aids, the minimum longitudinal separation minima based on time for aircraft on same level and same tracks is:
questions.explanation: Absolute minimum time separation is 15 minutes.

questions.question: The minimum longitudinal separation minimum based on time for aircraft that have departed from the same aerodrome for same level and same tracks is 03 minutes provided:

options.A: Both aircraft are maintaining same speed
options.B: Both aircraft may not be maintaining same speed.
options.C: Preceding aircraft is maintaining TAS of 40 kts or faster.

questions.explanation: 3 minutes separation applies when preceding is 40 kt faster.

questions.question: When Nav aids permit frequent determination of speed and position, the minimum longitudinal separation based on time for aircraft at same level on crossing tracks is:
questions.explanation: With frequent nav aid fixes, 10 minutes for crossing tracks.

questions.question: BBC002 A300 Dacca / Dubai FL320 estimating Karachi VOR at 0635 via Chore, SVA752 A300 LAH / RYD FL320 estimating Karachi VOR at 0638 via NH, your action will be:
questions.explanation: They are at same level and same point with 3 min separation; must change level of one aircraft, typically the later one.

questions.question: For aircraft at the same level when preceding aircraft is maintaining 40 kts or more faster, the minimum longitudinal separation based on time for crossing tracks is:
questions.explanation: When preceding is 40 kt faster, crossing track separation is 10 minutes.

questions.question: PIA319 A310 F320 OPLA/OPKC reported MURLI 0253 UTC. PIA300 B747 KAR/ISL F330 MURLI 0255UTC. PIA 319 is requesting F340. PIA319 can be cleared to F340 at:
questions.explanation: For same point, need 10 minutes separation for crossing? Actually PIA319 at 0253, PIA300 at 0255 – they are 2 min apart. To climb PIA319, we need to ensure separation. Typically 10 min between same point for same level? The answer key says 0304 UTC (11 min after 0253? Actually 0253+11=0304) – so likely 10 min + 1 min buffer.

questions.question: PIA303 B747 LHR/KAR TAS 440 Kts maintaining F320, position 120 NM inbound to RK. SAI 234 B737 LHR/KAR TAS 400 Kts maintaining F300, positions 130 NM inbound to RK requesting F320. SAI 234 can be cleared to F320 by applying longitudinal separation based on distance using DME.
questions.explanation: DME separation can be used if both on same DME station and distance separation meets minima.

questions.question: In application of longitudinal separation based on distance using DME there is a clause Which states that each aircraft utilizes the same on track DME station. The term "on track" means:

options.A: Aircraft is flying directly inbound to the DME station.
options.B: Aircraft is flying directly outbound from the DME station
options.C: Both aircraft are flying either directly inbound to the DME station or directly outbound from the DME station.

questions.explanation: On track means either all inbound or all outbound.

questions.question: Application of longitudinal separation minimum with Mach number technique is only possible after the aircraft concerned have reported over the same reporting point.
questions.explanation: Mach number technique requires common reporting point.

questions.question: Longitudinal separation minimum with Mach number technique between two turbojet aircraft on same track, whether in level, climbing or descending shall be 07 min if the preceding aircraft is:
questions.explanation: 0.04 Mach difference gives 7 min separation.

questions.question: DME based separation between aircraft at same level and speed on crossing tracks is:
questions.explanation: For crossing tracks at same speed, DME separation is 20 NM.

questions.question: DME based separation between aircraft at same level on crossing tracks with preceding aircraft TAS 20 kts faster is:
questions.explanation: If preceding is 20 kt faster, DME separation reduces to 10 NM.

questions.question: Which of the following equipment enables frequent determination of position and speed of the aircraft?
questions.explanation: VOR and DME together give position and speed; radar is not a navigational aid for position/speed determination.

questions.question: PIA302 B747 KAR/LHR F330 estimate RK 0340. AIC187 A320 AMRATSAR / OMDB F340 estimate Rk 0350 with a request for F350. When AIC187 can be given climb to F360?

options.D: All a), b) and c) correct

questions.explanation: AIC187 can climb to F360 after 0355 (10 min after PIA302 estimate) or after reporting RK, or before 0335 if before conflict.

questions.question: The airspace around designated track which has been used for fuel dumping giving full allowance for vapour zone shall be treated reserved airspace for:
questions.explanation: Fuel dumping area reserved for 15 minutes.

questions.question: What is the lateral separation minimum between holding area and en-route aircraft?

options.A: 3 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.B: 5 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.C: 10 minutes flying time from the holding area.
options.D: 15 minutes flying time from the holding area.

questions.explanation: 5 minutes separation between holding area and en-route traffic.

questions.question: Minimum separation between departing aircraft from the same aerodrome is one minute when aircraft are to fly on tracks diverging by:
questions.explanation: 1 minute separation requires divergence of at least 45 degrees.

questions.question: Minimum separation between departing aircraft is one minute when aircraft are to fly on tracks diverging by 45 degrees. If approved by the appropriate ATS authority this minima may be reduced when aircraft are using:
questions.explanation: Parallel runways may allow reduced separation.

questions.question: Minimum take-off separation between two departing aircraft when preceding aircraft is more than 40 Kts faster than the following aircraft and both aircraft propose follow the same route is:
questions.explanation: If preceding is >40 kt faster, 2 minutes.

questions.question: What is the required separation if a departing aircraft will be flown through the level of preceding departing aircraft and both aircraft propose to follow the same track and the separation will be maintained or increased while levels are crossed.
questions.explanation: 5 minutes when passing through level.

questions.question: If an arriving aircraft is making a complete instrument approach, a departing aircraft may take-off in any direction until:

options.A: Arriving aircraft is 4 NM on final.
options.B: Arriving aircraft has started its procedure turn or base turn leading to final approach.
options.C: Arriving aircraft leaves its initial approach altitude for the instrument approach.

questions.explanation: Departure may continue until arriving aircraft starts procedure turn or base turn.

questions.question: Some of the longitudinal separation minima based on time or distance require that the preceding aircraft to maintain TAS of 20 kts or 40 kts faster than the succeeding aircraft at the time of application. How will you fulfill this restriction?

options.A: TAS is given in the flight plan.
options.C: Ascertain TAS from the flight crew at the time of application.

questions.explanation: Must get actual TAS from flight crew at time of application.

questions.question: If an arriving aircraft is making a straight-in approach, a departing aircraft may take-off in any direction:

options.A: 5 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.B: 15 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.C: 20 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.
options.D: 25 minutes before the arriving aircraft is estimated over the instrument runway.

questions.explanation: 5 minutes before estimated time over threshold.

questions.question: The ATC unit concerned shall not be required to apply wake turbulence separation for arriving VFR flights landing behind on the same runway as the preceding landing HEAVY or MEDIUM aircraft.
questions.explanation: Wake turbulence separation may not be required for VFR if they maintain visual separation.

questions.question: The ATC unit concerned shall not be required to apply wake turbulence separation between arriving IFR flights executing visual approach when it has reported the preceding aircraft in sight and has been instructed to follow and maintain own separation.
questions.explanation: Visual separation with own separation maintains safety.

questions.question: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minima between light aircraft arriving behind a heavy aircraft;
questions.explanation: Light behind Heavy: 3 minutes.

questions.question: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between departing light aircraft behind a departing heavy aircraft when both aircraft use the same runway?
questions.explanation: Departing Light behind Heavy: 2 minutes.

questions.question: Longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between departing light aircraft behind a departing heavy aircraft when both aircraft use the same runway is two minutes or when the aircraft are using parallel runways separated by less than:
questions.explanation: Parallel runways less than 760 m apart require 2 min.

questions.question: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between LIGHT aircraft departing behind a MEDIUM aircraft from the same runway?
questions.explanation: Light behind Medium: 2 minutes (for departure).

questions.question: What is the longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between MEDIUM aircraft arriving behind a HEAVY aircraft?
questions.explanation: Medium behind Heavy: 2 minutes (arriving).

questions.question: Longitudinal wake turbulence separation minimum between a departing Medium aircraft behind a departing Heavy aircraft is three minutes when Medium aircraft is taking-off from:

options.A: Parallel runway less than 760 m apart.
options.B: An intermediate part of a parallel runway less 760 m apart.
options.C: An intermediate part of same runway.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: 3 min required if taking off from intermediate point or parallel less than 760 m.

questions.question: A situation in which in the opinion of a pilot or air traffic services personnel, the distance between the aircraft as well as their relative positions and speed have been such that the safety of the aircraft involved may have been compromised is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of aircraft proximity (AIRPROX).

questions.question: An aircraft proximity in which the safety of the aircraft may have been compromised is classified as:
questions.explanation: Safety not assured is the classification when safety may be compromised.

questions.question: What is the wake turbulence minima between a Medium and a Heavy aircraft when Heavy aircraft is making a missed approach and the Medium aircraft is utilizing an opposite direction runway for take-off?
questions.explanation: 2 minutes for opposite runway operations.

questions.question: A procedural controller can give direct routing to an aircraft.
questions.explanation: Procedural controller can issue direct routing if separation can be ensured.

questions.question: A Pakistani registered aircraft hijacked at an airport in Pakistan or any foreign country is landing or has landed at an airport within Pakistan will be considered from hijacking category:
questions.explanation: As per Pakistan procedures, category B.

questions.question: Only controlled flights constitute essential traffic for each other when not separated by the appropriate separation minima.
questions.explanation: Essential traffic is defined for controlled flights not separated.

questions.question: A VFR flight would not constitute essential traffic to other VFR flight except in:
questions.explanation: In Class B, VFR flights are separated from each other, so they are essential traffic.

questions.question: The elevation of the highest point of the aerodrome is called:
questions.explanation: Aerodrome elevation is the highest point elevation.

questions.question: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if adequate separation can be provided by this controller when:

options.A: He is confident of handling the traffic.
options.B: A superior officer is present in Control Tower.
options.C: Each aircraft is continuously visible to this controller.
options.D: Controller and pilot are known to each other.

questions.explanation: Visibility of each aircraft is key for reduced separation.

questions.question: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if each aircraft is continuously visible to the flight crews of the other aircraft concerned and:

options.B: Pilots report that they can maintain their own separation.
options.C: Pilots report that they are visual with each other.
options.D: Pilots report that they are changing from IFR too VFR and visual with each other.

questions.explanation: Pilots must report they can maintain own separation.

questions.question: Separation minimum in the vicinity of aerodrome may be reduced by the aerodrome controller if one aircraft is following another and the flight crew of the succeeding aircraft reports that:

options.A: Preceding traffic is a light aircraft.
options.B: Preceding traffic is in sight and separation can be maintained.
options.C: Changing from IFR to VFR and field in sight.

questions.explanation: Both B and D are required: in sight and ability to maintain separation.

questions.question: Information on essential local traffic known to the controller shall be transmitted without delay to departing aircraft only.
questions.explanation: Information should be transmitted to all affected aircraft, not just departing.

questions.question: The air traffic services units should be when practicable advises aircraft operators or the designated representatives when anticipated delays are expected to exceed:
questions.explanation: Delays exceeding 30 minutes should be advised.

questions.question: The approach sequence shall established in a manner which will facilitate arrival of the maximum number of aircraft with:
questions.explanation: Goal is least average delay.

questions.question: If departures are delayed, the delayed flights shall normally be cleared in order based on their estimated time of departure, except that deviation from this order may be made to facilitate maximum number of departures with least average delay.
questions.explanation: Order is based on ETD, but exceptions for efficiency.

questions.question: In the event the aerodrome controller, after a take-off clearance or a landing clearance has been issued, becomes aware of a runway incursion or the imminent occurrence thereof, or the existence of any obstruction on or in close proximity to the runway likely to impair the safety of an aircraft taking off or landing, appropriate action shall be taken as follows:

options.A: cancel the take-off clearance for a departing aircraft
options.B: instruct a landing aircraft to execute a go-around or missed approach
options.C: in all cases inform the aircraft of the runway incursion or obstruction

questions.explanation: All actions are appropriate.

questions.question: An aircraft moving on a taxiway of an aerodrome is considered to be:
questions.explanation: Aircraft on taxiway is part of air traffic.

questions.question: When communication by a system of visual signals is deemed to be adequate, what will a signal of white flashes from Control Tower will mean to a vehicle on the runway?

options.B: Vacate the maneuvering area in accordance with local procedure.
options.C: Vacate the runway immediately and watch out for aircraft.

questions.explanation: White flashes mean vacate maneuvering area per local procedure.

questions.question: When the visual signals from control tower are not observed by the vehicle on the runway, what is the alternate way of getting the runway cleared?

options.A: Send another vehicle to the runway.
options.B: Ask the aircraft to make a low go.

questions.explanation: Flashing runway/taxiway lights is alternate method.

questions.question: Which of the following aircraft shall be given priority for landing?

options.C: Aircraft with shortage of crew members.

questions.explanation: Hospital/medical emergency aircraft have priority.

questions.question: A departing aircraft shall normally have the priority over the aircraft landing or in the final stages of an approach using the same or intersecting runway.
questions.explanation: Landing aircraft normally have priority over departing.

questions.question: In the interest to expedite traffic, if a clearance for immediate take-off is issued to an aircraft, the pilot of the aircraft shall:

options.A: Arrange take-off as early as possible.
options.D: Establish visual contact with the arrival.

questions.explanation: Immediate take-off means one continuous movement without stopping.

questions.question: Landing aircraft will not normally be permitted to cross the runway threshold on its final approach until the preceding departing aircraft has crossed the end of the runway-in-use, or has started a turn, or until all preceding landing aircraft are clear of the runway-in-use.
questions.explanation: Standard runway separation.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be included in the essential information on aerodrome conditions:

options.A: Aircraft and vehicle operating on the manoeuvring area.
options.B: Construction or maintenance work on the manoeuvring area.
options.C: Rough or broken surfaces on a runway a taxiway.
options.D: Failure or irregular operation of part or all of the aerodrome lighting system.
options.E: All of the above can be included

questions.explanation: All are essential information; the question asks what cannot be included, so the answer is E (none of the above are excluded).

questions.question: Any or all VFR operation on or in the vicinity of aerodrome may be suspended by:

options.A: The approach control unit or the appropriate ACC

questions.explanation: All three can suspend VFR operations.

questions.question: An aircraft may not sustain a specified rate of climb or descent. The controller shall ensure that alternative methods of maintaining separation are applied in a timely manner.
questions.explanation: If aircraft cannot comply, apply alternative separation.

questions.question: What will you understand if in response to your instructions to an aircraft you receive this call from the aircraft? Unable, TCAS resolution advisory.

options.A: The pilot is unable to comply with TCAS resolution advisory.
options.B: The pilot is unable to comply with ATC instructions due TCAS resolution advisory.
options.C: The pilot is unable to comply with TCAS resolution advisory and ATC instruction.
options.D: None of the above is correct.

questions.explanation: Pilot is following TCAS RA and cannot comply with ATC instruction.

questions.question: What will a pilot understand when he receives this call from ATC. "RVR RUNWAY 36R 200m, 250m and 150 m"

options.C: RVR RWY 36R Stop end zone is 150m
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: The three values correspond to touchdown, midpoint, and stop end zones respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following is the call-sign for the Area Control Centre in Lahore?
questions.explanation: Call-sign for ACC is 'Lahore Control'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the call-sign for an Approach control office in Karachi?
questions.explanation: Approach control call-sign is 'Karachi Approach'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the call-sign for a Flight Information Centre Mumbai?
questions.explanation: FIC call-sign is 'Mumbai Information'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the call-sign for an Aeronautical Station Dushanbe?
questions.explanation: Aeronautical station call-sign is 'Dushanbe Radio'.

questions.question: Except in dire emergency, fuel dumping shall not be permitted:
questions.explanation: Fuel dumping restricted over populated areas, thunderstorms, and holding areas.

questions.question: Unless specially authorized by DG CAA, a flight shall not be cleared in compliance with VFR in Pakistan airspace:

options.C: Over the sea, more than 16 km from the coast for more than one hour.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All three are restrictions on VFR.

questions.question: Single engine aircraft are not permitted to fly over the high sea at a distance from where they cannot glide to the aerodrome.
questions.explanation: The statement is false; single-engine aircraft may fly over sea if within gliding distance of land.

questions.question: As per laid down VVIP procedures no aircraft shall be allowed to land or depart from an aerodrome or operate in the circuit of the aerodrome for the period:

options.A: 5 minutes before take-off till 5 minutes after take-off
options.B: 5 minutes before ETA till door close.
options.C: From 'DOOR CLOSE' to 5 minutes after take-off.
options.E: From door close to 15 minutes after take-off

questions.explanation: VVIP restrictions apply from door close to 5 minutes after take-off.

questions.question: As per laid down VVIP procedures, no aircraft shall be allowed to land or depart from an aerodrome or operate in the circuit of the aerodrome for the period:

options.A: 5 minutes before take-off till 5 minutes after take-off
options.B: 5 minutes before ETA till door open.
options.C: From 'DOOR CLOSE' to 15 minutes after take-off.
options.E: From door close to 5 minutes after take-off

questions.explanation: For VVIP, restrictions apply from 5 min before ETA to door open.

questions.question: All aircraft flying in Pakistan airspace are to keep listening watch on:
questions.explanation: 121.5 MHz is the emergency frequency and listening watch is required.

questions.question: No VFR operation shall be allowed during the period when the VVIP is expected to operate in:

options.C: In the block of 25 NM around VVIP aircraft in controlled airspace.
options.D: 1000 feet above or below the VVIP aircraft.

questions.explanation: VVIP restrictions apply in controlled airspace.

questions.question: When VVIP is flying in Pakistan no aircraft shall be cleared to operate in the block of 25 NM either side of intended route of the VVIP flight irrespective of type of airspace.
questions.explanation: The block restriction may not apply to all airspace; often only controlled.

questions.question: When RA is initiated and pilot deviates from ATC clearance, he is not considered to be violating ATC instructions.
questions.explanation: Pilot following TCAS RA is not considered in violation.

questions.question: Except when a clearance is obtained from ATC unit, a VFR flight shall not take-off or land within a control zone or enter in aerodrome traffic pattern when:

options.A: Ground visibility is less than 8 Km.
options.B: Ground visibility is less than 5 Km.
options.C: Ground visibility is less than 3 Km.
options.D: Ground visibility is less than 1.5 Km.

questions.explanation: VFR requires ground visibility ≥5 km.

questions.question: Except when necessary for take-off or landing, or except by permission from the Director General, CAA, a VFR flight shall not be flown Over the congested areas of cities, towns or settlements or over an open-air assembly of persons at a height less than _______ above the highest obstacle within a radius of 600 m from the aircraft;
questions.explanation: Minimum height over congested areas is 1000 ft above obstacles.

questions.question: Except necessary for take-off or landing a VFR flight shall not operate over the congested area of cities, town or settlement at a height less than 1000 feet else where not less than:
questions.explanation: Elsewhere minimum height is 500 ft.

questions.question: Two aircraft at the same track and the same level, if succeeding aircraft with TAS 450 Kts and preceding aircraft is TAS 490 Kts. The first aircraft departed at 1835, the 2nd aircraft can be cleared to depart at:
questions.explanation: Preceding is 40 kt faster, so 2 minutes separation required, so 1835 + 2 = 1837.

questions.question: A VFR flight below F150 may be authorized to enter a control zone for landing or depart on a direct track in IMC conditions provided that:

options.A: Only one aircraft or formation of aircraft is cleared for a specific period.
options.B: No other traffic is operating or expected to operate within the control zone.
options.C: Ground visibility is not less than 1.5 km.

questions.explanation: All conditions must be met for special VFR.

questions.question: An IFR flight shall relay estimates and flight level over the FIR boundary prior to entry into FIR at least by:
questions.explanation: 15 minutes prior to boundary.

questions.question: Only IFR flights are permitted in:
questions.explanation: Class A is IFR only.

questions.question: In which classification of airspace IFR and VFR flights are permitted and are separated from each other.
questions.explanation: In Class B, IFR and VFR are separated.

questions.question: In which classification of airspace VFR flights are separated from IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of other VFR flights.
questions.explanation: In Class C, VFR separated from IFR, and receive traffic info on other VFR.

questions.question: "IFR and VFR flights are permitted and all flights are subject to Air Traffic Control service. IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights and receive traffic information in respect of VFR flights. VFR flights receive traffic information in respect of all other flights". Name the classification of airspace.
questions.explanation: Description matches Class D.

questions.question: In class E airspace IFR flights are separated from other IFR flights.
questions.explanation: Class E provides separation between IFR flights.

questions.question: In which classification of airspace IFR flights receive Advisory service in respect of other IFR flights.
questions.explanation: Class F provides advisory service.

questions.question: IFR and VFR flights are permitted and receive flight information service if requested. Name the classification of airspace:
questions.explanation: Class G provides FIS on request.

questions.question: SIGMET information passed to the aircraft should cover a portion of the route up to the flying of:
questions.explanation: SIGMET covers up to two hours of flight.

questions.question: In Pakistan airspace the revised ATS route structure is based on RNAV.
questions.explanation: Pakistan has implemented RNAV-based routes.

questions.question: Aircraft on Int'l flights proceeding to Afghanistan from Pakistan should be:
questions.explanation: Q-3.201 answer key indicates RNP 10 required for Afghanistan flights.

questions.question: Pilots of aircraft meeting RNP type requirements shall indicate this at item 10 of the Flight Plan as:
questions.explanation: Item 10 uses 'R' to indicate RNP capability (e.g., R for RNP approved).

questions.question: In Karachi and Lahore FIR, airspace outside the control areas and outside the ATS routes, irrespective of vertical limits, the classification of airspace is G.
questions.explanation: Outside CTA and ATS routes, airspace is Class G.

questions.question: Karachi and Lahore FIR airspace is prescribed as RVSM airspace between F290 to F410 inclusive, in:

options.B: Control Areas and ATS routes above F155.

questions.explanation: RVSM applies in controlled airspace from F290-F410.

questions.question: In Pakistan visual climb/descent is permitted for IFR flights below F 150 in VMC in class A, B and C airspace.
questions.explanation: Visual climb/descent is not permitted in Class A, B, C; only in D and E maybe.

questions.question: Two aircraft are considered to be on reciprocal track when the angular difference between their tracks is:

options.C: Less than 135° and more than 45° or less than 315° and more than 225°

questions.explanation: Reciprocal when angular difference >135° and <225°.

questions.question: Procedures for RVSM require that to operate in RVSM airspace an aircraft shall have two primary altimeter systems, one automatic altitude keeping device, one altitude operating transponder and one:
questions.explanation: RVSM requires an altitude alerting device.

questions.question: When operating in RVSM airspace an RVSM compliant aircraft must inform ATC whenever:

options.A: No longer RVSM compliant due equipment failure.
options.B: Experience loss of redundancy of altimetry system.
options.C: Encounter turbulence that can affect the capability to maintain the flight level.

questions.explanation: All are reportable events.

questions.question: During the cleared transition between levels an RVSM approved aircraft should not over shoot the assigned flight level by more than:
questions.explanation: Overshoot limit is 150 ft.

questions.question: An RVSM aircraft should be given priority for levels allocation over Non-RVSM approved aircraft.
questions.explanation: RVSM aircraft have priority for level allocation.

questions.question: Non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to climb to and operate above F410 or descend to and operate below F290 under certain conditions.
questions.explanation: Non-RVSM can operate outside RVSM airspace.

questions.question: All the civilian aircraft are fitted with emergency VHF frequency 121.5MHZ.
questions.explanation: Civilian aircraft are required to carry 121.5 MHz.

questions.question: Non-RVSM approved aircraft being utilized on humanitarian purpose may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace.
questions.explanation: Humanitarian flights may be exempted.

questions.question: A non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace if it is transporting an spare engine mounted under its wings.
questions.explanation: Aircraft with external stores may be exempted.

questions.question: A non-RVSM aircraft shall not be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace even if the aircraft was RVSM approved and has experienced an equipment failure and is being flown to a maintenance facility for repair in order to meet RVSM requirement.
questions.explanation: May be cleared if flown to maintenance facility.

questions.question: A non-RVSM approved aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace if it is an state aircraft.
questions.explanation: State aircraft may be exempted.

questions.question: Non-RVSM approved aircraft owned by Military or Police may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace at RVSM levels.
questions.explanation: Military/Police may be cleared.

questions.question: Non-RVSM approved aircraft of Military, Custom and Police may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace keeping RVSM separation.
questions.explanation: If not RVSM approved, they cannot be separated using RVSM minima.

questions.question: A Non-RVSM approved State-aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace. The status of the aircraft shall be mentioned in the ICAO approved flight plan in item:
questions.explanation: Item 18 is for other information, including STS/.

questions.question: A Non-RVSM approved Police aircraft may be cleared to operate in RVSM airspace. The status of the aircraft shall be mentioned in the ICAO approved flight plan in item 18 as:
questions.explanation: Use STS/Police in Item 18.

questions.question: If in Karachi or Lahore FIR greater than moderate turbulence are reported by the pilot than RVSM operation may be suspended in the affected area only.
questions.explanation: Turbulence may cause suspension of RVSM.

questions.question: Essential information on aerodrome shall include information relating to:

options.A: Water on a runway, a taxiway or an apron,
options.B: Failure or irregular operation of part or all of the aerodrome lighting system
options.C: Work in progress on the terminal building outside apron

questions.explanation: Essential info includes runway/taxiway/apron conditions and lighting; terminal building work outside apron is not essential.

questions.question: An RVSM aircraft must have functioning ACAS on board.
questions.explanation: RVSM does not require ACAS; only altimetry and altitude alerting.

questions.question: Which frequency has been allotted for inter pilot communication.
questions.explanation: 123.45 MHz is for air-to-air communication.

questions.question: If an RVSM approved aircraft when flying in RVSM airspace, is unsure of its vertical position due degradation of primary altimeter system then the pilot of the aircraft should:

options.C: Broadcast position, flight level and intentions on 121.5Mhz
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All actions are required.

questions.question: When flying in RVSM airspace if a pilot reports unsure of vertical position due to degradation of primary altimetry systems and requests to continue in RVSM airspace. ATC may accommodate the aircraft if:

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

questions.explanation: Any of these separation methods may be used.

questions.question: When flying in RVSM airspace if a pilot reports failure of all altitude control system and requests to continue in RVSM airspace, ATC may accommodate this aircraft if:

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

questions.explanation: Same as previous.

questions.question: When flying in RVSM airspace, if a pilot reports loss of redundancy in primary altimetry systems, and requests to continue in RVSM airspace. Aircraft can be permitted to operate in RVSM airspace under certain conditions.
questions.explanation: May be permitted with separation.

questions.question: A circle of radius of 13,000 feet measured from the reference point. Within this area obstructions are to be limited to a height of 150 feet above level of the reference point. According to the National Airfield Clearance Policy the above surface is known as:
questions.explanation: Inner Horizontal Surface has radius 13,000 ft and height limit 150 ft.

questions.question: When flying in RVSM airspace a pilot reports turbulence (greater than moderate) which may impact the aircraft's capability to maintain a flight level. The aircraft may continue to operate in RVSM airspace provided:

options.C: Conventional vertical separation can be provided.

questions.explanation: All separation methods may be used.

questions.question: Local flights at an uncontrolled aerodrome outside control zone may not submit a flight plan provided such flights operate below:
questions.explanation: Below 1000 ft, local flights may not need flight plan.

questions.question: A flight wholly conducted in the vicinity of an aerodrome i.e. take-off from an aerodrome, fly in the circuit and land back. Such a flight is called.
questions.explanation: Definition of local flight.

questions.question: Flight plan for a flight is accepted before ETD within:
questions.explanation: Accepted up to 2 hours before ETD.

questions.question: Flight Plan must be submitted at least 30 minutes before for:

options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: IFR and international VFR require 30 min advance.

questions.question: In Pakistan airspace it is mandatory for turbojet aircraft to carry ACAS if its maximum certified take-off mass is:
questions.explanation: ACAS required for turbojet > 15000 kg.

questions.question: In Pakistan airspace it is mandatory for turbojet aircraft to carry ACAS if the aircraft is carrying:
questions.explanation: ACAS required if carrying 30 passengers or more.

questions.question: In Pakistan serviceable transponder including Mode C is mandatory in controlled airspace above:
questions.explanation: Transponder with Mode C required above FL250.

questions.question: In Karachi, Lahore CTA and Cherat Control Zone, the carriage of serviceable transponder is mandatory for all aircraft.
questions.explanation: All aircraft require transponder in those areas.

questions.question: No scheduled flight shall be cleared to depart if delay exceeds 06 hours of its scheduled ETD unless cleared by concerned ATS directorate. This rule shall not apply if the delay is due to:

options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All are valid reasons for delay beyond 6 hours.

questions.question: A notice containing information that does not qualify for the origination of a NOTAM or for inclusion in the AIP, but which relates to flight safety, air navigation, technical, administrative or legislative matters is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of AIC.

questions.question: Which of the following will constitute essential traffic?

options.A: Any flight not separated in Class A and B airspace;
options.B: IFR flights not separated in Class C, D and E airspace;
options.C: IFR flights not separated from VFR flights in Class C airspace;

questions.explanation: All are essential traffic situations.

questions.question: Essential traffic information shall include:

options.B: Cruising level of the aircraft concerned.
options.C: Estimate of the reporting point nearest to the point of confliction.

questions.explanation: All three are included.

questions.question: COSPAS SARSAT is a satellite system designed to provide distress alert and location data to KC RCC for 406MHZ beacons activated:
questions.explanation: COSPAS-SARSAT provides data to Pakistan RCC for beacons activated in Pakistan.

questions.question: Mach number technique shall not be applied if cruise phase of a flight is less than two hour.
questions.explanation: No such restriction; it is applied regardless of cruise length.

questions.question: Mach number technique may be applied to aircraft which propose to follow diverging or converging tracks.
questions.explanation: Mach number technique is for same track only.

questions.question: What shall be the separation using Mach Number technique if the following aircraft maintains a Mach number .02 greater than the preceding aircraft and the distance to cover is 1200 NM?
questions.explanation: For 0.02 Mach difference and distance 1200 NM, separation is 14 minutes.

questions.question: What shall be the separation using Mach Number technique if the following aircraft maintains a Mach number .02 greater than the preceding aircraft and the distance to cover is 1800 NM?
questions.explanation: For 0.02 Mach difference and 1800 NM, separation is 16 minutes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a content of ATC clearance?

options.C: Name of the pilot in command.

questions.explanation: Pilot's name is not part of clearance.

questions.question: Clearance limit is a point to which an aircraft is granted an air traffic control clearance.
questions.explanation: Definition of clearance limit.

questions.question: An aircraft shall be cleared for the entire route to the aerodrome of first intended landing when there is reasonable assurance that prior coordination will be affected between those units under whose control the aircraft will subsequently come.
questions.explanation: Cleared to destination if coordination assured.

questions.question: If the clearance for levels covers only part of the route, that part of the route shall be specified in ATC clearance so that the pilot can comply with:
questions.explanation: To ensure pilot knows which part to fly at cleared levels in case of RCF.

questions.question: VFR flight shall file a flight plan when operated within class-B airspace.
questions.explanation: VFR in Class B requires a flight plan.

questions.question: An aircraft operating on a controlled aerodrome shall not taxi on the manoeuvring area without clearance from the aerodrome control tower.
questions.explanation: Taxi clearance required.

questions.question: As AIP Pakistan part (2) en-route (1.2) Para 9a), more than one aircraft or formation of aircraft can be cleared to operate within a control zone.
questions.explanation: Only one aircraft or formation at a time in a control zone.

questions.question: A VFR flight can be operated at transonic and ultrasonic speeds.
questions.explanation: VFR flights are not permitted at transonic/supersonic speeds.

questions.question: Ceiling is the height above the ground or water of the base of the lowest layer of cloud below 10,000 feet covering more than half of the sky.
questions.explanation: Ceiling is below 20,000 ft (6,000 m) per ICAO definition.

questions.question: A controlled airspace extending upwards from a specified limit above the earth is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of control area.

questions.question: TAF or TAFOR means:
questions.explanation: TAF stands for Terminal Aerodrome Forecast.

questions.question: Which of the following phase of emergency will start if an aircraft which has been cleared to land but fails to land within five minutes of its ETA.
questions.explanation: Failure to land within 5 min after ETA triggers Alert Phase.

questions.question: A situation wherein there is reasonable certainty that an aircraft and its occupants are threatened by grave and imminent danger or require immediate assistance is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of Distress Phase.

questions.question: Which of the following flights has the top most priority?

options.D: A flight being subject to unlawful interference

questions.explanation: Unlawful interference has highest priority.

questions.question: If only a portion of flight is to be provided with air traffic control service, a flight plan may not be submitted.
questions.explanation: A flight plan must be submitted for any controlled portion.

questions.question: In IMC an arriving aircraft cannot be handed over to aerodrome control tower by the unit providing approach control service after landing.
questions.explanation: Handover is possible after landing in IMC.

questions.question: In IMC, Tower Controller may keep the control of a departing aircraft till the time the aircraft leaves the vicinity of the aerodrome.
questions.explanation: In IMC, control is transferred to approach after take-off.

questions.question: When IMC prevails, the responsibility for the control of departing aircraft may be transferred by aerodrome controller to the approach control immediately before the aircraft enters the runway in use for take-off.
questions.explanation: Transfer can occur before entering runway.

questions.question: When IMC prevails, the responsibility for the control of departing aircraft may be transferred by Tower to the approach control immediately after the aircraft is air borne.
questions.explanation: Transfer after airborne is common.

questions.question: Personnel near the manoeuvring area who may constitute a hazard to the aircraft concerned shall be considered as essential local traffic.
questions.explanation: They are essential traffic.

questions.question: Initiating overdue action is not the responsibilities of Approach control unit.
questions.explanation: Approach control may initiate overdue action.

questions.question: Approach control unit shall inform ACC about:

options.A: Lowest level available at the holding facility.
options.B: Average time interval between successive approaches.
options.C: Revision in EAT when variation is of 5 minutes or more.

questions.explanation: All are required to be advised.

questions.question: A Cessna is proceeding from Lahore to Faisalabad at 6500 ft. What altimeter setting the pilot is supposed to keep en route?
questions.explanation: At 6500 ft, above transition altitude, use standard 1013.2.

questions.question: As Tower controller which of the following you will ensure before clearing a VFR flight to operate in the control zone?

options.A: The flight shall operate below F150.
options.B: The flight shall not conflict with any IFR flight.
options.C: Traffic information can be passed to all IFR flight regarding the VFR flight.

questions.explanation: All three conditions must be met.

questions.question: Except where special VFR clearance is issued, VFR flights shall not take-off or land at an aerodrome within a control zone or enter the aerodrome traffic zone or traffic pattern when the ceiling is less than 1500 ft or the ground visibility is less than:
questions.explanation: VFR requires 8 km visibility.

questions.question: For successive visual approaches, radar or non radar separation shall be maintained until the pilot of succeeding aircraft reports having the preceding aircraft insight.
questions.explanation: Separation maintained until visual contact.

questions.question: For successive visual approaches, if the pilot of succeeding aircraft reports having the preceding aircraft insight, the succeeding aircraft then may be instructed to follow and maintain own separation from the preceding aircraft.
questions.explanation: Then own separation can be applied.

questions.question: The controller shall issue a caution of possible wake turbulence when two successive arriving aircraft are of a Heavy wake turbulence category.
questions.explanation: Wake turbulence caution issued for heavy-heavy? Actually for all heavy behind heavy.

questions.question: From which sector an aircraft has entered the holding pattern if having reached the fix it turns onto an outbound heading for the appropriate period of time then turns left onto the holding side to intercept the inbound track.
questions.explanation: Description matches Sector 1 entry (parallel entry).

questions.question: Buffer area for a holding area is the area extending NM beyond the boundary of the holding area.
questions.explanation: Buffer area extends 5 NM.

questions.question: A revised expected approach time shall be transmitted to the aircraft without delay whenever it differs from that previously transmitted EAT by minutes or more.
questions.explanation: Revision of 5 min or more must be transmitted.

questions.question: Expected approach time shall be transmitted to the aircraft by the most expeditious means whenever it is anticipated that the aircraft will be required to hold for minutes or more.
questions.explanation: If hold of 30 min or more anticipated, EAT must be given.

questions.question: Local flight can file a flight plan, prior to departure on R/T with Control Tower.
questions.explanation: Local flights may file by R/T.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a critical position in the circuit?
questions.explanation: Crosswind is not a standard critical position; the critical positions are downwind, base, final.

questions.question: When the runway surface condition changes its colour due to moisture, it is said to be:
questions.explanation: Colour change due to moisture means damp.

questions.question: When aerodrome lighting is required to be on, it shall be displayed from minutes before any ETA.
questions.explanation: Lighting on 15 min before ETA.

questions.question: Aerodrome lighting shall be displayed until minutes after any departure.
questions.explanation: Lighting remains on 15 min after departure.

questions.question: As per laid down timing, aerodrome lighting shall be displayed at high intensity during day time whenever:

options.B: The cloud base is less than 700 feet.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: High intensity when visibility <5 km or cloud base <700 ft.

questions.question: An aircraft may be advised, delay not determined, if the delay in approach is due to weather.
questions.explanation: Weather delays may not have a fixed EAT.

questions.question: During night, irrespective of weather conditions, approach lighting shall be displayed minutes before ETA.
questions.explanation: Approach lighting on 15 min before ETA at night.

questions.question: During night, irrespective of weather conditions, approach lighting shall be displayed until minutes after any ATD.
questions.explanation: Approach lighting remains on 15 min after ATD.

questions.question: During night, irrespective of weather conditions, runway lighting shall be displayed minutes before ETA.
questions.explanation: Runway lighting on 15 min before ETA.

questions.question: During night, irrespective of weather conditions, runway lighting shall be displayed until minutes after any departure.
questions.explanation: Runway lighting remains on 15 min after departure.

questions.question: During night, irrespective of weather conditions, runway lighting shall be displayed before any startup:
questions.explanation: Runway lighting displayed immediately before start-up at night.

questions.question: An expected approach time shall be determined for an arriving aircraft that will subjected to a delay of minutes or more.
questions.explanation: EAT determined if delay of 10 min or more expected.

questions.question: EAT shall be transmitted to aircraft as soon as possible preferably not latter than:

options.A: Commencement of its initial descent from cruising level.

questions.explanation: EAT should be given before descent from cruising level.

questions.question: EAT shall be transmitted to aircraft as soon as possible so that:

options.A: Pilot can proceed to the departure point
options.B: Pilot can divert to the alternate.
options.C: Pilot can choose appropriate method of absorbing delay.

questions.explanation: EAT helps pilot decide how to absorb delay.

questions.question: If an aircraft is held en route or at a location other than the initial approach fix, the aircraft concerned shall as soon as practicable be given:
questions.explanation: If held en route, need onward clearance time, not EAT.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an objective of air traffic control service?

options.D: Maintain orderly flow of air traffic.

questions.explanation: Enhancing business is not an ATC objective.

questions.question: The aggregation of the airborne functions and the ground-based functions (air traffic services, airspace management and air traffic flow management) required to ensure the safe and efficient movement of aircraft during all phases of operations is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of Air Traffic Management.

questions.question: A service established with the objective of contributing to a safe, orderly and expeditious flow of air traffic by ensuring that ATC capacity is utilized to the maximum extent possible, and that the traffic volume is compatible with the capacities declared by the appropriate ATS authority is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of ATFM.

questions.question: A control area or portion thereof established in the form of a corridor is called.
questions.explanation: Definition of airway.

questions.question: A situation wherein apprehension exists as to the safety of an aircraft and its occupants is called:
questions.explanation: Apprehension exists in Alert Phase.

questions.question: The vertical distance of a level, a point or an object considered as a point, measured from mean sea level is known as:
questions.explanation: Altitude is vertical distance from mean sea level.

questions.question: A defined rectangular area on the ground or water under the control of appropriate authority selected or prepared as a suitable area over which an aeroplane may make a portion of its initial to a specified height.
questions.explanation: Definition of clearway.

questions.question: A specified altitude or height in a 3D instrument approach operation at which a missed approach must be initiated if the required visual reference to continue the approach has not been established:
questions.explanation: Definition of Decision Altitude/Height (DA/H).

questions.question: Over high terrain and mountainous areas the minimum obstacle clearance is:
questions.explanation: In mountainous areas, minimum obstacle clearance is 2000 ft.

questions.question: Radio telephony logs shall be retained at the location for at least:
questions.explanation: RTF logs retained for 120 days.

questions.question: Flight progress strips shall be retained at the location for at least:
questions.explanation: Flight progress strips retained for 30 days? Wait earlier answer for Q-3.88 said 30 days, but here the answer key might say 120? Actually from earlier: Q-3.88 answer was 30 days. But this question Q-3.306 in original? The numbering is off. In the original, Q-3.306 is about FPS retention. The answer key says for Q-3.306 it is 120 days? Let's check: In the answer key for Non-Radar, Q-3.88 was 30 days, Q-3.306 was 120 days? Actually we need to check the original document: The list of answers shows Q-3.88 answer b (30 days), Q-3.306 answer b (120 days). So we follow that.

questions.question: Messages in fixed service that need special handling should be assigned priority indicator.
questions.explanation: Special handling messages use priority DD.

questions.question: Runway of Gawadar Airport is a:
questions.explanation: Gawadar runway is non-precision approach.

questions.question: No aircraft shall be operated within Karachi and Lahore CTA, CTR and Cherat CTR unless equipped with a serviceable transponder including Mode C.
questions.explanation: Transponder including Mode C is required above FL250, not all areas. Actually earlier Q-3.309 says False because transponder without Mode C may be acceptable? The answer key says False.

questions.question: No aircraft shall be operated within Karachi and Lahore CTA, CTR and Cherat CTR unless equipped with a serviceable transponder.
questions.explanation: Transponder required in those areas.

questions.question: What is the next flight level available after F410 on tracks 000 to 179 degrees?
questions.explanation: Above F410, next levels are in 50 ft increments? Actually above FL410, the standard cruising levels are in 50 ft increments? But ICAO: above FL410, use 2000 ft separation? Actually RVSM ends at FL410, above that non-RVSM uses 2000 ft separation. The answer key says F450, so we follow.

questions.question: What is the next RVSM Flight level available on tracks between 180 to 359 degrees after F400?
questions.explanation: RVSM airspace is F290-F410, after F400 the next is F410 but that is still within RVSM; actually after F400, the next is F410, but the question says 'after F400' on tracks 180-359, the next RVSM level is F410? But answer key says D (None of the above) because F410 is the next, but maybe they mean after F410? Actually question wording: 'after F400' - the next is F410, which is an option? But options don't include F410? They have A: F410, so why D? Let's check original: answer key says Q-3.312 answer d. That might be because F410 is the next, but they consider F410 as the end, so maybe no level after? I'll follow answer key.

questions.question: A runway served by visual aids and non-visual aid(s) intended for landing operations following an instrument approach operation type A and a visibility not less than 1 000 m., is categorized as:
questions.explanation: Non-precision approach runway type A with visibility ≥1000m.

questions.question: A runway served by visual aids and non-visual aid(s) intended for landing operations following an instrument approach operation type B with a decision height (DH) lower than 60 m (200 ft) but not lower than 30 m (100 ft) and a runway visual range not less than 300, is categorized as:

options.D: Precision approach runway CAT III A
options.E: Precision approach runway CAT III B

questions.explanation: CAT II has DH 30-60 m and RVR ≥300 m.

questions.question: A runway served by visual aids and non-visual aid(s) intended for landing operations following an instrument approach operation type B with a decision height (DH) not lower than 60 m (200 ft) and either a visibility not less than 800 m or a runway visual range not less than 550 m, is categorised as:

options.D: Precision approach runway CAT III A
options.E: Precision approach runway CAT III B

questions.explanation: CAT I has DH ≥60 m and visibility ≥800 m or RVR ≥550 m.

questions.question: A runway served by visual aids and non-visual aid(s) intended for landing operations following an instrument approach operation type B to and along the surface of the runway and intended for operations with a decision height (DH) lower than 30 m (100 ft), or no decision height and a runway visual range not less than 175 m, is categorized as:

options.C: Precision approach runway CAT III A
options.D: Precision approach runway CAT III B
options.E: Precision approach runway CAT III C

questions.explanation: CAT III A has DH <30 m or no DH, RVR ≥175 m.

questions.question: A runway served by visual aids and non-visual aid(s) intended for landing operations following an instrument approach operation type B to and along the surface of the runway and intended for operations with a decision height (DH) lower than 15 m (50 ft), or no decision height and a runway visual range less than 175 m but not less than 50 m, is categorized as:

options.A: Precision approach runway. CAT I II
options.B: Precision approach runway CAT III A
options.C: Precision approach runway CAT III B
options.D: Precision approach runway CAT III C

questions.explanation: CAT III B has DH <15 m or no DH, RVR between 50 m and 175 m.

questions.question: The length of an arc of a great circle which subtends an angle of one degree at the center of the earth is called:
questions.explanation: One nautical mile is defined as one minute of latitude, equivalent to one degree of arc at the center? Actually one nautical mile is 1 minute of latitude, not 1 degree. But the question says an angle of one degree - that would be 60 NM? Actually the definition: The nautical mile is defined as one minute of arc along a meridian. So if it's one degree, that's 60 NM. But the answer is C: Nautical Mile. Probably they refer to the definition.

questions.question: A runway intended for the operation of aircraft using visual approach procedures or an instrument approach procedure to a point beyond which the approach may continue in visual meteorological conditions. is known as:
questions.explanation: Non-instrument runway is for visual approaches or IAP to VMC point.

questions.question: A number expressing the bearing strength of a pavement for unrestricted operations is known as:
questions.explanation: PCN is Pavement Classification Number.

questions.question: SSR code used by the aircraft in emergency:
questions.explanation: 7700 is emergency.

questions.question: Decision altitude / height is a specified altitude or height (A/H) in the precision approach at which a missed approach must be initiated if the required visual reference to continue the approach has not been established. What is the reference datum for decision height?
questions.explanation: Decision Height is referenced to the threshold elevation.

questions.question: Minimum Sector Altitude is determined for:
questions.explanation: MSA is for IFR flights.

questions.question: Information issued by a meteorological watch office concerning the occurrence or expected occurrence of specified en-route weather phenomena which may affect the safety of aircraft operations are known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of SIGMET.

questions.question: The projection on the earth's surface of the path of an aircraft, the direction of which (path) at any point is usually expressed in degrees from North (true, magnetic or grid) is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of track.

questions.question: A first solo flight shall only be authorized when:

options.B: When it doesn't conflict with any other flight.
options.C: The Instructor present in the control tower during the flight.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All conditions must be met.

questions.question: The stop way is to be constructed to flexible pavements to a bearing strength which is equal to:
questions.explanation: Stopway strength is 1/3 of runway.

questions.question: The basic designator of an ATS Route shall consist of one letter of the letter from the alphabet followed by a number from 1 to 999. Which of the following alphabets indicate that the route is an area navigation route which form the part of regional network:
questions.explanation: Area navigation routes are designated with letters L, M, N, P.

questions.question: A method of navigation which permits aircraft operation on any desired flight path with in the coverage of station -- referenced navigation aids or within the limits of the capability of self-contained aids, or a combination of these is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of Area Navigation (RNAV).

questions.question: The basic designator of an ATS Route shall consist of one letter of the letter from the alphabet followed by a number from 1 to 999. Which of the following alphabets indicate that the route does form the part of regional network but is not an area navigation route?
questions.explanation: Routes with letters A, B, G, R are non-RNAV regional routes.

questions.question: A side clearance of feet on either side of the runway is to be maintained free of all obstructions for the full length of the runway including over-run.
questions.explanation: Side clearance is 750 ft.

questions.question: The outer margin of the side clearance 750 ft feet on either side of the runway is known as:
questions.explanation: The outer margin is the runway clearance line.

questions.question: A defined area where aircraft can be held, or bypassed, to facilitate efficient surface movement of aircraft is known as:
questions.explanation: Holding bay is defined.

questions.question: One Meter is equal to feet.
questions.explanation: 1 m = 3.28084 ft, approximately 3.281.

questions.question: One Nautical Mile is equal to:
questions.explanation: 1 NM = 1.15 statute miles.

questions.question: One Kilogram is equal to:
One Kilometer is equal:

options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: 1 km = 0.54 NM and 0.62 statute mile.

questions.question: An airspace of defined dimensions, above land areas or territorial waters of a State, within which the flight of aircraft is restricted in accordance with certain specified conditions, is know as:
questions.explanation: Restricted area has specified conditions.

questions.question: Who shall give way to other aircraft when two aircraft are converging at approximately same level.

options.A: Aircraft that has other aircraft on its left.
options.B: Aircraft that has other aircraft on its right.
options.C: Aircraft that has other aircraft on its left and right.
options.D: Aircraft that has other aircraft on its 9 O' clock.

questions.explanation: Aircraft with other on right gives way (right-of-way rule).

questions.question: At Lahore we have LEMOM 3 A departure for Runway 36 R. The validity indicator Three (3) indicates that:

options.A: A change has been made from the previous 2nd version.
options.B: There are three types of LEMOM departures.
options.C: The aircraft cannot proceed on LEMOM 1 or 2 Departure due traffic.
options.D: It is the original Designator of the LEMOM departure.

questions.explanation: The number indicates version (3rd version).

questions.question: A reporting plan which establishes the conditions of ADS-C data reporting (i.e. data required by the air traffic services unit and frequency of ADS-C reports which have to be agreed to prior to using ADS-C in the provision of air traffic services) is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of ADS-C agreement.

questions.question: A means by which the terms of an ADS-C agreement will be exchanged between the ground system and the aircraft, via a data link, specifying under what conditions ADS-C reports would be initiated, and what data would be contained in the reports is known as:
questions.explanation: That is an ADS contract.

questions.question: When it is requested or necessary to for a helicopter to proceed at a slow speed above the surface air taxiing maybe authorized, normally:
questions.explanation: Air-taxiing is normally below 20 kt and in ground effect.

questions.question: An airspace of defined dimensions established around an aerodrome for the protection of aerodrome traffic is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of Aerodrome Traffic Zone.

questions.question: An aircraft system based on secondary surveillance radar (SSR) transponder signals which operates independently of ground-based equipment to provide advice to the pilot on potential conflicting aircraft that are equipped with SSR transponders is known as:
questions.explanation: ACAS (Airborne Collision Avoidance System) is defined.

questions.question: Except where special VFR clearance is issued, VFR flights shall not take-off or land at an aerodrome within a control zone or enter the aerodrome traffic zone or traffic pattern when the ground visibility is less than 05 Km and the ceiling is less than:
questions.explanation: Ceiling must be at least 1500 ft.

questions.question: The projection on the earth's surface of the path of an aircraft, the direction of which path at any point is usually expressed in degrees from North (true, magnetic or grid) is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of track.

questions.question: Regional QNH is the actual QNH value within an altimeter region.
questions.explanation: Regional QNH is the lowest actual QNH in the region.

questions.question: The airspace between the transition altitude and the transition level is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of transition layer.

questions.question: At any time the thickness of transition layer shall not be less than:
questions.explanation: Minimum thickness is 1000 ft.

questions.question: A pilot of an aircraft at 3000 ft above mean sea level outside aerodrome traffic zone shall set his altimeter to:
questions.explanation: At 3000 ft above sea level, above transition altitude? Actually if outside ATZ and at 3000 ft AMSL, it's below transition altitude likely, but the answer key says QNE? No, QNE is standard pressure; but if below transition altitude, should use QNH. However the question says 'outside aerodrome traffic zone', maybe they refer to en-route below transition? The answer key says A (QNE). So we follow.

questions.question: Aerodrome elevation is the elevation of the highest point at the aerodrome.
questions.explanation: Aerodrome elevation is the elevation of the highest point of the landing area, not the entire aerodrome.

questions.question: At an aerodrome QNH is 1003 Hpa and over the airfield an aircraft is maintaining F70. What will be the altitude of the aircraft?
questions.explanation: If QNH is lower than 1013, altitude is lower than flight level. Flight level 70 = 7000 ft at standard. Difference in pressure: 1013-1003=10 hPa, approx 300 ft. So altitude = 7000 - 300 = 6700 ft.

questions.question: For visual maneuvering over an aerodrome, the height is based on:

options.C: Elevation of the highest point on the landing area.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Height is referenced to aerodrome elevation or threshold elevation? Actually for visual maneuvering, height is above aerodrome elevation.

questions.question: Runway of Gilgit Airport is:
questions.explanation: Gilgit has non-instrument runway.

questions.question: What is the next VFR level after F285 in RVSM airspace?

options.D: VFR operation is not permitted in RVSM airspace.

questions.explanation: VFR flights are not permitted in RVSM airspace.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct for Lahore Runway 36R where the RWY length is 3360m, Stopway is 122m and Clearway is 305m.
questions.explanation: TORA = runway length = 3360; ASDA = TORA + stopway = 3482; TODA = TORA + clearway = 3665. So only C is correct? But the answer key says C (TODA=3665).

questions.question: The airspace classification for Hyderabad aerodrome traffic zone is:
questions.explanation: Hyderabad ATZ is Class C.

questions.question: All the VFR flights shall comply with air traffic control clearance when:

options.A: Operated in class B, C and D airspace.
options.B: Forming a part of aerodrome traffic at controlled aerodrome.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: In all those situations VFR must comply with ATC clearance.

questions.question: CNS/ATM is an abbreviation of:

options.A: Communication Navigation Satellite / Air Traffic Management.
options.B: Communication Navigation System / Air Traffic Management.
options.C: Communication Navigation Surveillance / Air Traffic Management system
options.D: Communication Navigation Surveillance / Air Traffic Management.

questions.explanation: CNS/ATM stands for Communication, Navigation, Surveillance / Air Traffic Management.

questions.question: All the piston engine aircraft with maximum certified take-off mass of more than 5700 kg are to carry Ground Proximity Warning System (GPWS).
