questions.explanation: GPWS is required for turbine engine aircraft, not piston, above certain mass.

questions.question: All the ATS routes are class A:
questions.explanation: ATS routes are Class A above F155 outside control areas.

questions.question: In the event of delay of in excess of the proposed departure time or EET to be spent on ground at intermediate stop, a new FPL should be submitted and the old be cancelled.
questions.explanation: If delay exceeds one hour, new FPL required.

questions.question: No H/C shall operate under VFR in flight visibility of less than:
questions.explanation: Helicopter VFR visibility minimum is 1.5 km.

questions.question: All the aircraft are required to establish two way communication with the concerned ACC at least prior to entry in Karachi or Lahore FIR.
questions.explanation: 15 minutes prior to entry.

questions.question: The movement of pedestrians or vehicles on the maneuvering area will be subject to authorization from:
questions.explanation: Aerodrome controller authorizes movement on maneuvering area.

questions.question: At Nawabshah Airport a control zone of 10 NM around the airport with an upper limit of F 75 has been established. The classification of this airspace is:
questions.explanation: Nawabshah control zone is Class C.

questions.question: A defined rectangular area on the ground at the end of take-off run available prepared as a suitable area in which an aircraft can be stopped in the case of an abandoned take-off is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of stopway.

questions.question: Isolation bay shall not be less than from the parking, buildings or public areas.
questions.explanation: Isolation bay distance is 100 m.

questions.question: R.E.S.A is an abbreviation of:
questions.explanation: RESA stands for Runway End Safety Area.

questions.question: A.C.L is an abbreviation of:
questions.explanation: ACL stands for Altimeter Check Location.

questions.question: Which of the following requirement is correct for fire category 9?
questions.explanation: All quantities are correct for fire category 9.

questions.question: Fire prevention vehicle Barracuda can carry:
questions.explanation: Barracuda carries all those.

questions.question: Selection of the Runway-in-Use is the responsibility of:
questions.explanation: Aerodrome controller selects runway-in-use.

questions.question: Normally the aircraft shall land and take-off into the wind but the aerodrome controller can select runway-in-use on which aircraft do not land and take-off into the wind depending on:

options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All factors may influence runway selection.

questions.question: If the QNH value falls lower than the standard pressure value i.e. 1013.2 Hpa than the thickness of the transition layer will:
questions.explanation: Lower QNH reduces transition layer thickness.

questions.question: Required discharge rate for Fire category 9 is:
questions.explanation: Fire Cat 9 requires 4500 L/min.

questions.question: Response time for RFF vehicles to reach the site is:

options.A: Not more the 2 min and 30 seconds.
options.C: Not more than 3 minutes and 30 seconds.

questions.explanation: Response time is ≤3 minutes.

questions.question: The actual path of the aeroplane flown over the ground is known as:
questions.explanation: Track made good is the actual path over ground.

questions.question: Which of the following is a lift augmentation device?
questions.explanation: All are lift augmentation devices.

questions.question: Function of spoiler at the leading edge is to spoil the lift producing capability of the wing.
questions.explanation: Spoilers reduce lift by disrupting airflow.

questions.question: Spoilers are used to reduce the speed of the aircraft in the air.
questions.explanation: Spoilers increase drag and reduce lift; they can reduce speed but are primarily for lift dump and roll control.

questions.question: Spoilers are used after landing to enable the aircraft to stop in a comparatively short distance.
questions.explanation: Spoilers increase drag and reduce lift, aiding braking.

questions.question: Aircraft which have double wings are called biplanes.
questions.explanation: Biplanes have two wings.

questions.question: A flight which is subject to an ATC Clearance is called a:
questions.explanation: Definition of controlled flight.

questions.question: When so requested by an aircraft and provided it is agreed by the pilot of the other aircraft and so authorized by the appropriate ATS authority an ATC unit may clear a controlled flight in VMC during the hours of day light to fly subject to maintaining own separation to one other aircraft remaining in VMC operating in:

options.C: Airspace Classes A, B and C

questions.explanation: Own separation is allowed in Class D and E.

questions.question: The lateral limit of CTR shall extend to at least:

options.A: 10 NM from the centre of the aerodrome in the direction which approaches may be made.
options.B: 5 NM from the centre of the aerodrome or aerodromes in the direction from which the approaches may be made.
options.C: 15 NM from the centre of the aerodrome.
options.D: 25 NM from the centre of the aerodrome.

questions.explanation: CTR lateral limit is at least 5 NM in approach direction.

questions.question: If a control zone is located within lateral limits of a control area it shall extend upwards from:

options.B: 1000 feet above the highest obstruction in control zone.
options.C: Surface of the earth ( ground or water )
options.D: Any level determined by the appropriate authority.

questions.explanation: Control zone extends from surface.

questions.question: The lower limit of control area shall be established at a height above ground or water of not less then:
questions.explanation: Lower limit of control area is 700 ft AGL.

questions.question: Alerting service shall be provided to:

options.A: All aircraft provided with Air Traffic Control Service.
options.B: In so far as practicable to all other aircraft having filed a flight plan or otherwise known to the air traffic services.
options.C: To any aircraft known or believed to be the subject of unlawful interference.

questions.explanation: Alerting service provided to all those.

questions.question: A VFR flight can request F 65 provided it proposes to fly on track:
questions.explanation: VFR levels: for tracks 180-359, odd levels +500? Actually F65 is for tracks 180-359? Standard VFR: eastbound (000-179) odd thousands +500? Actually ICAO: VFR below FL150 use +500 ft. For F65, that's 6500 ft, which is for westbound tracks (180-359).

questions.question: N9002 estimates position A at 1232 UTC. There is no communication with the aircraft since 1224 UTC. The aircraft will be considered overdue or in uncertainty phase at:
questions.explanation: Uncertainty phase starts 30 minutes after ETA or time of last communication? Actually if no communication since 1224, and ETA at 1232, then after 30 min from ETA? The answer key says D (1224) - maybe because no communication for 8 minutes before ETA? Actually the rule: uncertainty phase when aircraft fails to report within 30 minutes after ETA or 30 min after last communication. Since last communication at 1224, 30 min later is 1254. But options don't include that. The answer key says D (1224) - possibly they consider overdue at the time communication was lost? I'll follow answer key.

questions.question: VOR transmitter operates on VHF band from 108 to 118 MHz.
questions.explanation: VOR frequency range is 108-118 MHz.

questions.question: The entry into the holding pattern shall be according to the bearing.
questions.explanation: Entry is according to heading, not bearing.

questions.question: Generally the effect of jet stream on the estimates of aircraft remains from:

options.A: From end of November to end of March.
options.B: From end of October to end of January.
options.C: From end of December to end of February.

questions.explanation: Jet stream effects are from November to March.

questions.question: A special geographical location used to define an area navigation route or the flight path of an aircraft employing area navigation is called a way point.
questions.explanation: Definition of waypoint.

questions.question: The phrase cleared via flight plan route shall not be used unless the flight Plan has been read.
questions.explanation: Must have read the flight plan before using that phrase.

questions.question: AIRPROX is a code word used in an air traffic incident report to designate aircraft proximity.
questions.explanation: Definition of AIRPROX.

questions.question: The relevant authority designated by the state responsible for providing air traffic services in the airspace concerned is known as:
questions.explanation: Appropriate ATS authority is defined.

questions.question: Which of the following will be considered as an essential local traffic to an aircraft?

options.A: A vehicle close to maneuvering area which constitutes hazard to the concerned aircraft.
options.B: Personnel crossing close to runway, which constitute hazard to the concerned aircraft.
options.C: Any other aircraft constituting hazard to the concerned aircraft.

questions.explanation: All are essential local traffic.

questions.question: The range over which the pilot of an aircraft on the center line of runway can see the runway surface markings or lights delineating the runway or identifying its center line is known as:
questions.explanation: Definition of RVR.

questions.question: IFR and VFR flights are permitted. IFR flights are provided with Air Traffic Control Service and are separated from other IFR flights. All flights receive traffic information as far as is practical. The classification of airspace is:
questions.explanation: Class E: IFR separated from IFR, all receive traffic info as far as practicable.

questions.question: An aircraft stand should provide the following minimum clearance between aircraft using the stand and any adjacent building, aircraft on another stand and other object with code letter E.
questions.explanation: Code E requires 7.5 m clearance.

questions.question: The lateral and vertical limits of aerodrome traffic zone are 5 NM around and 2000 ft above from the ground or water.
questions.explanation: ATZ limits are 5 NM and 2000 ft AGL.

questions.question: PAPI's shall be operated:

options.C: During night and during day in poor visibility.
options.D: During day and night irrespective of visibility and weather conditions.

questions.explanation: PAPI operated day and night regardless of visibility.

questions.question: ATS route G202 indicates that:

options.A: It is in class A airspace.
options.C: It forms a part of regional network.

questions.explanation: Routes with letter G are regional (non-RNAV).

questions.question: Unless otherwise instructed all turns in the standard race track holding pattern shall be made:

options.C: In accordance with the entry pattern.

questions.explanation: Standard holding pattern turns right.

questions.question: Angular difference between direction of True north and Magnetic north at any given place is called:
questions.explanation: Variation is the angle between true north and magnetic north.

questions.question: A collision of a vehicle with an aircraft parked on a stand with no person on board will be considered as an accident.
questions.explanation: Without injury or substantial damage, it may be an incident, not accident.

questions.question: The time at which the ATC expects that an arriving aircraft following a delay will leave the holding pattern to complete its approach for a landing is known as:
questions.explanation: EAT is Expected Approach Time.

questions.question: The estimated time at which an aircraft will commence its movement associated with departure is called estimated elapsed time.
questions.explanation: That is estimated off-block time (EOBT), not EET.

questions.question: Information on essential local traffic known to the controller should be transmitted to departing and arriving aircraft when practicable.
questions.explanation: The earlier answer says false? Actually Q-3.412 in original says False. So follow that.

questions.question: The estimated time required to proceed from one significant point to another is known as Estimated Elapsed Timed.
questions.explanation: EET is estimated elapsed time.

questions.question: For IFR flights, the time at which it is estimated that the aircraft will arrive over that designated point, defined by reference to navigation aids, from which it is intended that an instrument approach procedure will be commenced is called:
questions.explanation: That is Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA).

questions.question: The time at which it is estimated that a VFR flight will arrive over the aerodrome is called its:
questions.explanation: VFR ETA is over the aerodrome.

questions.question: For IFR flights, if no navigation aid is associated with the aerodrome, the time at which the aircraft will arrive over the aerodrome is called its:
questions.explanation: ETA over aerodrome.

questions.question: Specified information provided to air traffic services units, relative to an intended flight or portion of a flight of an aircraft is called:
questions.explanation: Definition of flight plan.

questions.question: A specified altitude or height in a 2D instrument approach operation or circling approach operation below which descent must not be made without the required visual reference is called:
questions.explanation: MDA/H is defined as the minimum altitude/height in non-precision or circling approaches.

questions.question: Unsynchronized transponder responses received by a ground interrogator unit triggered by interrogation signals from other ground units are known as:
questions.explanation: FRUIT (False Replies Unsynchronized In Time) are unsynchronized responses from other interrogators.

questions.question: False codes displayed as the result of a SSR Radar interrogator's inability to differentiate between the individual response codes of two or more aircraft closer together in space than the minimum acceptable to the equipment are known as:
questions.explanation: Garble occurs when replies from close aircraft overlap, causing false codes.

questions.question: The level of unwanted signals coming into the receiver from the aerial or generated within the receiver itself is known as:
questions.explanation: Noise is internal or external unwanted signal.

questions.question: The direction of the plane of the electric field of an electromagnetic wave is known as:
questions.explanation: Polarization is the orientation of the electric field.

questions.question: Visual indication, in non symbolic form, on a radar display of the position of an aircraft obtained by primary or secondary radar is known as:
questions.explanation: Radar blip is the non-symbolic indication.

questions.question: The visual indication on a radar display of a radar signal reflected from an object is known as:
questions.explanation: Radar echo is the reflected signal indication.

questions.question: In the Radar display the top most line of display is called:
questions.explanation: The top line is the General Information Area.

questions.question: For surveillance radar approach rate of descent for 3 degree glide path is calculated by:
questions.explanation: Rate of descent (ft/min) ≈ 5 × Ground speed (kts) for 3° glide path.

questions.question: In SSR interrogation transmission are made on a frequency of 1090 MHz.
questions.explanation: SSR interrogations are on 1030 MHz; replies on 1090 MHz.

questions.question: In a transponder reply spacing between the second framing pulse and SPI pulse is:
questions.explanation: SPI pulse occurs 4.35 µs after the second framing pulse.

questions.question: When echoes beyond the normal range of radar are seen on a radar display. This phenomenon is known as:
questions.explanation: Anomalous propagation causes echoes beyond normal range due to atmospheric conditions.

questions.question: Anomalous propagation is caused due to:
questions.explanation: Meteorological conditions (temperature inversions, humidity) cause anomalous propagation.

questions.question: An aircraft speed measured along the sweep, at which, Radar with MTI cannot detect an aircraft is known as:
questions.explanation: Blind velocity occurs when the target's radial velocity causes cancellation in MTI.

questions.question: Radar echoes from ground objects, terrain and clouds are known as:
questions.explanation: Clutter is unwanted echoes from terrain, weather, etc.

questions.question: Circular polarization is useful for:

options.A: Canceling the returns from unwanted object.
options.B: Canceling the returns from ground object
options.C: Canceling the returns from circular object
options.D: Canceling the returns from stationary objects

questions.explanation: Circular polarization helps cancel returns from symmetrical (circular) objects like raindrops.

questions.question: An electronic device used in Radar, which cancel the returns from stationary objects isknown as:
questions.explanation: MTI (Moving Target Indicator) cancels stationary clutter.

questions.question: The speed of the aircraft directly read from Air Speed Indicator is known as:
questions.explanation: IAS is the direct reading from the airspeed indicator.

questions.question: IAS corrected for position and instrument error is known as:
questions.explanation: CAS is IAS corrected for position and instrument error.

questions.question: The speed at which an aircraft passes through the air is known as:
questions.explanation: TAS is the actual speed relative to the air mass.

questions.question: The speed of the air at which it is passing over the surface of the aircraft, will be the true airspeed of the aircraft.
questions.explanation: TAS is the speed of the aircraft relative to the air, equal to the speed of air over the surface.

questions.question: The speed of the aircraft expressed as a ratio of True Air Speed to the local speed of sound is known as:
questions.explanation: Mach number is TAS divided by speed of sound.

questions.question: The True airspeed of a descending aircraft will increase if the aircraft maintains constant Indicated airspeed:
questions.explanation: Descending to lower altitude increases air density, so TAS decreases for constant IAS.

questions.question: The color of a VFR flight observe in the display:
questions.explanation: VFR flights are displayed in yellow on the radar display.

questions.question: The alarm indicator "PR" normally appears on the display at:
questions.explanation: PR (Pre-Runway?) appears 120 seconds before? Actually according to answer key, it's 120 seconds.

questions.question: Echoes caused by pockets of air or flock of birds are known as:
questions.explanation: Angels are radar returns from birds, insects, or atmospheric inhomogeneities.

questions.question: Radar control service is provided outside controlled airspace along the ATS routes in Karachi and Lahore FIR's above:
questions.explanation: Radar service outside controlled airspace is provided above FL155.

questions.question: Within collocated primary and secondary Terminal Approach Radar of Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad, the radar separation up to a height of F255 is:

options.D: 5 NM in all direction except for reciprocal traffic.

questions.explanation: Within TMA radar coverage, separation is 5 NM up to FL255.

questions.question: Radar separation using SSR position symbol of transponder equipped aircraft is 15 NM while aircraft are on:
questions.explanation: 15 NM separation applies for all track types when using SSR position symbols.

questions.question: Where lateral separation is not established vertical separation shall be maintained between transponder equipped aircraft on reciprocal tracks outside coverage of Terminal Approach Radars from the time their SSR position symbol before passing each other are apart by:
questions.explanation: For reciprocal tracks, vertical separation required when symbols are 50 NM apart before passing.

questions.question: Where lateral separation is not established vertical separation shall be maintained between transponder equipped aircraft on reciprocal tracks outside coverage of Terminal Approach Radars from the time their SSR position symbol before passing are apart by 50 NM until they have passed each other and are apart by:
questions.explanation: After passing, separation can be released when symbols are 5 NM apart.

questions.question: Out side Terminal Approach Radar of Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad, when the progress of the transponder equipped aircraft on same or crossing tracks whether at same level, climbing or descending is continuously monitored on radar, the longitudinal separation is:

options.A: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 30 NM.
options.B: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 50 NM.
options.C: 05 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 60 NM.
options.D: 20 minutes and distance between these aircraft is not less than 30 NM.

questions.explanation: Longitudinal separation: 5 minutes and 30 NM distance.

questions.question: When the radar based spacing required for the avoidance of turbulent wake differ from the spacing required for traffic separation, which spacing shall be applied:

options.B: Separation minima required for air traffic
options.C: The larger of the two shall be applied

questions.explanation: The larger separation must be applied.

questions.question: When providing vectors to IFR flights, level assigned by the radar controller shall provide minimum terrain clearance.
questions.explanation: IFR vectors must ensure terrain clearance.

questions.question: When ATC provides vectors to VFR flights, the controller retains the responsibility of terrain clearance.
questions.explanation: VFR pilots are responsible for terrain clearance even when vectored.

questions.question: Radar controller shall always provide accurate information of weather cells to the aircraft.
questions.explanation: Radar may not always detect weather accurately; pilot weather radar is primary.

questions.question: Radar controller may not agree to provide radar vectors round the weather because of other operational priorities.
questions.explanation: Other priorities may preclude vectoring for weather.

questions.question: Radar controller may not agree to provide radar vectors to an aircraft because of other operational priorities.
questions.explanation: Operational priorities may prevent vectoring.

questions.question: Visual indication on a radar display of unwanted signals is called:
questions.explanation: Clutter is unwanted signals.

questions.question: The situation which exists when the radar blip or radar position symbol of a particular aircraft is seen and identified on radar display is known as:
questions.explanation: Radar contact is when the aircraft is seen and identified.

questions.question: The situation which exists when the radar position of a particular aircraft is seen on a radar-display and positively identified by the air traffic controller is known as:
questions.explanation: Radar identification is positive identification.

questions.question: AT rate one turn, 360 degrees will be completed in:
questions.explanation: Rate one turn is 3°/sec, so 360° takes 2 minutes.

questions.question: The vertical speed of the aircraft shown in:
questions.explanation: Vertical speed is shown in Line2.

questions.question: How does the Ground Speed calculated for the calculation of rate of descent in SRA.

options.A: Head wind component is subtracted to the True Air Speed.
options.B: Tail wind component is added from the True Air Speed.
options.C: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: GS = TAS + tailwind or TAS - headwind.

questions.question: Total numbers of codes available in 12 information pulses of transponder reply are:
questions.explanation: 12 pulses give 2^12 = 4096 codes.

questions.question: Which of the following formula is correct to compute the altitude at each NM on final in surveillance radar approach for a 3 degree glide path?

options.A: 106 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation.
options.B: 100 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation should be rounded to nearest 100 of feet.
options.C: 106 x 3 (angle of glide slope) x distance from touch down + Aerodrome elevation. ( altitude obtained should be rounded to nearest 100 of feet)

questions.explanation: Formula: Altitude = 106 × 3 × DME + elevation, rounded to nearest 100 ft.

questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect action of the radar controller in case of radar failure?

options.B: Establish vertical separation of 500 ft in critical cases.
options.C: Inform aircraft of the radar failure.
options.D: Hand over traffic to the procedure controller immediately.

questions.explanation: Immediate handover without coordination is incorrect.

questions.question: Radar echoes from vehicles, trains, birds, meteorological phenomena etc are known as:
questions.explanation: These are angels.

questions.question: The Minimum Safe Altitude Warning (MSAW) is defined in adaptation and it is activated/inhibited by the CMD. When it is activated, the icon text colour is YELLOW and, when it is inhibited, the icon text colour turns to WHITE.
questions.explanation: Description matches system behavior.

questions.question: Blind speed, tangential fade and concentric fade are the disadvantages of a suppressor known as:
questions.explanation: MTI has disadvantages like blind speeds.

questions.question: The minimum range at which a target can be detected depends on:

options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All factors affect minimum range.

questions.question: The minimum distance between two targets on the same bearing which can exist and still make it possible to distinguish between the targets on a SDD of radar is known as:
questions.explanation: Range resolution is the minimum distance along the same bearing.

questions.question: The pulse recurrence frequency is the:

options.A: The number pulses produced in one second.
options.B: The number pulses produced in one minute.
options.C: The number pulses produced in one hour.

questions.explanation: PRF is pulses per second.

questions.question: The minimum angular separation between two targets at the same range which can exist and still make it possible to distinguish between the targets on the SDD of radar is known as:
questions.explanation: Azimuth resolution is the minimum angular separation.

questions.question: In the radar display the transfer of radar identity (hand over) appears close to the:

options.A: Change over point or when required

questions.explanation: Handover appears near changeover point or when required.

questions.question: What is the disadvantage of the use of MTI in Primary radar?

options.A: Targets going away from radar head will be lost.
options.B: Targets circling the radar head will be lost.
options.C: Targets coming towards radar head will be lost.
options.D: Targets North of the radar head will be lost.

questions.explanation: Targets with no radial component (circling) are not detected.

questions.question: When an aircraft's radial speed is equal to multiples of wavelength and PRF divided by 102 is known as:
questions.explanation: Blind speed occurs when radial velocity equals multiples of (λ × PRF)/2.

questions.question: The blind speed is one of the disadvantages of MTI. Which of the following method can be used to minimize the effects of the blind speeds?

options.C: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Staggering or varying PRF helps mitigate blind speeds.

questions.question: Which of the following is not included in the basic components of Radar?
questions.explanation: On/Off switch is not a basic component.

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of primary radar?
questions.explanation: All are limitations.

questions.question: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by:

options.A: The spacing between P1 and P3.
options.C: The frequency of the interrogation signals
options.D: The power of the interrogation signals.

questions.explanation: Mode is determined by P1-P3 spacing.

questions.question: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by the determination of spacing between P1 and P3 by the transponder. The spacing between P1 and P3 in mode A is:
questions.explanation: Mode A spacing is 8 µs.

questions.question: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by the specific spacing between P1 and P3. The spacing between P1 and P3 in Military mode 3 is:
questions.explanation: Mode 3 is same as Mode A (8 µs).

questions.question: In SSR system the ground transmissions or interrogations are composed of pairs of pulse signals known as P1 and P3. The determination of mode of interrogation is done by the spacing between P1 and P3. The spacing between P1 and P3 in Military mode 3 and mode A is same.
questions.explanation: Both are 8 µs.

questions.question: In civil Mode A and Military Mode 3 the spacing between the ground transmitted interrogation signals P1 and P3 is same. This compatibility achieves civil and military common usage and is referred to as mode 3/A.
questions.explanation: Correct.

questions.question: The reply from the transponder consists of two framing pluses F1 and F2. The spacing between these two framing pulses is for all modes of interrogation.
questions.explanation: F1-F2 spacing is 20.3 µs.

questions.question: Which information is missing from the following Radar handover? "Radar handover PIA301 squawking 1033 Flight level 360."

options.D: Wake turbulence category of the aircraft.

questions.explanation: Position is missing.

questions.question: To request termination of pressure altitude transmission because of faulty operation which of the following phraseology is correct?

options.C: SWITCH OFF MODE CHARLIE DUE WRONG INDICATION.
options.D: PUT OFF MODE CHARLIE WRONG INDICATION.

questions.explanation: Correct phraseology: STOP SQUAWK CHARLIE WRONG INDICATION.

questions.question: Which of the following phraseology is correct when you want an aircraft to switch on Mode C?
questions.explanation: Standard phrase: SQUAWK CHARLIE.

questions.question: At Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad TMA, PSR and SSR are collocated and radar separation is 5NM. Radar services will continue to be provided in case the PSR fails.
questions.explanation: SSR can still provide separation if PSR fails.

questions.question: At Karachi, Lahore and Islamabad TMA, PSR and SSR are collocated and radar separation is 5NM. Radar services will still be provided in case the PSR fails but the radar separation will change from 5 NM to 15 NM.
questions.explanation: If PSR fails, SSR-only separation may be larger, but not automatically 15 NM; depends.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an essential requirement in the provision of radar service?
questions.explanation: Direct communication is essential; radar map and Mode A are not always required.

questions.question: Number of aircraft simultaneously provided with the radar service depends upon certain factors. Which of the following factors may not be considered in this regard?

options.D: Type of radar service to be provided.

questions.explanation: Seniority is not a factor.

questions.question: Which of the following codes has been reserved internationally for use by the pilot entering in a state of emergency?
questions.explanation: 7700 is emergency.

questions.question: Adherence of which of the following is required by the controllers and pilots to ensure the safe and efficient use of SSR?
questions.explanation: All are required.

questions.question: The tolerance value used to determine that Mode C-derived level information displayed to the controller in RVSM Airspace shall be:
questions.explanation: In RVSM, tolerance is ±200 ft.

questions.question: Verification of accuracy of Mode C-derived information displayed to the controller shall be effected at least once by suitably equipped ATS unit:

options.B: As soon as possible if not done on initial contact.
options.C: On initial contact and before radar handover.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Verification on initial contact, or as soon as possible.

questions.question: The pilot of the aircraft whose Mode C-derived level information is within the approved tolerance value need not be advised of the discrepancy.
questions.explanation: No need to advise if within tolerance.

questions.question: What shall be the action of a controller if the displayed Mode C level information is not within the approved tolerance value or when discrepancy in excess of the approved tolerance value is detected subsequent to verification:

options.A: Request the pilot to stop the Mode C transmission
options.B: Advised the pilot of the discrepancy.
options.C: Request the pilot to check pressure setting and confirm aircraft's level.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Advise pilot and request check.

questions.question: What shall be the action of the controller if, following confirmation of the correct pressure setting the discrepancy continues to exist?

options.A: Request the pilot to stop Mode C transmission.
options.B: Request the pilot to stop Mode C transmission if it does interrupt the operation of Mode A.
options.C: Inform the accepting unit of the action taken.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Stop Mode C only if it doesn't affect Mode A, and inform accepting unit.

questions.question: The criterion which shall be used to determine that a specific level is occupied by an aircraft shall be in RVSM airspace:
questions.explanation: In RVSM, level occupancy is within ±200 ft.

questions.question: An aircraft is considered to be maintaining its assigned level in CVSM airspace as long as the SSR Mode C-derived level information indicates that it is within:
questions.explanation: In CVSM, ±300 ft.

questions.question: An aircraft cleared to leave a level is considered to have commenced its manoeuvre and vacated the previously occupied level when the pressure-altitude-derived level information indicates a change of in the anticipated direction from its previously assigned level:
questions.explanation: Change of more than 300 ft indicates vacated.

questions.question: An aircraft in climb or descent is considered to have crossed a level when the SSR Mode C-derived level information indicates that it has passed this level in the required direction by more than:
questions.explanation: Crossed when passed by more than 300 ft.

questions.question: An aircraft is considered to have reached the level to which it has been cleared when the elapsed time of three display updates, three sensor updates or , whichever is the greater, has passed since the pressure-altitude-derived level information has indicated that it is within the appropriate tolerances of the assigned level:
questions.explanation: 15 seconds is the greater.

questions.question: Which of the following action is required to be taken by the radar controller before taking over watch?

options.C: Get the actual/ forecast of upper winds.

questions.explanation: All are required.

questions.question: Before taking over the radar controller has to ensure that the technical officer is satisfied with the functional capabilities of the radar system as well as the information presented on the radar display.
questions.explanation: The controller must check themselves, not rely on technical officer.

questions.question: Before providing radar service to an aircraft, the radar controller shall ensure:

options.B: Pilot has been informed of the identification.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Identification and informing pilot are required; clearance may already be given.

questions.question: Till what time the identification of the aircraft which has been provided with radar service shall be maintained?

options.B: Till the time radar hand over has been done.
options.C: Till the termination of radar service.
options.D: Till the time the accepting unit accepts the traffic.

questions.explanation: Identification maintained until radar service terminated.

questions.question: What action a radar controller shall take, if the identification of an aircraft being provided with radar service is lost?
questions.explanation: Inform the pilot immediately.

questions.question: If you find a correlated radar label indicating PIA 706 at MOLTA along with other information in the label and PIA 706 establishes radio contact with you, which SSR method of identification can be most appropriate?

options.A: Recognition of an assigned discrete code, the setting of which has been verified.
options.B: Recognition of the aircraft identification in a radar label.
options.C: Observation of compliance with an instruction to set a specific code.
options.D: Observation of compliance with an instruction to squawk IDENT.

questions.explanation: Correlated label with aircraft identification is a valid method.

questions.question: When two or more position indications are observed in close proximity, or are observed to be making similar movements at the same time, or when doubt exists as to the identity of a position indication for any other reason, changes of _______ should be prescribed or repeated as many times as necessary, or additional methods of identification should be employed, until all risk of error in identification is eliminated:
questions.explanation: Changes of heading help confirm identity.

questions.question: While performing duty as a radar controller you accept a radar handover for an aircraft. Which SSR method of radar identification is most appropriate in this case?

options.A: Recognition of an assigned discrete code, the setting of which has been verified.
options.B: Recognition of the aircraft identification in a radar label.
options.C: Observation of compliance with an instruction to set a specific code.

questions.explanation: Transfer of radar identification is the method.

questions.question: An aircraft is having problem with selection of a given code by ATC. Which of the following can be most appropriate SSR method of identification in this case?

options.A: Recognition of an assigned discrete code, the setting of which has been verified.
options.B: Observation of compliance with an instruction to squawk IDENT.
options.C: Observation of compliance with an instruction to set a specific code.

questions.explanation: If code selection fails, use IDENT.

questions.question: When SSR and PSR both are collocated; PSR method of identification is more reliable.
questions.explanation: SSR identification is more reliable.

questions.question: When working on a PSR an aircraft reports over head Nawabshah and informs you proceeding from OPSK to OPKC. The radar blip you observe is consistent with the position report and movement of the aircraft. Identification can be done without giving a turn of 30 degree or more.
questions.explanation: If position report matches blip, identification is established.

questions.question: When working on a PSR; an aircraft reports 35 southwest of Lahore proceeding to Lahore. The radar blip you observe is consistent with the position report of the aircraft. Identification can be done without executing any other method of identification.
questions.explanation: Position report consistency is enough.

questions.question: You can identify this aircraft within 01NM from the runway in use after getting air-born by executing one of the SSR methods of identification.
questions.explanation: Within 1 NM from runway, identification is not required; radar contact can be established by departure method.

questions.question: An aircraft reports orbiting overhead Sheikhupura and with no transponder on board. As an approach controller you are observing a only one radar blip consistent with maneuvers reported by the aircraft. You can identify this aircraft without executing any other method of identification.
questions.explanation: If the blip is consistent with reported orbiting, identification is established.

questions.question: Which of the following action needs to be ensured before transferring radar identity by a radar controller to another radar controller.

options.A: Aircraft is exiting the radar coverage.
options.B: Aircraft is within the radar coverage of the accepting controller.
options.C: Accepting unit is able to handle the traffic being transferred.
options.D: Accepting controller is experienced enough to handle the traffic being transferred.

questions.explanation: The accepting controller must have radar coverage and ability; experience is not a formal requirement.

questions.question: Which of the following is an incorrect method of transfer of radar identification?

options.A: Designation of the radar position indication over the shoulder by voice.
options.B: Designation of radar position indication by automated means.
options.C: Notification of the discrete code of the aircraft.
options.D: Direct designation by pointing a finger.

questions.explanation: Pointing a finger is not a standard method; voice or automated means are used.

questions.question: In which of the following circumstances, an aircraft provided with the radar service should be informed of its position.

options.A: Upon identification based on pilot report of aircraft position.
options.B: Upon identification within 01 NM of the runway after getting air-born.

questions.explanation: The answer key says 'None of the above' – position information is not required in those specific cases.

questions.question: An aircraft provided with radar service should be informed of its position:

options.A: When pilot is instructed to resume own navigation after radar vectoring.
options.B: When pilot estimate differ significantly from the radar controller.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All three situations require position information.

questions.question: Aircraft shall not be vectored closer than a distance equivalent to one half of the prescribed separation minimum, from limits of the airspace for which the radar controller is responsible unless:

options.A: Transfer of radar control is to be effected.
options.B: Accepting radar controller is well known.
options.C: Local arrangements ensure separation with radar controlled aircraft operating in the adjacent areas.
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Vectoring can be closer if transfer is imminent or local arrangements exist.

questions.question: Position information shall not be passed to an aircraft as a distance and direction from the centre line of an ATS route.
questions.explanation: Position information may be passed as distance and direction from a route centre line.

questions.question: When so informed the pilots may omit position reports. Pilot shall resume position reporting when:

options.C: Informed of loss of radar identification.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: Resume reporting in all those cases.

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of vectoring the aircraft along the ATS routes?

options.A: Pilot can monitor the aircraft position with reference to pilot interpreted Nav aids.
options.B: It will minimize the amount of radar navigation assistance required.
options.C: It will alleviate the consequences resulting from radar failure.

questions.explanation: All are advantages.

questions.question: While vectoring, an aircraft may not be informed of the purpose of the vector.
questions.explanation: Purpose may be omitted if not necessary.

questions.question: Controlled flights may be vectored into uncontrolled airspace:
questions.explanation: Vectors into uncontrolled airspace are not permitted for those reasons.

questions.question: Which of the following should be done prior to the issuing of manoeuvring instructions an aircraft which has reported unreliable directional instruments:

options.A: Request pilot to make all turns at an agreed rate.
options.B: Inform pilot of the weather at the alternate aerodrome.
options.C: Request pilot to carry out instruction immediately upon receipt.
options.D: Both a) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Agree on turn rate and instruct immediate action.

questions.question: The Short Term Conflict Alert (STCA) can be activated/inhibited by the controller from SDD position.
questions.explanation: STCA is usually system-automated; controller may inhibit via command, but the statement as given may be false.

questions.question: Radar controller retains the responsibility of terrain clearance for IFR flights.
questions.explanation: For IFR flights, controller ensures terrain clearance when vectoring.

questions.question: Whenever possible, minimum vectoring altitude should be sufficiently high in order to:

options.A: Give sufficient room to the pilot to be visual with airfield.
options.B: Minimize the activation of Ground Proximity Warning System (GPWS).
options.C: To minimize the work load of radar controller.
options.D: To minimize the track miles of the aircraft being vectored.

questions.explanation: Sufficient altitude reduces GPWS alerts.

questions.question: An aircraft provided with ATS surveillance service should be informed of its position upon identification, except when the identification is established by transfer of identification.
questions.explanation: Upon transfer, position is already known.

questions.question: The pilot of an aircraft requesting navigation assistance from an air traffic control unit providing radar service shall state reason.
questions.explanation: Reason for assistance request must be given.

questions.question: Information that an aircraft is going to penetrate an area of adverse weather shall be issued by the radar controller in sufficient time to permit the pilot to decide on appropriate course of action.
questions.explanation: Timely weather information is required.

questions.question: An aircraft's weather radar will normally provide better detection and definition of adverse weather than ATC radar.
questions.explanation: Airborne weather radar is more sensitive.

questions.question: In vectoring an aircraft for circumnavigating an area of adverse weather, the radar controller should ascertain that:

options.A: The aircraft can be returned to its departure point.
options.B: The aircraft can be returned to its intended flight path within the available radar coverage.
options.C: The pilot is informed about the circumstances in case the radar controller is unable to ensure b).
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: Ensure return to flight path or inform if not possible.

questions.question: Areas of adverse weather are always visible on a ATC radar display.
questions.explanation: Not all weather is visible on ATC radar.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of ATC radar?
questions.explanation: Detecting weather is not a primary function of ATC radar (though possible).

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of ATC radar?

options.A: Provide radar vectoring to aircraft to resolve conflicts.
options.B: Provide separation between RCF aircraft and other aircraft.
options.C: Provide vectoring around areas of adverse weather.

questions.explanation: All are functions.

questions.question: Radar separation shall only be provided:

options.B: When procedural separations cannot resolve traffic conflict
options.D: To all the aircraft whose blips are visible on radar display.

questions.explanation: Radar separation only for identified aircraft.

questions.question: An identified aircraft may not be provided with radar separation if in the opinion of radar controller the identification cannot be maintained.
questions.explanation: If identification cannot be maintained, radar separation is not applied.

questions.question: A synthetic track is a track generated by the system upon controller request. The information displayed on synthetic track is based upon:
questions.explanation: Synthetic track uses flight plan data.

questions.question: Radar separation based on the use of PSR blips and SSR responses shall be applied so that the distance between the ______ is never less than a prescribed minimum.

options.A: Center of the PSR blip and the nearest edge of the SSR response.
options.B: Nearest edge of the PSR blip and the center of the SSR response
options.C: Center of the PSR blip and the center of the SSR response

questions.explanation: Distance between center of PSR blip and nearest edge of SSR response.

questions.question: Radar separation based on the use of SSR responses shall be applied so that the distance between the _____ of the SSR responses is never less than a prescribed minimum.

options.C: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Distance between closest edges.

questions.question: Radar separation based on the use of SSR responses shall be applied so that the distance between the centers of the SSR responses is never less than a prescribed minimum if:

options.A: Performance of the radar is reliable.
options.C: The SSR responses from the wide body aircraft.

questions.explanation: Center-to-center separation requires authorization.

questions.question: Radar separation based on the use PSR blips shall be applied so that the distance between the centers of the PSR blips is never less than a prescribed minimum. Attention may be drawn to the fact that under certain circumstances the edges of PSR blips may touch each other.
questions.explanation: For PSR, separation is between edges, not centers.

questions.question: Radar separation may be applied between an aircraft taking off and a preceding departing aircraft or other radar controlled traffic provided that:

options.A: Aircraft taking off has a required db rating.
options.B: There is reasonable assurance that the departing air craft will be identified with 01 NM from the end of the runway.
options.C: There is reasonable assurance that radar failure will not occur.
options.D: The radar controller takes control of the departing aircraft before departure.

questions.explanation: Must be identified within 1 NM.

questions.question: Radar Separation may be applied between aircraft holding over the same holding point.
questions.explanation: Aircraft holding over same point cannot be separated by radar.

questions.question: Radar separation may be applied between holding aircraft and other flights subject to:

options.A: Consent from the pilot in command of the aircraft involved.
options.B: Requirements and approval from the appropriate ATS authorities.

questions.explanation: Approval from ATS authorities required.

questions.question: Unless otherwise prescribed, the horizontal separation minimum based on radar and/or ADS-B and/or MLAT systems shall be Doc4444/8.7.3.1:
questions.explanation: Standard radar separation is 5 NM.

questions.question: Subject to approval from appropriate ATS authorities and other specified conditions, minimum radar separation between succeeding aircraft established on the same final approach track is:
questions.explanation: On final approach, 2.5 NM may be used.

questions.question: Wake turbulence radar separation minima for Medium aircraft behind Heavy is 5 NM when both aircraft are using parallel runways separated by more the 760 meters.
questions.explanation: For parallel runways separated by more than 760 m, normal separation (not wake) applies.

questions.question: An aircraft encountering a state of emergency which has previously been directed by ATC to operate the transponder on a specific code, shall be advised to set code A 7700.
questions.explanation: Pilot should set 7700 without being advised.

questions.question: Which of the following is the identification method using primary radar?

options.C: Position report from the aircraft method.

questions.explanation: All are PSR identification methods.

questions.question: When an identified IFR flight operating outside controlled airspace is observed to be on a conflicting path with an other aircraft, the pilot should be:

options.A: Informed as to the need for collision avoidance action to be initiated.
options.B: Suggested a course of avoiding action if situation warrants.
options.C: Suggested a course of avoiding action on request.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All are appropriate.

questions.question: Which of the following information should be passed to a identified controlled flight regarding an unknown traffic on a conflicting path?

options.A: Relative bearing and distance of the conflicting traffic.
options.B: Direction in which unknown conflicting traffic seems to be moving.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All may be passed.

questions.question: When an identified controlled flight is observed to be on a conflicting path with an unknown aircraft deemed to constitute a collision hazard, the pilot of the controlled flight shall whenever practicable:

options.A: Be informed of the unknown aircraft.
options.B: Be suggested with course of avoiding action if situation warrants.
options.C: Be suggested with course of avoiding action on request.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All actions.

questions.question: If two way communication is lost with an aircraft, the radar controller should:

options.B: Inform other aircraft in the vicinity.
options.C: Plot the position the position of the aircraft on the radar.
options.D: Determine whether the receiver of the aircraft is working.

questions.explanation: Must determine if receiver is working.

questions.question: Radar separation can be applied using the synthetic target.
questions.explanation: Synthetic target is not used for radar separation.

questions.question: When an identified IFR flight operating outside controlled airspace is observed to be on a conflicting path with an other unidentified aircraft then an immediate avoiding vector shall be initiated by the radar controller to prevent collision:
questions.explanation: Vectoring may not be immediate; traffic information may suffice.

questions.question: If two way communication is lost with an aircraft the radar controller should determine whether the receiver of the aircraft is working by instructing the aircraft to acknowledge by:

options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All methods can be used.

questions.question: When it has been determined that the receiver of the RCF aircraft is working, the control of the aircraft may be continued if:

options.A: Aircraft can acknowledge instructions by making specified manoeuvres.
options.B: Aircraft is equipped with S/A transponder.
options.C: Aircraft is in the position to continue with the flight plan.
options.D: Aircraft is in the controlled airspace and can be separated with other flights.

questions.explanation: If equipped with S/A transponder (Mode S), control can continue.

questions.question: If a controlled aircraft experiences complete RCF in the radar covered airspace, the radar separation may continue to be used between RCF aircraft and other aircraft.
questions.explanation: Radar separation can continue if identification is maintained.

questions.question: If the aircraft experiencing complete RCF is unidentified, the radar separation may continue to be used provided:

options.A: The performance of the radar is satisfactory for the purpose.
options.B: Radar separation between identified aircraft and all the unidentified aircraft along the expected route of RCF aircraft is ensured.
options.C: Radar separation between identified aircraft can be ensured.

questions.explanation: Separation must be ensured between identified and all unidentified on that route.

questions.question: What should be the action if an aircraft experiences transponder failure after departure and the carriage of transponder is mandatory is the airspace.

options.A: Aircraft may be returned to the departure aerodrome.
options.B: Aircraft may be permitted to continue to the destination preferably.
options.C: Aircraft may asked to land at the nearest suitable aerodrome if acceptable to the operator.

questions.explanation: All options are possible.

questions.question: The final vector shall enable the aircraft to be established in level flight on the final approach track prior to intercepting the glide path. The final vector should provide an intercept angle with the final approach path of:
questions.explanation: Intercept angle should be 45 degrees or less.

questions.question: Radar controller may initiate radar vectoring for visual approach to an IFR flight provided:

options.A: The aircraft is number one in the approach sequence.
options.B: VMC prevail and reasonable assurance exists that visual approach and landing can be made.
options.C: Ceiling is above the minimum vectoring altitude.
options.D: Both b) and c) are correct.

questions.explanation: VMC and ceiling above MVA are required.

questions.question: Clearance for visual approach shall be issued only after the pilot has reported:
questions.explanation: Runway or preceding traffic in sight.

questions.question: An aircraft making SRA should be directed to execute missed approach on the instruction of the aerodrome controller.
questions.explanation: SRA missed approach is directed by tower.

questions.question: The tolerance value used to determine that Mode C-derived level information displayed to the controller for an RVSM compliant aircraft is accurate shall be:
questions.explanation: In RVSM, accuracy tolerance is ±200 ft.

questions.question: The tolerance which shall be used to determine that ADS level information displayed to the controller in RVSM airspace is accurate shall be:
questions.explanation: ADS level accuracy in RVSM is ±200 ft.

questions.question: Codes shall be reserved internationally for use by pilots encountering a state of emergency, radio communication failure or unlawful interference, respectively.
questions.explanation: 7700=emergency, 7600=radio failure, 7500=hijack.

questions.question: The RPM of Thomson CSF Primary Radar is:
questions.explanation: Thomson CSF primary radar rotates at 10 RPM.

questions.question: The speed for a given aircraft weight and runway conditions to which an aircraft can be accelerated and then brought to a stop in the remaining runway is known as:
questions.explanation: V1 is the decision speed for take-off.

questions.question: Which of the following is the disadvantage of primary radar?
questions.explanation: All are disadvantages.

questions.question: Where PSR and SSR are required to be used in combination, SSR alone may be used in the event of PSR failure to provide separation between ______, provided the accuracy of the SSR position indications has been verified by monitor equipment or other means.
questions.explanation: SSR alone can separate identified transponder-equipped aircraft.

questions.question: An aircraft flying in advisory airspace shall be provided with:
questions.explanation: Advisory airspace gets radar advisory service.

questions.question: An aircraft may be authorized by ATC to operate with an unserviceable transponder in airspace where carriage of serviceable transponder is mandatory provided that:

options.A: Pilot puts up request on R/T.
options.C: Operator puts up request recorded line.

questions.explanation: Operator must put up request on recorded line.

questions.question: Misaligned radar scope may be used for radar monitoring service provided due allowance is given for the extent of misalignment.
questions.explanation: If allowance is given, misalignment is acceptable.

questions.question: Radar scope alignment accuracy checks can be made by:

options.A: Comparison of observed aircraft tracks with known VOR radial, ILS localizer course.
options.B: Comparison of target track with controller-assigned heading.
options.C: Comparison of aircraft position reports with known geographic locations.
options.D: All of the above are correct.

questions.explanation: All methods are valid.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct method of passing weather cells information to an aircraft?

options.A: Range and bearing of the center of the observed cell echo from the airport or other well known location.
options.B: Orientation and length of major and minor axis.
options.C: Apparent intensity, direction and speed of movement of the cell.

questions.explanation: All are correct.

questions.question: For SAR purposes, which of the following is required to be preserved by the radar controller when a radar-tracked aircraft believed to be experiencing an emergency is lost from radar contact?

options.C: The time at which radar contact ended.

questions.explanation: All must be preserved.

questions.question: The civil SSR identification mode (Mode-A) is the same as the military IFF/SIF mode (Mode-3). IFF stands for?
questions.explanation: IFF stands for Identification of Friend or Foe.

questions.question: If the colour of the label shows the call sign in pink colour it means:

options.A: error in the Squawk of aircraft with the squawk in FPL
options.B: error in FL of aircraft with FL in FPL

questions.explanation: Pink indicates squawk mismatch.

questions.question: Whenever an apparent discrepancy is observed between the SSR code given to an aircraft and the displayed result, the controller shall request the pilot to confirm the code and reset his transponder using the phrase:
questions.explanation: Phrase: Reset squawk (mode) (code).

questions.question: Whenever an apparent discrepancy is observed between the SSR code given to an aircraft and the displayed result, the controller shall request the pilot to confirm the code and reset his transponder. If discrepancy still persists the pilot may be asked to:

options.C: Squawk 0000 followed by instruction to reselect the assigned code.

questions.explanation: Pilot may be asked to squawk 0000 and reselect.

questions.question: You must have read the phraseologies SQUAWK LOW or SQUAWK NORMAL. These phraseologies are related with:
questions.explanation: They relate to the IDENT feature? Actually SQUAWK LOW/NORMAL are for transponder sensitivity? The answer key says C.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct phraseology to request a pilot for temporary suspension of transponder operation?
questions.explanation: SQUAWK STANDBY is used.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct phraseology to request a pilot to squawk emergency mode?

options.C: SQUAWK EMEGENCY MODE A [ CODE SEVEN-SEVEN-ZERO-ZERO]

questions.explanation: Correct phrase is SQUAWK MAYDAY [CODE SEVEN-SEVEN-ZERO-ZERO].

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct phraseology to request a pilot for termination of transponder operation?
questions.explanation: STOP SQUAWK is correct.

questions.question: Vertical separation using Mode C is not to be applied against aircraft with transponder Mode A0000.
questions.explanation: Mode A0000 indicates no altitude reporting.

questions.question: An aircraft cleared to a level will be considered to have commenced its manoeuvre and vacated the previously occupied level when the SSR Mode-C derived level information indicates a change of:

options.A: More than 300 feet in the anticipated direction.
options.B: More than 200 feet in the anticipated direction.
options.C: More than 100 feet in the anticipated direction.

questions.explanation: Vacated when change >300 ft.

questions.question: Invalid or invalidated Mode-C read-outs shall not be used to determine an aircraft's altitude but may be used to observe whether an aircraft is climbing, descending or is in level flight.
questions.explanation: Invalid data can still show trends.

questions.question: An aircraft may be unable to accept or continue to comply with ATC speed restrictions in turbulent conditions.
questions.explanation: Turbulence may prevent speed compliance.

questions.question: Formation flight should not normally be subjected to speed control.
questions.explanation: Formation flights are not speed controlled.

questions.question: Radio communications with the aircraft shall be retained by the transferring radar controller until accepting radar controller has agreed to assume responsibility for providing radar services to the aircraft.
questions.explanation: Transfer only after acceptance.

questions.question: If an anticipation radar hand-over cannot be accomplished for any reason, the transferring radar controller shall:

options.A: Establish non-radar separation between the subject aircraft.
options.B: Terminate radar control service with the subject aircraft.
options.C: Request the aircraft to inform the accepting unit of the separation between them.
options.D: Both a) and b) are correct.

questions.explanation: Establish non-radar separation or terminate service.

questions.question: Radar controllers have the responsibility for the terrain clearance to aircraft in receipt of a radar information service when not subject to radar vectors.
questions.explanation: If not vectoring, pilot is responsible for terrain.

questions.question: Radar controllers have the responsibility for the terrain clearance to operating Special VFR within controlled airspace provided with radar vectors.
questions.explanation: When vectoring, controller ensures terrain clearance even for Special VFR.

questions.question: The extent of radar service which an Approach Control unit may provide will depend upon the:

options.B: Operational procedures approved by the Appropriate ATS authority.
options.C: Types of navigational aids available at the aerodrome.

questions.explanation: All factors influence service extent.

questions.question: Which of the following can be the function of an approach radar controller?
questions.explanation: All are approach radar functions.

questions.question: The approach radar controller when assessing distance must take into account:

options.A: Distance indicated by range rings is measured from the radar antenna head.
options.B: Distance indicated by range marks on extended center (video) is measured from touchdown.

questions.explanation: All factors must be considered.

questions.question: Critical military aircraft shall not normally be subjected to speed control.
questions.explanation: Critical military flights are exempt.

questions.question: Aircraft carrying out surveillance radar approaches shall be informed of the:
questions.explanation: All must be informed.

questions.question: Vectoring should be arranged so that an aircraft closes the final approach track at not less than miles from touchdown.
questions.explanation: Not less than 5 NM from touchdown.

questions.question: If glide path is inoperative; the pilot cleared for a 'localizer only' procedure may request advisory heights appropriate to the surveillance radar approach for the runway.
questions.explanation: Advisory heights may be provided.

questions.question: A multi-engine turbojet or a multi-engine propeller driven aircraft between 30 to 50 NM from the airfield shall not be instructed to reduce speed below:
questions.explanation: Minimum speed is 250 kts in that range.

questions.question: A multi-engine turbojet or a multi-engine propeller driven aircraft between 20 to 30 NM from the airfield and above F 100, shall not be instructed to reduce speed below:
questions.explanation: Above F100, minimum 250 kts.

questions.question: A multi-engine turbojet or a multi-engine propeller driven aircraft between 20 to 30 NM from the airfield and below F 100 shall not be instructed to reduce speed below:
questions.explanation: Below F100, minimum 200 kts.

questions.question: A multi-engine turbojet aircraft less than 20 NM from the airfield shall not be instructed to reduce speed below:
questions.explanation: Less than 20 NM, minimum 180 kts.

questions.question: Landing clearance or alternative instructions must be passed to an aircraft making a Surveillance radar approach before it reaches:
questions.explanation: Before reaching 2 NM.

questions.question: Aircraft making a Surveillance radar approach shall be reminded, when on final approach, to check that wheels are down and locked.
questions.explanation: Standard reminder.

questions.question: When carrying out Surveillance radar approach, the radar controller shall not be made responsible for any duties other than those directly connected with the SRA.
questions.explanation: Controller focuses on SRA only.

questions.question: Which of the following shall apply in case the termination range is 2 NM for a surveillance radar approach:

options.A: Aircraft shall be informed of the termination range.
options.B: Advisory heights and ranges shall be passed at each NM.
options.C: The pilot shall be instructed to check decision height at a range of 3 NM from touch down.

questions.explanation: All actions apply.

questions.question: An aircraft on Surveillance radar approach shall be instructed to carry out a missed approach when:

options.B: When no landing clearance is received till 2 NM from touchdown
options.C: When the aircraft appears to be dangerously positioned on final approach.

questions.explanation: All are valid reasons.

questions.question: AIP Pakistan consists of following three parts:
questions.explanation: AIP has three parts: General (GEN), En-route (ENR), and Aerodrome (AD).

questions.question: AIP stands for:
questions.explanation: AIP stands for Aeronautical Information Publication.

questions.question: AIM stands for:
questions.explanation: AIM stands for Aeronautical Information Management.

questions.question: How many minimum days required for publication of aeronautical data through AIRAC cycle before effective date of AIRAC?
questions.explanation: AIRAC requires 42 days notice before effective date.

questions.question: WGS-84 stands for:
questions.explanation: WGS-84 stands for World Geodetic System 1984.

questions.question: Which color is used for promulgation of AIP Supplement / AIRAC?
questions.explanation: AIP Supplements and AIRAC are published on yellow pages.

questions.question: How many aerodromes are certified by the Regulatory Division?
questions.explanation: 13 aerodromes are certified by the Regulatory Division.

questions.question: How many airports are non operational in Karachi & Lahore FIR?
questions.explanation: 8 airports are non-operational.

questions.question: AIP is prepared in accordance with ICAO Standard & Recommended Practices (SARPs) of:
questions.explanation: AIP follows SARPs of Annex 15 (AIS), Doc 8126 (AIS Manual), and Annex 4 (Charts).

questions.question: Charts Contained in AIP are prepared in accordance with ICAO Standard & Recommended Practices (SARPs) of:
questions.explanation: AIP charts follow Annex 4 (Aeronautical Charts) and Doc 8697 (Aeronautical Chart Manual).

questions.question: SNOWTAM is a special series of NOTAM notifying the presence or removal of hazardous conditions due to _______, ice, slush or standing water associated with snow, slush and ice on the movement area, by means of a specific format.
questions.explanation: SNOWTAM notifies snow conditions.

questions.question: The List of Radio Navigation Aids are published in AIP Section:
questions.explanation: Radio navigation aids are in GEN 2.5.

questions.question: The Area-1 specified for obstacle and terrain Data covers:

options.A: Within the vicinity of an aerodrome.
options.D: The area bordering an aerodrome movement area.

questions.explanation: Area-1 covers the entire territory of the State for obstacle and terrain data.

questions.question: Aeronautical Charges for Aerodrome / Heliport & Air Navigation Services are Published in AIP Section:
questions.explanation: Aeronautical charges are in GEN 4.1 and 4.2.

questions.question: International / Domestic ATS routes are published in AIP section?
questions.explanation: ATS routes are in ENR 3.1.

questions.question: Area Navigation Routes / RNAV Routes/ published in AIP section?
questions.explanation: RNAV routes are in ENR 3.3.

questions.question: Navigational Warnings are published in AIP section?
questions.explanation: Navigational warnings are in ENR 5.

questions.question: AIS is an Abbreviation of:
questions.explanation: AIS stands for Aeronautical Information Service.

questions.question: AIC stands for:
questions.explanation: AIC stands for Aeronautical Information Circular.

questions.question: Permanent changes of any facilities of Airports will be published through:
questions.explanation: Permanent changes are published via AIP Amendment.

questions.question: Which series of NOTAMs are issued by International NOTAM office?
questions.explanation: International NOTAM office issues NOTAM A & C series.

questions.question: What type of AIP data published by AIS?

options.C: Aeronautical Static Data of Karachi / Lahore FIRs

questions.explanation: All are published by AIS.

questions.question: Checklist of valid NOTAMs (A & C series) are issued:
questions.explanation: NOTAM checklist is issued monthly.

questions.question: AIRAC stands for:

options.C: Airport Information Required for Aeronautical Circulation

questions.explanation: AIRAC stands for Aeronautical Information Regulation and Control.

questions.question: How many International NOTAM offices are established in Pakistan?
questions.explanation: There is 1 International NOTAM office in Pakistan.

questions.question: Dimension of Runway of X Airport has been changed. What type of NOTAM series will be issued by AIS?
questions.explanation: Runway dimension change is an aerodrome-related NOTAM, so NOTAM A series (international).

questions.question: If a NOTAM is valid for more than three months, it will be replaced with:
questions.explanation: NOTAMs valid >3 months are replaced with AIP Supplement.

questions.question: Which Airport is owned by the private owner in Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Sialkot Airport is privately owned.

questions.question: ILS CAT-III-B is installed at:
questions.explanation: ILS CAT-III-B is installed at Lahore.

questions.question: List of aeronautical charts are available in:
questions.explanation: List of charts is in GEN 3.

questions.question: Which aerodrome have highest Aerodrome elevation in Pakistan:
questions.explanation: Quetta has the highest elevation among these.

questions.question: An area symmetrical along the extended runway centerline and adjacent to the end of the strip primarily intended to reduce the risk of damage to an aeroplane undershooting or overrunning the runway:
questions.explanation: Definition of Runway End Safety Area (RESA).

questions.question: PCN stands for:
questions.explanation: PCN stands for Pavement Classification Number.

questions.question: Private aircraft intend to operate private flight to Pakistan or overflying its territory is required to obtain prior permission from:
questions.explanation: Prior permission from Director Air Transport & Economic Regulation (AT & ER).

questions.question: Advanced notice (No. of days) is required for major changes to be distributed by AIS in paper copy form by at least:
questions.explanation: Paper copy requires 56 days notice.

questions.question: Activation of established Danger, Restricted or Prohibited Areas and activities requiring temporary airspace restrictions should be notified at least (No. of days) in advance.
questions.explanation: 7 days notice required for such activations.

questions.question: A number expressing the bearing, strength of a pavement for unrestricted operations is called PCN:
questions.explanation: PCN is Pavement Classification Number.

questions.question: Quarterly Schedule Maintenance of Nav.Aids is issued through AIC:
questions.explanation: QSM of nav aids is published via AIC.

questions.question: Aerodrome Obstacle Chart is developed in accordance with DOC 4444 by AIS for publication in AIP:
questions.explanation: Obstacle charts are in accordance with Annex 4 and Doc 8697, not Doc 4444.

questions.question: Aerodrome Obstacle data in approach and take-off area at an Aerodrome is published in AIP Para AD 2.10.
questions.explanation: Obstacle data is in AD 2.10.

questions.question: AIP Amendment / Supplement is published under AIRAC Cycle system:
questions.explanation: AIP Amendments are under AIRAC, but Supplements may not always be.

questions.question: Checklist of valid AIP Supplements is issued at the interval of 3 months:
questions.explanation: Checklist of Supplements is issued at least annually, not quarterly.

questions.question: Trigger NOTAM is issued when AIP amendment / supplement published on AIRAC date:
questions.explanation: Trigger NOTAM alerts to AIRAC publication.

questions.question: Aeronautical Data to be issued as part of integrated Aeronautical Information Package shall be thoroughly checked by the concerned quarter before it is submitted to the Aeronautical Information Service:
questions.explanation: Data is checked by AIS, not the originating quarter.

questions.question: Israeli registered aircraft is permitted to operate or to overfly Pakistan.
questions.explanation: Israeli registered aircraft are not permitted.

questions.question: Aerodrome elevations are published in AIP, GEN section:
questions.explanation: Aerodrome elevations are published in AD section, not GEN.

questions.question: NOTAM contained information according to the NOTAM format as mentioned in Annex 4, Appendix-6:
questions.explanation: NOTAM format is in Annex 15, Appendix 6, not Annex 4.

questions.question: When error is observed in a NOTAM, a NOTAM with a new number is issued to replace the erroneous NOTAM:
questions.explanation: A correction NOTAM is issued with the same number and a suffix.

questions.question: A NOTAM is issued regarding X Airport Runway closure due to VVIP movement and also VOR is not available:
questions.explanation: VVIP movement is not normally NOTAMed; only facility changes.

questions.question: The NOTAM A & C series is issued for different international & domestic airports:
questions.explanation: A series for international, C series for domestic.

questions.question: Temporary changes for long duration and information of short duration containing extensive text or graphics shall be published as AIP Supplement:
questions.explanation: AIP Supplement is for such changes.

questions.question: The runway at Quetta Airport is the longest runway in Pakistan:
questions.explanation: Quetta has the longest runway in Pakistan.

questions.question: Sunrise and sunset table is provided by Survey of Pakistan:
questions.explanation: Sunrise/sunset table is provided by Meteorological Department.

questions.question: Sunrise / sunset table is published in GEN 2 of AIP.
questions.explanation: It is in GEN 2.

questions.question: Nawabshah is an Alternate Airport for Rahim Yar Khan Airport.
questions.explanation: Nawabshah is not an alternate for Rahim Yar Khan.
